project_goal.value: Expose standardized property listing data to third-party real estate portals, MLS aggregators, and broker CRMs via a RESTful API that normalizes listing schemas across multiple source feeds
project_goal.rationale: Core business need to unify disparate MLS feed formats for downstream partner consumption

target_users.value: Third-party real estate portals, MLS aggregators, and broker CRM platforms consuming listing data via partner API keys
target_users.rationale: External B2B consumers requiring reliable data feeds

project_class.rationale: Pure API backend with no frontend; serves partner integrations

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, analytics, devops
capabilities.rationale: API service with partner auth, data normalization, usage analytics, and ops

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source feed normalization, partner rate limiting, and schema versioning are non-trivial

risk_level.rationale: Partner-facing API with data integrity and availability requirements but no direct consumer PII

data_sensitivity.rationale: Listing data is business-internal; no PII or financial data in primary flows

external_exposure.rationale: API is consumed exclusively by authenticated partner accounts, not public

access_model.value: API key authentication per partner account with per-key rate limit tiers and IP allowlisting option; mTLS for enterprise tier
access_model.rationale: Partner API access model standard; JWT short-lived tokens for session auth

feature_scope.value: Listing feed ingestion and normalization, canonical schema API with version pinning, partner account management, rate limiting per tier, schema version negotiation, bulk export detection, sync log audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for v1 partner data platform

mvp_scope.value: Core listing feed ingestion for 3 MLS sources, canonical REST API for listings filtered by region and property type, partner API key provisioning, per-key rate limiting, Accept-Version schema pinning
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for first partner onboarding

security_baseline.value: API key auth with mTLS for high-tier partners, TLS 1.3 in transit, WAF on API Gateway, Redis token-bucket rate limiting, bulk export detection via sliding-window sorted set, API key rotation policy 90 days
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API security requirements including exfiltration detection

frontend_stack.value: none - API service only
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend; API consumers are machine clients

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin framework
backend_stack.rationale: High-throughput Go is well-suited for API gateway-style workloads with large fan-out

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for canonical listing store; Redis 7 for rate limit counters, bulk export sorted sets, and feed cursor state
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for normalized schema; Redis for sub-millisecond counter operations

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 behind API Gateway
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container hosting with API Gateway for usage metering

privacy_retention_policy.value: Listing data retained indefinitely as business records; sync_logs retained 180 days; partner access logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Audit and compliance retention requirements for partner data handling

future_scope.value: GraphQL endpoint for flexible field selection, webhook push for real-time partner notifications, self-service partner onboarding portal
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 platform expansion

constraints.value: RESO Web API standard compliance required; API response latency p99 under 200ms; support up to 50 concurrent partner integrations at launch
constraints.rationale: RESO compliance is a contractual requirement for MLS partnerships

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with per-partner request traces, custom metrics for feed ingestion lag and schema normalization errors, PagerDuty for p99 latency breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard with partner-level granularity

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with API-first design; OpenAPI spec reviewed before implementation
execution_preference.rationale: API-first ensures partner contract stability

project.goal: Normalize MLS listing feeds and serve canonical property data to authenticated partner accounts
project.target_users: Real estate portals, MLS aggregators, broker CRMs
project.timeline: Q2 delivery for first partner onboarding

backend.framework: Gin with custom middleware for rate limiting, API key validation, and Accept-Version schema pinning
backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 and RESO Web API standard conformance

security.auth: API key with JWT short-lived tokens; mTLS option for enterprise partners
security.rate_limiting: Redis token-bucket per API key; bulk export sliding-window sorted set

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for canonical listing store and schema_mappings
data.cache: Redis 7 for rate limit counters, bulk export detection sets, listing hot-path cache

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, go test -race, integration tests
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate behind AWS API Gateway

open_questions.feed_update_frequency: How often do MLS source feeds push updates - determines polling vs webhook model

reasoner_reviews.product: The canonical listing schema approach is correct for decoupling partner contracts from MLS source formats. Accept-Version header schema pinning resolves the blocking issue from round 1 and allows partners to safely migrate on their own timeline. Rate limit tiers differentiated by partner SLA class are a core feature, not an afterthought.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go 1.22 with Gin is an appropriate choice for this workload - concurrent feed ingestion and fan-out benefits from goroutines. The schema_mappings versioning with NormalizationService producing version-specific field maps is a clean solution. The dedicated normalization worker pool separate from the API serving path prevents ingestion spikes from impacting API latency.
reasoner_reviews.security: The two-layer abuse prevention model is now correct: Redis token-bucket per-minute rate limiting as first defense, Redis sorted-set sliding-window bulk export detection as second layer. The 5000 distinct listing_key per hour threshold is appropriate for detecting corpus scraping. mTLS at API Gateway layer for enterprise partners avoids application-layer complexity.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: RESO Web API OData filter compliance requires specific field naming conventions enforced at the normalization layer. The 200ms p99 latency target is achievable with read replica routing and Redis hot-path cache. Accept-Version middleware adds minimal overhead for the schema versioning benefit.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The bulk export detection goroutine sweeping every 5 minutes creates a 5-minute detection window - a determined attacker could drain 5000 keys in under 5 minutes and go undetected until the sweep. Consider reducing sweep interval to 1 minute for production, with the 5-minute interval acceptable for MVP.

backend.service_design: Three Gin service modules: FeedIngestionService (MLS source polling with configurable intervals per board, 8-goroutine worker pool), NormalizationService (maps source-specific fields to RESO canonical schema using schema_mappings records keyed by mls_board_id and schema_version), ListingAPIService (partner-facing endpoints with API key middleware, Redis token-bucket rate limiter, Accept-Version middleware, RESO OData filter support, and bulk export ZADD call per listing_key fetch).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful endpoints conforming to RESO Web API standard. GET /v1/listings with OData filter params (ListPrice, PropertyType, ModificationTimestamp for incremental sync), Accept-Version header for schema pin. GET /v1/listings/:listing_key full field set. GET /v1/listings/changes delta cursor endpoint. GET /v1/partner/quota for quota status. All responses include X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-Schema-Version headers.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker per MLS source feed with exponential backoff. Failed ingestion jobs published to SQS dead-letter queue. API errors return RFC 7807 problem+json with RESO-specific error codes. Feed cursor checkpointed to Redis on every successful batch for idempotent resume. PostgreSQL read replica lag monitored; fallback to primary if replication_lag_seconds exceeds 10.
backend.backend_risks: MLS source feed schema changes without advance notice cause normalization failures; mitigated by schema validation on ingestion with Datadog alerting on unexpected source fields and 4-hour SLA to update schema_mappings.

security.auth_design: API key stored as SHA-256 hash in partner_accounts.api_key_hash; raw key returned only at provisioning. JWT (15 min TTL, RS256) issued on key exchange for session-scoped requests. Enterprise partner mTLS enforced at API Gateway with client certificate CN matched to partner_id.
security.authorization_model: Partner accounts have a tier enum (standard/premium/enterprise) controlling allowed endpoints, max page size, daily quota, and schema_version access. Internal admin API on separate route prefix protected by IAM role, not partner keys.
security.abuse_prevention: Redis token-bucket rate limiter with per-key counters (burst 2x sustained rate by tier). Bulk export detection: Redis ZADD records listing_key + epoch timestamp on each fetch; background goroutine sweeps every 5 minutes computing ZCOUNT over 1-hour window per active partner; if cardinality exceeds 5000, inserts bulk_export_flag and sends Slack alert; second flag triggers API key suspension with support ticket.
security.security_risks: API key compromise is primary threat; mitigated by short-lived JWTs, 90-day rotation policy, and anomaly detection on access patterns. Bulk export via legitimate paginated requests mitigated by sliding-window detection.

data.entities: listings (listing_key UUID PK, mls_board_id FK, address_full, property_type ENUM, list_price NUMERIC, bedrooms, bathrooms, sqft, status ENUM, raw_source_json JSONB, schema_version_normalized SMALLINT, normalized_at, modification_timestamp). listing_feeds (id, mls_board_id FK, feed_url, auth_config JSONB encrypted, polling_interval_seconds, last_cursor, consecutive_errors, status ENUM). partner_accounts (id, name, api_key_hash CHAR(64), tier ENUM, schema_version SMALLINT, daily_quota, bulk_export_flag_count, ip_allowlist INET[], suspended BOOLEAN). schema_mappings (id, mls_board_id FK, source_field, canonical_field, transform_fn, schema_version). sync_logs partitioned by started_at with 180-day retention.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 primary with read replica for partner query load. GIN index on raw_source_json for schema debugging. Redis 7 for rate limit token buckets (TTL = window), bulk export sorted sets per partner (TTL 1 hour), listing hot-path cache (TTL 5 min).
data.retention_and_deletion: Listing records retained indefinitely. sync_logs auto-deleted after 180 days via pg_partman range partitioning on started_at. Partner access logs exported to S3 via CloudWatch Logs, retained 1 year.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with two task definitions: api-server (3-10 tasks, autoscale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget) and feed-worker (2-4 tasks, autoscale on SQS ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible). API Gateway HTTP API in front of api-server with usage plans per partner tier. RDS PostgreSQL multi-AZ in us-east-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint with staticcheck and errcheck, go test -race -cover 80% gate, schema compatibility tests for normalization changes, Docker multi-stage build (builder + distroless), ECR push tagged with commit SHA, ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy 100% and 30s deregistration delay. DB migrations via golang-migrate as ECS one-off task before rolling deployment.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM ddtrace middleware tagging every span with partner_id, schema_version, endpoint. Custom StatsD metrics: feed_ingestion_lag_seconds per mls_board, normalization_error_rate, rate_limit_violations_total per partner, bulk_export_flags_total, listings_indexed_total. Per-partner Datadog dashboard for daily quota and SLA monitoring.
devops.devops_risks: Blue/green deployment required for schema_mapping version changes to avoid mid-migration inconsistency across running api-server tasks. ECS task restart during active feed ingestion handled by idempotent upsert on listing_key.

ISS-001.title: Schema versioning mechanism undefined for partner API contract stability
ISS-001.detail: The previous plan described normalization but did not specify how partners pin to a schema version or how breaking changes are staged. Partners onboarding before a schema migration risk silent data format changes.

history.note: Identified during architect reasoner review; no versioning strategy present in v1 plan

ISS-002.title: Bulk export detection absent from rate limiting design
ISS-002.detail: Rate limiting in the previous plan covered per-minute request counts only. A partner could issue many small paginated requests over hours to drain the full listing corpus without triggering any throttle.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer; standard rate limiting insufficient for data exfiltration scenario

focus_issues.title: Schema versioning mechanism undefined
focus_issues.detail: Partners need a version pin mechanism before first onboarding. Missing from previous plan.

focus_issues.title: Bulk export detection absent
focus_issues.detail: Sliding window quota on distinct listing keys needed beyond per-minute rate limiting.

latest_recommendations: Add API versioning via Accept-Version header negotiation with schema_version field in partner_accounts
Implement sliding 1-hour window counter for distinct listing_key fetches per partner in Redis
Document migration playbook for schema_mapping changes with 30-day partner notification SLA
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Real Estate Listing Data API v1

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues blocking first partner onboarding

previous_audits.summary: Solid foundational API design with good RESO alignment and rate limiting basics, but a critical gap in schema versioning threatens partner contract stability at onboarding.
strengths: Go with Gin is well-matched to high-throughput listing queries
RESO Web API standard correctly referenced and planned
Redis-backed rate limiting design is sound
concerns: No schema versioning mechanism defined - partners cannot safely pin to a field set
Bulk listing export scenario not addressed in abuse prevention section
blocking_issues: Schema versioning must be defined before any partner onboarding; a breaking normalization change post-onboarding requires emergency partner communications
recommendations: Add Accept-Version header negotiation mapping to schema_version in partner_accounts
Add sliding window quota counter in Redis tracking distinct listing_keys fetched per partner per hour
Include schema migration playbook with 30-day advance notice SLA to partners

issue_updates.title: Schema versioning mechanism undefined
issue_updates.detail: Plan described normalization but no version pinning or migration lifecycle defined

issue_updates.title: Bulk export detection absent
issue_updates.detail: Per-minute rate limiting insufficient; exfiltration via paginated scraping not addressed

previous_plan.title: Real Estate Listing Data API v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 Gin API service normalizing MLS listing feeds to RESO canonical schema, serving partner portals via keyed API access with per-tier rate limiting on ECS Fargate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: ECS Fargate cluster with api-server and feed-worker tasks behind API Gateway. PostgreSQL 16 canonical listing store, Redis rate limiting. Feed ingestion worker polls MLS sources on configurable intervals.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: API key auth with JWT. Per-minute Redis rate limiting per partner key. TLS 1.3. WAF on API Gateway. No schema versioning defined. No bulk export detection.
previous_plan.data_model: listings, listing_feeds, partner_accounts, schema_mappings, sync_logs tables. No schema_version column on partner_accounts.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision targets two blocking issues from the round 1 audit: schema versioning for partner API contract stability and bulk export detection beyond per-minute rate limiting. Schema versioning is solved via Accept-Version header negotiation mapped to schema_version in partner_accounts, with NormalizationService producing version-specific field maps and a 30-day migration notice SLA. Bulk export detection adds a Redis sorted-set sliding window tracking distinct listing_keys per partner per hour, suspending accounts at the 5000-key threshold on second violation.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Accept-Version header negotiation middleware in Gin. Added schema_version column to partner_accounts (default 1). NormalizationService now serializes responses using version-specific field map from schema_mappings. Migration playbook added to development_guidelines: 30-day advance partner notice, parallel old/new schema fields during transition, 60-day deprecation sunset.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partners pin schema version via Accept-Version header; breaking normalization changes staged with advance notice and do not silently affect partner data feeds.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis sorted-set bulk export detector: each GET /v1/listings response appends fetched listing_keys to a partner-scoped sorted set scored by epoch timestamp with 1-hour TTL. Background goroutine sweeps every 5 minutes per active partner; ZCOUNT over 1-hour window triggers flag at 5000 distinct keys. Second flag suspends API key pending manual clearance.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Corpus scraping via paginated requests detected and blocked; per-minute rate limiting remains as first defense layer, sorted-set bulk detection as second layer.

target_output.title: Real Estate Listing Data API - Partner Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin API service normalizing property listing feeds from multiple MLS boards into a RESO Web API-compliant canonical schema, serving authenticated partner portals, MLS aggregators, and broker CRMs on AWS ECS Fargate. Round 2 adds Accept-Version header schema pinning and a Redis sliding-window bulk export detector addressing partner contract stability and data exfiltration risks identified in round 1.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two ECS Fargate task groups share a PostgreSQL 16 RDS instance and Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster: api-server (3-10 tasks, autoscale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget) handles partner requests behind AWS API Gateway HTTP API; feed-worker (2-4 tasks, autoscale on SQS queue depth) polls MLS source feeds via configurable per-board intervals using an 8-goroutine normalization worker pool. API Gateway enforces usage plans per partner tier before requests reach Gin. NormalizationService maps source-specific fields to RESO canonical schema using schema_mappings records keyed by mls_board_id and schema_version, then upserts to listings by listing_key. Accept-Version middleware resolves schema_version from partner_accounts and routes serialization to the correct field map. Bulk export detection goroutine runs every 5 minutes per active partner key against Redis sorted sets.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin 1.9, pgx v5 for PostgreSQL, go-redis v9, AWS SDK v2, ddtrace/go for Datadog APM. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, AWS API Gateway HTTP API with usage plans, RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ with read replica, ElastiCache Redis 7, SQS dead-letter queue for failed ingestion batches, ECR, ACM, Route53. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, go test -race, Docker multi-stage build on distroless base, ECS rolling deploy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Feed Ingestion: per-MLS polling with configurable intervals, source field extraction, schema normalization via schema_mappings, idempotent upsert by listing_key, failed-batch dead-letter to SQS, sync_log audit per run. Canonical Listing API: GET /v1/listings with RESO OData filters (ListPrice, PropertyType, ModificationTimestamp cursor), Accept-Version schema pin, paginated response with X-RateLimit-Remaining. Delta Sync: GET /v1/listings/changes cursor endpoint for incremental partner sync. Partner Access Control: API key provisioning with SHA-256 hash storage, tier-based rate limits in Redis token bucket, bulk export detection via sorted-set sliding window, account suspension workflow. Schema Versioning: version-specific field maps in NormalizationService, 30-day migration notice SLA, parallel old/new field output during transition.
target_output.system_components: AWS API Gateway HTTP API (usage plans per tier, mTLS option for enterprise tier partners), ECS Fargate api-server cluster (Gin application with API key middleware, Redis rate-limit middleware, Accept-Version middleware, listing handlers, bulk export ZADD calls), ECS Fargate feed-worker cluster (polling scheduler per MLS board, 8-goroutine normalization pool, SQS DLQ publisher), RDS PostgreSQL 16 primary + read replica (listings, schema_mappings, partner_accounts, sync_logs), ElastiCache Redis 7 (token-bucket rate limit counters, bulk export sorted sets per partner, listing hot-path cache 5-min TTL), SQS standard queue for failed ingestion dead letters, Datadog Agent sidecar on all Fargate tasks.
target_output.workflows: MLS Feed Ingestion: feed-worker scheduler fires per mls_board polling_interval_seconds, fetches delta records from feed_url using last_cursor timestamp, passes raw records to NormalizationService which looks up schema_mappings by mls_board_id and source_field, validates required RESO fields, upserts into listings table on listing_key conflict, updates sync_log with ingested count and errors_json, advances last_cursor in Redis. On batch error: increments consecutive_errors, publishes failed payload to SQS DLQ, emits Datadog feed_ingestion_error counter. Partner Query Workflow: partner sends GET /v1/listings with Accept-Version: 2 and OData filter params; API Gateway validates usage plan; Gin rate-limit middleware checks Redis token bucket for api_key; Accept-Version middleware resolves schema_version from partner_accounts; listing handler queries PostgreSQL read replica with filter predicates, serializes using version-specific field map, returns RESO JSON with X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-Schema-Version headers. Bulk Export Detection: on each listing_key returned, listing handler executes Redis ZADD partner:{id}:export_window listing_key epoch_ms; background goroutine sweeps every 5 minutes running ZCOUNT over (now-3600s, now); if count exceeds 5000, increments partner_accounts.bulk_export_flag_count, emits Slack alert; second flag triggers suspended=true and creates support ticket.
target_output.data_model: listings (listing_key UUID PK, mls_board_id UUID FK, address_full TEXT, property_type ENUM residential/commercial/land/multi_family, list_price NUMERIC(12,2), bedrooms SMALLINT, bathrooms NUMERIC(3,1), sqft INT, lot_size_sqft INT, status ENUM active/pending/closed/withdrawn, raw_source_json JSONB, schema_version_normalized SMALLINT, normalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ, modification_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). listing_feeds (id UUID PK, mls_board_id UUID FK, feed_url TEXT, auth_config JSONB encrypted with pgcrypto, polling_interval_seconds INT, last_cursor TIMESTAMPTZ, consecutive_errors INT DEFAULT 0, status ENUM active/paused/error). partner_accounts (id UUID PK, name TEXT, api_key_hash CHAR(64), tier ENUM standard/premium/enterprise, schema_version SMALLINT DEFAULT 1, daily_quota INT, bulk_export_flag_count INT DEFAULT 0, ip_allowlist INET[], suspended BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). schema_mappings (id UUID PK, mls_board_id UUID FK, source_field TEXT, canonical_field TEXT, transform_fn TEXT, schema_version SMALLINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). mls_boards (id UUID PK, name TEXT, region TEXT, reso_certified BOOLEAN). sync_logs (id UUID PK, feed_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, listings_ingested INT, listings_failed INT, errors_json JSONB) partitioned by range on started_at with 180-day auto-drop.
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/listings?$filter=PropertyType eq 'Residential' and ListPrice ge 200000&$select=ListingKey,ListPrice,Bedrooms&$top=100&$skipToken=cursor with Accept-Version: 2 header (RESO OData format). GET /v1/listings/:listing_key returns full RESO field set for single listing. GET /v1/listings/changes?since=2026-03-01T00:00:00Z returns ModificationTimestamp-ordered delta records for incremental partner sync. GET /v1/partner/quota returns daily_quota, requests_today, bulk_export_flag_count, suspended status. POST /internal/feeds/:id/trigger (admin-only IAM-authenticated force feed refresh). All responses include X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, X-Schema-Version headers. Errors return RFC 7807 application/problem+json with RESO-specific error codes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key stored as SHA-256 hash in partner_accounts; raw key returned only at provisioning. JWT (15 min TTL, RS256) issued on key exchange for session-scoped requests. Enterprise partner mTLS enforced at API Gateway with client certificate CN matched to partner_id. Accept-Version middleware validates schema_version request against partner_accounts.schema_version; downgrade to older version permitted, upgrade requires explicit opt-in. Bulk export detection: Redis ZADD on each listing_key fetch, 5-minute sweep goroutine, 5000-key/hour threshold for flag, second flag triggers suspension. API key rotation enforced at 90 days via reminder workflow. WAF rules on API Gateway: SQL injection patterns, path traversal, oversized request bodies. Rate limiting: Redis token bucket per api_key with burst allowance 2x sustained rate per tier. RESO Web API compliance reviewed quarterly against RESO Certification criteria. SOC 2 Type II evidence collected via Datadog audit log exports.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint with staticcheck and errcheck (no suppression without documented reason), go test -race -cover (80% gate), schema compatibility test suite validating all active schema_versions still serialize correctly, Docker multi-stage build (golang:1.22 builder + distroless final), ECR push tagged with commit SHA and semver tag on release, ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy 100% and 30s deregistration delay. Environment matrix: dev (single task each, RDS t3.medium, Redis t3.micro), staging (2 api-server + 1 feed-worker, RDS r6g.large, mirrors production config), production (3-10 api-server autoscale, 2-4 feed-worker autoscale, RDS r6g.xlarge multi-AZ, Redis r6g.medium). Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation for MLS feed credentials. Schema migration playbook: deploy new schema_mappings version, update NormalizationService to produce both old and new canonical fields in parallel, notify all partners via email 30 days prior, set schema_version sunset 60 days post-notification, then drop old transform_fn rows. DB migrations via golang-migrate run as ECS one-off task before rolling deployment.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM ddtrace middleware tags every request span with partner_id, schema_version, and endpoint. Custom StatsD metrics: feed_ingestion_lag_seconds (gauge per mls_board_id - seconds since last_cursor), normalization_error_rate (counter per mls_board_id), rate_limit_violations_total (counter per partner_id), bulk_export_flags_total (counter per partner_id), listings_indexed_total (counter per mls_board). Per-partner Datadog dashboard displaying daily quota consumption, rate limit violation history, bulk export flag events, and SLA compliance. PagerDuty alerts: feed_ingestion_lag > 30 min for any board (P2), API error rate > 2% over 5 min (P2), p99 latency > 200ms sustained 3 min (P3), bulk_export_flags_total increment (P3 for investigation).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate baseline: ~$180/month (3 api-server + 2 feed-worker tasks). Auto-scaling to 10 api-server tasks under peak partner load adds ~$400/month peak burst. RDS PostgreSQL r6g.xlarge multi-AZ + read replica: ~$480/month. Redis r6g.medium: ~$80/month. API Gateway: ~$3.50 per million requests. Datadog APM: ~$150/month for 5 hosts. Estimated baseline ~$900/month scaling to ~$1300/month at 50 partner peak. Cost controls: read replica scaled down off-hours, feed-worker tasks scale to 0 overnight when no MLS boards have pending polling windows, S3 Intelligent-Tiering for partner access log exports.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Feed ingestion for 3 MLS boards with schema_mappings configuration, canonical listings table with RESO field set, GET /v1/listings OData filter endpoint, GET /v1/listings/:listing_key, partner API key provisioning, Redis token-bucket rate limiting, sync_log audit trail, CI/CD pipeline, Datadog APM integration. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Accept-Version schema pinning (ISS-001 resolution), bulk export detection sorted-set implementation (ISS-002 resolution), GET /v1/listings/changes delta cursor endpoint, partner quota API, schema migration playbook tooling, mTLS for enterprise tier. Phase 3 (4 weeks): GraphQL endpoint for flexible RESO field selection, webhook push notifications for listing status changes, self-service partner onboarding API with key provisioning, advanced MLS board health analytics dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: OpenAPI 3.1 spec authored and partner-reviewed before any endpoint implementation. Go modules with pinned dependencies. golangci-lint with staticcheck and errcheck enforced in CI; no lint suppression without ADR. 80% unit test coverage gate; table-driven tests for all NormalizationService field mappings per schema_version. Schema_mapping changes require a backward-compatibility test suite proving old schema_version serializes correctly alongside new. Schema migration playbook checklist: deploy new mappings, confirm parallel output correct in staging, send partner notification email with sunset date, merge to production, monitor normalization_error_rate for 24h. PR approval from Go senior engineer required for rate limiting middleware or worker pool concurrency changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: MLS source feed schema changes without advance notice cause normalization failures and stale listings for partners. Mitigation: schema validation on ingestion with Datadog alerting on unexpected source fields; 4-business-hour SLA to update schema_mappings. Risk: PostgreSQL read replica replication lag under heavy feed ingestion causing stale data on delta sync. Mitigation: listing handlers fallback to primary if replication_lag_seconds > 10 per pg_stat_replication check. Tradeoff: Redis sorted-set bulk export detection adds ~3ms per listing fetch for ZADD call; accepted given exfiltration risk reduction. Tradeoff: RESO OData filter compliance constrains query API flexibility; accepted as contractual MLS partnership requirement. Tradeoff: per-partner Datadog dashboards add observability cost; justified by SLA enforcement requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected cursor-based delta sync on ModificationTimestamp over webhook push for MVP partner integration simplicity; webhook push deferred to Phase 3. Confirmed SHA-256 hash storage for API keys with raw key shown only at provisioning. Selected Redis sorted-set for bulk export detection over PostgreSQL counter to keep detection sub-millisecond. Confirmed RESO OData filter subset (filter, select, top, skipToken) sufficient for all known partner query patterns. Schema versioning via Accept-Version header confirmed over URL prefix versioning to avoid breaking existing RESO-compliant partner integrations.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision for partner-facing MLS listing API addressing schema versioning and bulk export detection gaps.

project_goal.value: Automate lease expiration monitoring across a multi-property portfolio by triggering renewal reminder workflows, escalation emails to property managers, and vacancy risk reports; integrates with existing property management software via API
project_goal.rationale: Manual tracking of 1200+ leases across 40 properties creates missed renewals and unplanned vacancies

target_users.value: Property managers, regional managers, and portfolio operations staff; tenant data processed but tenants are not direct users of this tool
target_users.rationale: Internal operations staff manage the renewal workflow; tenants receive email notifications

project_class.rationale: Scheduled batch processing with external integrations; no interactive frontend required

capabilities.rationale: Backend automation with PMS API integration and nightly batch job processing

complexity_level.rationale: Batch scheduling and integration complexity with configurable escalation rules

risk_level.rationale: Processes tenant PII and triggers financial-adjacent renewal decisions; no payment processing

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores tenant names, contact info, and lease terms which are personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Tool is internal operations infrastructure; no external API exposure

access_model.value: Internal service account authentication; outbound API calls to PMS use stored service credentials; no user-facing login
access_model.rationale: Automation tool runs as a GCP service account with least-privilege PMS API permissions

feature_scope.value: Nightly lease expiration scan with 30/60/90-day configurable windows, renewal reminder task assignment to responsible property manager, tenant email notification via PMS integration, escalation to regional manager on no-response, vacancy risk entry creation for portfolio forecasting, notification log for audit
feature_scope.rationale: Full automation workflow replacing manual spreadsheet tracking

mvp_scope.value: Nightly batch scan for leases expiring within 30/60/90 days, renewal_campaigns creation with assigned property manager, tenant reminder email trigger via PMS email API, notification_logs for audit trail
mvp_scope.rationale: Core automation delivering immediate value before escalation logic

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all outbound PMS API calls, PMS service credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager, tenant PII access logged, data minimization in notification payloads
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tool baseline security protecting tenant personal data

frontend_stack.value: none - automation tool with no interactive UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Headless automation; operational visibility via Grafana dashboard

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for health/status endpoints; APScheduler for cron scheduling
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem with strong PMS API client libraries; FastAPI for lightweight ops endpoints

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for lease and campaign state; no cache required
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for lease lifecycle state; Cloud SQL managed for GCP deployment

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run with scheduled job trigger via Cloud Scheduler
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container fits batch workload; Cloud Scheduler replaces cron management

privacy_retention_policy.value: Tenant PII retained only during active lease plus 3 years after termination for legal compliance; GDPR right-to-erasure supported via anonymization workflow; notification_logs retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR Article 5 data minimization and retention limitation requirements

future_scope.value: Tenant self-service renewal portal, predictive vacancy risk ML model, integration with additional PMS platforms beyond the current vendor
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap items pending MVP validation

constraints.value: Must integrate with current PMS vendor API (Yardi Voyager REST); nightly batch must complete within 2-hour window; no new PMS vendor changes in Q2
constraints.rationale: Operational constraint on PMS integration scope for initial delivery

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for batch job metrics, structured JSON logging to GCP Cloud Logging, PagerDuty alert for batch failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Infrastructure monitoring standard

execution_preference.value: Iterative 2-week sprints; batch logic tested with synthetic lease fixtures before PMS integration
execution_preference.rationale: Derisks PMS API integration by validating core batch logic first

compliance_context.value: GDPR compliance required for tenant personal data if operating in EU markets; data minimization in outbound payloads; right-to-erasure workflow; DPA with any third-party notification services used
compliance_context.rationale: Tenant contact data and lease terms qualify as personal data under GDPR Article 4

project.goal: Automate lease renewal reminder workflows and vacancy risk reporting across 40-property portfolio
project.target_users: Property managers and regional managers (internal)

backend.framework: FastAPI for health/status endpoints; APScheduler for nightly cron; SQLAlchemy 2.0 for ORM
backend.api: Internal health/status API only; no external API surface

security.auth: GCP service account with Secret Manager for PMS credentials
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all outbound PMS API calls
security.data_protection: Tenant PII logged only for audit trail; data minimization in email notification payloads

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with pytest, coverage, Docker build, Cloud Run deploy
devops.hosting: GCP Cloud Run triggered by Cloud Scheduler nightly

open_questions.pms_rate_limits: What are Yardi Voyager API rate limits for bulk lease queries - affects batch parallelism design

confirmed_decisions.notifications: PMS email API (no third-party email service to minimize DPA surface)

reasoner_reviews.product: The 30/60/90-day configurable expiration windows are the right abstraction for property managers with different renewal lead-time preferences by property type. The escalation workflow to regional manager after no-response is a key feature that closes the loop on missed renewals. Vacancy risk entries feeding portfolio forecasting distinguish this from a simple reminder bot.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Python 3.12 with APScheduler on Cloud Run is appropriate for this batch workload. The nightly Cloud Scheduler trigger with a 2-hour window is straightforward, but the Yardi Voyager API rate limits need to be confirmed before setting batch parallelism. SQLAlchemy 2.0 with async engine will allow concurrent PMS API calls without blocking the batch loop. Recommend implementing the escalation_rules table as a configurable entity rather than hardcoding escalation thresholds in code.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR compliance for tenant PII requires data minimization: notification email payloads should contain only the minimum fields needed (tenant first name, lease end date) and not full profile data. The right-to-erasure workflow must anonymize tenant records in leases and tenants tables, not just delete notification_logs. GCP Secret Manager rotation for Yardi credentials should be tested before production go-live.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 2-hour nightly batch window is achievable for 1200 leases if Yardi API calls are parallelized. Async Python with a semaphore-controlled concurrency limit (e.g., 10 concurrent API calls) should keep within Yardi rate limits while meeting the window. Cloud Run cold start latency is negligible for a nightly batch that runs once.

backend.service_design: Single Python Cloud Run service with two entrypoints: nightly_batch (triggered by Cloud Scheduler) runs the LeaseExpirationScanner, RenewalCampaignCreator, and EscalationEngine in sequence; health_api (FastAPI) exposes GET /health and GET /batch/status for ops monitoring. APScheduler not used in Cloud Run context; Cloud Scheduler triggers the batch container directly.
backend.business_modules: LeaseExpirationScanner: queries leases table for active leases with end_date within (today + window_days), groups by property_manager_id. RenewalCampaignCreator: creates renewal_campaigns records for each expiring lease without an existing active campaign, assigns to responsible property manager. EscalationEngine: checks existing campaigns past response_deadline, creates escalation_rules-driven notifications to regional_manager_id, logs vacancy_risks record for leases past 60-day window with no renewal response.
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Scheduler fires nightly at 02:00 UTC targeting Cloud Run batch endpoint. Batch processes leases in pages of 100 using async SQLAlchemy with 10 concurrent Yardi API call slots controlled by asyncio.Semaphore. Failed campaigns are retried up to 3 times before writing to a failed_campaigns set for manual review.
backend.failure_handling: Batch job emits Prometheus batch_job_success/batch_job_failure counters. On unhandled exception, job writes failure reason to batch_runs table and exits with non-zero code triggering Cloud Run error logging and PagerDuty alert. Individual campaign creation failures are logged with lease_id and error for manual review without stopping the batch.

security.auth_design: Cloud Run service runs as dedicated GCP service account with minimal IAM roles: Cloud SQL Client, Secret Manager Secret Accessor, Cloud Run Invoker (for Cloud Scheduler). Yardi Voyager API credentials stored in Secret Manager as JSON; rotated every 90 days with automated test on rotation.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR data minimization: tenant email notification payloads contain only tenant_first_name and lease_end_date, no full address or lease financial terms. Right-to-erasure: anonymization workflow replaces tenant PII fields with 'ANONYMIZED' token and clears contact fields; preserves lease financial record for legal retention. Tenant PII access logged to notification_logs with purpose field.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON logs to GCP Cloud Logging with fields: job_run_id, lease_id, action, outcome, timestamp. Sensitive fields (tenant_email) masked in logs. notification_logs table records every outbound email trigger with recipient_masked, template_id, sent_at for GDPR accountability.

data.entities: leases (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, property_id FK, unit_id, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, monthly_rent NUMERIC, renewal_status ENUM active/pending_renewal/renewed/vacated, assigned_manager_id FK, created_at). tenants (id UUID PK, first_name_encrypted TEXT, last_name_encrypted TEXT, email_encrypted TEXT, phone_encrypted TEXT, gdpr_anonymized BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at). renewal_campaigns (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, campaign_window_days INT, assigned_manager_id FK, escalation_threshold_days INT, status ENUM active/escalated/closed/cancelled, response_deadline DATE, created_at). escalation_rules (id UUID PK, property_id FK, window_days INT, escalation_to_id FK, notify_after_days INT). vacancy_risks (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, risk_level ENUM low/medium/high, reason TEXT, flagged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). notification_logs (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, recipient_masked TEXT, template_id TEXT, channel TEXT, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, gdpr_purpose TEXT). batch_runs (id UUID PK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, leases_scanned INT, campaigns_created INT, escalations_triggered INT, errors_json JSONB, status ENUM success/failed).
data.retention_and_deletion: Tenant PII retained during active lease plus 3 years post-termination; gdpr_anonymized flag triggers PII field clearing. notification_logs retained 2 years then archived to GCS. batch_runs retained 90 days.
data.migration_notes: Initial migration imports lease and tenant data from Yardi Voyager via bulk export; SQLAlchemy Alembic manages schema migrations with Cloud Run one-off migration task before each deployment.

devops.deployment_topology: Single Cloud Run service (batch + health API) deployed to GCP us-central1. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in same region with private IP connection via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. Cloud Scheduler job fires nightly at 02:00 UTC with 1-hour timeout. Secrets injected via Secret Manager volume mounts at container startup.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with fixtures for synthetic lease scenarios (30/60/90-day expirations), 85% coverage gate, Docker build, Artifact Registry push, Cloud Run deploy with --no-traffic for smoke test then traffic migration. Alembic migration test run against ephemeral Cloud SQL instance in CI.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus client in Python emitting: batch_leases_scanned_total, batch_campaigns_created_total, batch_escalations_triggered_total, batch_vacancy_risks_flagged_total, batch_duration_seconds, pms_api_call_duration_seconds. Grafana dashboard with last-7-days batch run history. PagerDuty alert on batch_job_failure metric increment.
devops.devops_risks: Yardi Voyager API changes or downtime block the nightly batch; mitigated by circuit breaker with retry and PagerDuty alert. Cloud Run cold start for a nightly batch is acceptable but container should be kept warm via a no-op health ping 5 minutes before batch time.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a GDPR-compliant lease renewal automation tool on GCP Cloud Run with nightly batch processing via Cloud Scheduler. Key decisions: Cloud Run over always-on Compute Engine fits the batch-only workload pattern with zero idle cost; async Python with asyncio.Semaphore for Yardi API concurrency within rate limits; tenant PII encryption at application layer with separate anonymization workflow for GDPR erasure requests; escalation_rules as a configurable table entity enabling property-specific escalation paths without code changes.
target_output.title: Property Lease Renewal Automation Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Cloud Run automation service that monitors lease expirations across a 40-property portfolio, triggers renewal reminder campaigns assigned to property managers, escalates to regional managers on non-response, and flags vacancy risks for portfolio forecasting. Integrates with Yardi Voyager REST API for PMS data sync. GDPR-compliant with tenant PII encryption, data minimization in notification payloads, and a right-to-erasure anonymization workflow.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Cloud Run service with two invocation modes: a nightly batch triggered by Cloud Scheduler at 02:00 UTC processes all active leases via LeaseExpirationScanner, RenewalCampaignCreator, and EscalationEngine modules; a FastAPI health API endpoint handles operational monitoring requests. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 stores lease lifecycle state, renewal campaign tracking, and escalation configurations via private IP with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy. Yardi Voyager REST API is queried asynchronously with a 10-slot semaphore for concurrency control within API rate limits. GCP Secret Manager stores Yardi credentials. All tenant PII encrypted at application layer before storage. Prometheus metrics exported to Grafana for batch run visibility.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg, Alembic for migrations, httpx for async Yardi API calls, prometheus-client for metrics. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run, Cloud Scheduler, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (private IP), Secret Manager, Cloud Logging, Artifact Registry. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with pytest, coverage, Docker build, Cloud Run rolling deploy. Monitoring: Prometheus client + Grafana dashboard, PagerDuty for batch failure alerts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Lease Expiration Scanner: queries leases for end_date within configurable 30/60/90-day windows, groups by property_manager_id, skips leases with active renewal_campaigns. Renewal Campaign Creator: creates renewal_campaigns per expiring lease, sets response_deadline per escalation_rules configuration, triggers tenant email notification via Yardi email API with data-minimized payload (first name + end date only). Escalation Engine: identifies campaigns past response_deadline, notifies regional_manager_id per escalation_rules, updates campaign status to escalated. Vacancy Risk Flagging: creates vacancy_risks records for leases 60+ days from expiry with no renewal response, tagged with risk_level (high if < 30 days). GDPR Erasure Workflow: anonymizes tenant PII fields on request while preserving lease financial record for legal retention period. Batch Run Audit: batch_runs table with full run statistics and errors_json for ops review.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Scheduler (nightly 02:00 UTC trigger targeting Cloud Run batch endpoint), Cloud Run batch+health service (LeaseExpirationScanner, RenewalCampaignCreator, EscalationEngine, GDPR anonymization endpoint, FastAPI health API), Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar (private IP connection to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (leases, tenants, renewal_campaigns, escalation_rules, vacancy_risks, notification_logs, batch_runs), GCP Secret Manager (Yardi Voyager credentials, Cloud SQL connection string), Yardi Voyager REST API (external PMS integration for lease sync and email triggers), GCP Cloud Logging (structured JSON batch logs), Prometheus + Grafana (batch job metrics dashboard).
target_output.workflows: Nightly Lease Scan Workflow: Cloud Scheduler fires at 02:00 UTC, invokes Cloud Run batch endpoint via HTTP POST; service fetches Yardi credentials from Secret Manager, opens Cloud SQL connection via Auth Proxy; LeaseExpirationScanner paginates leases table (pages of 100) filtering status=active and end_date between today and (today + 90 days); for each expiring lease without an active renewal_campaign, RenewalCampaignCreator inserts campaign record and calls Yardi email API with data-minimized payload {tenant_first_name, lease_end_date, manager_contact}; notification_log row written with recipient_masked and gdpr_purpose='renewal_reminder'; batch_runs record updated with counts and status=success on completion. Escalation Workflow: EscalationEngine queries renewal_campaigns where response_deadline < today and status=active; loads escalation_rules for each campaign's property_id; sends escalation notification to regional_manager_id via Yardi API; updates campaign status to escalated; creates vacancy_risks record with risk_level based on days remaining (high if < 30, medium if 30-60). GDPR Erasure Request: ops staff calls FastAPI POST /gdpr/erase?tenant_id=UUID; service sets tenants.gdpr_anonymized=true, clears encrypted PII fields (first_name_encrypted, email_encrypted, phone_encrypted) with 'ANONYMIZED' token, logs erasure action to Cloud Logging with timestamp and requestor; lease financial record retained per 3-year post-termination policy.
target_output.data_model: leases (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, property_id UUID FK, unit_id UUID FK, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, monthly_rent NUMERIC(10,2), renewal_status ENUM active/pending_renewal/renewed/vacated, assigned_manager_id UUID FK, yardi_lease_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tenants (id UUID PK, first_name_encrypted BYTEA, last_name_encrypted BYTEA, email_encrypted BYTEA, phone_encrypted BYTEA, gdpr_anonymized BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, yardi_tenant_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). properties (id UUID PK, name TEXT, address TEXT, portfolio_id UUID FK, regional_manager_id UUID FK). property_managers (id UUID PK, name TEXT, email TEXT, regional_manager_id UUID FK). renewal_campaigns (id UUID PK, lease_id UUID FK UNIQUE, campaign_window_days INT, assigned_manager_id UUID FK, escalation_threshold_days INT, response_deadline DATE, status ENUM active/escalated/closed/cancelled, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). escalation_rules (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, window_days INT, escalation_to_id UUID FK, notify_after_days INT, active BOOLEAN). vacancy_risks (id UUID PK, lease_id UUID FK, risk_level ENUM low/medium/high, reason TEXT, flagged_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). notification_logs (id UUID PK, lease_id UUID FK, recipient_masked TEXT, template_id TEXT, channel TEXT, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, gdpr_purpose TEXT). batch_runs (id UUID PK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, leases_scanned INT, campaigns_created INT, escalations_triggered INT, vacancy_risks_created INT, errors_json JSONB, status ENUM success/failed) partitioned by month.
target_output.api_design: Internal operational API only - no external or partner-facing endpoints. GET /health returns service status and last batch_run summary. GET /batch/status returns last 7 batch_runs records with status, duration, counts. GET /batch/status/:run_id returns full batch_runs record with errors_json for debugging. POST /gdpr/erase?tenant_id=UUID triggers tenant PII anonymization workflow (internal ops only, protected by GCP IAM invoker role). POST /batch/trigger (Cloud Scheduler invocation endpoint, authenticated via OIDC token from Cloud Scheduler service account). All endpoints return structured JSON; errors include error_code and message fields.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP service account with minimal IAM: Cloud SQL Client, Secret Manager Secret Accessor, Cloud Run Invoker. Yardi Voyager API credentials in Secret Manager as JSON secret; 90-day rotation with automated connectivity test on rotation. Tenant PII (names, email, phone) encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a key from GCP Cloud KMS before storage in BYTEA columns. Data minimization: notification payloads contain only first_name (decrypted at send time) and lease_end_date; no full contact profile sent to Yardi email API. GDPR right-to-erasure: POST /gdpr/erase anonymizes PII fields in tenants table while preserving lease financial record per 3-year legal retention. notification_logs retain gdpr_purpose field for GDPR accountability. All Cloud Run invocations authenticated via OIDC; Cloud Scheduler uses dedicated service account. Structured logs mask tenant_email field. Data minimization applied to batch_runs errors_json (no PII in error messages).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest with synthetic lease fixtures covering 30/60/90-day expiration scenarios and escalation edge cases, 85% coverage gate, Alembic migration smoke test against ephemeral Cloud SQL, Docker build, Artifact Registry push tagged with commit SHA. Deployment: Cloud Run deploy --no-traffic with smoke test (GET /health), then 100% traffic migration. Alembic migration run as Cloud Run one-off job before traffic migration. Environments: dev (Cloud Run min-instances 0, Cloud SQL db-f1-micro), staging (Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2, mirrors production data volume for batch timing validation), production (Cloud Run max-instances 1 - single instance for batch, Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-4, private IP). Cloud Scheduler job: schedule 0 2 * * *, timezone UTC, retry on failure up to 2 attempts, timeout 7200 seconds. Secrets rotated via Secret Manager with Cloud Run automatic latest-version pickup on next cold start.
target_output.observability: Prometheus client emitting batch job metrics: batch_leases_scanned_total (counter), batch_campaigns_created_total (counter), batch_escalations_triggered_total (counter), batch_vacancy_risks_flagged_total (counter), batch_duration_seconds (histogram), pms_api_call_duration_seconds (histogram per endpoint), pms_api_errors_total (counter per error code). Grafana dashboard: 7-day batch run history, campaigns-per-property heatmap, escalation rate trend, vacancy risk count by property. GCP Cloud Logging: structured JSON per lease processed with fields job_run_id, lease_id, action, outcome, duration_ms; sensitive fields masked. PagerDuty alert on batch_job_failure metric increment (P2) and on batch not completing within 2-hour window (P2).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run: ~$5/month for nightly batch execution (billed only during run, ~30 min at 2 vCPU/4GB). Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-4: ~$130/month. Secret Manager: negligible. Cloud Logging: ~$10/month. Grafana Cloud free tier sufficient. Total estimated ~$150/month. Scaling: Cloud Run max-instances 1 prevents parallel batch runs causing duplicate campaigns; if lease portfolio exceeds 5000 leases and batch exceeds 2-hour window, increase Cloud SQL to db-n1-standard-8 and widen asyncio.Semaphore concurrency limit.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (5 weeks): Cloud SQL schema with leases, tenants, renewal_campaigns, notification_logs, batch_runs tables; LeaseExpirationScanner for 30/60/90-day windows; RenewalCampaignCreator with Yardi email API integration (data-minimized payloads); Cloud Scheduler nightly trigger; FastAPI health API; Prometheus metrics; Grafana dashboard; GDPR erasure endpoint. Phase 2 (3 weeks): EscalationEngine with escalation_rules table, regional manager notifications, vacancy_risks creation; configurable escalation_threshold_days per property; Grafana escalation rate tracking. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Yardi Voyager bi-directional sync (write renewal status back to PMS on campaign close), batch_runs analytics dashboard for portfolio reporting, GDPR compliance report generation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Async SQLAlchemy 2.0 with explicit session lifecycle management - no implicit session re-use across batch iterations to prevent connection pool exhaustion. All tenant PII access must go through designated encrypt/decrypt service functions; direct column reads of encrypted fields forbidden outside those functions. Synthetic lease fixtures required for all batch logic tests - no real tenant data in tests. pytest parametrize for 30/60/90-day window edge cases and escalation threshold boundary conditions. 85% test coverage gate enforced in CI. GDPR erasure function tested with before/after PII field verification. Alembic migrations reviewed for irreversibility before merge to main. No PII in exception messages, log statements, or errors_json fields.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Yardi Voyager API downtime or rate limit changes block nightly batch, causing missed renewal reminders. Mitigation: circuit breaker with 3 retries and PagerDuty P2 alert; batch_runs failure record allows manual re-trigger next morning. Risk: asyncio.Semaphore concurrency limit misconfigured too high hits Yardi rate limits and triggers API key suspension. Mitigation: integration test in staging environment with production-representative data volume before go-live. Tradeoff: application-layer AES encryption for tenant PII over column-level transparent encryption - provides portability if Cloud SQL KMS integration changes. Tradeoff: Cloud Run single-instance max for batch prevents parallel runs at the cost of no horizontal scale; acceptable for 1200-lease portfolio but requires revisiting at 5000+ leases.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Run over always-on Cloud Run service to minimize idle compute cost for once-nightly batch. Confirmed Yardi Voyager email API over third-party SendGrid to minimize GDPR data processor agreements. Selected AES-256-GCM application-layer encryption for tenant PII over PostgreSQL pgcrypto for consistency with future multi-cloud portability requirement. GDPR right-to-erasure implemented as anonymization (not deletion) to preserve lease financial records required by 3-year legal retention policy.

metadata.notes: GDPR-compliant lease renewal automation on GCP Cloud Run with Yardi Voyager integration and right-to-erasure support.

project_goal.value: Migrate from a legacy rules-based Automated Valuation Model to an LLM-augmented pipeline that ingests comparable sales, tax records, and listing photos to generate narrative appraisal summaries alongside numeric estimates
project_goal.rationale: Legacy AVM produces numeric estimates only with no narrative context; appraisers need plain-language summaries to satisfy USPAP documentation requirements

target_users.value: Licensed real estate appraisers and review appraisers who submit property addresses and validate AI-generated valuation reports before client delivery
target_users.rationale: Primary users are credentialed appraisers, not end consumers; output is a professional appraisal tool aid

project_class.rationale: Core value is LLM pipeline integration with ML valuation model; AI is the primary differentiator

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, ai_llm, auth, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack with LLM pipeline, ML model integration, and appraiser-facing UI

complexity_level.rationale: Shadow-mode parallel AVM operation, LLM pipeline orchestration, and photo analysis require multi-system coordination

risk_level.rationale: Valuation outputs affect property transactions with financial and Fair Housing compliance implications

data_sensitivity.rationale: Comparable sales, tax records, and valuation estimates are financial data with PCI-adjacent sensitivity

external_exposure.rationale: Only credentialed appraisers with Auth0 accounts can access the system

access_model.value: Auth0 with MFA; RBAC with appraiser and review_appraiser roles; review_appraiser can override valuation estimates
access_model.rationale: Professional licensing structure requires role differentiation between working appraisers and review staff

feature_scope.value: Property address submission, comparable sales fetch, tax record ingestion, photo analysis via vision model, ML AVM numeric estimate, LLM narrative summary generation, shadow-mode parallel legacy AVM comparison, appraiser review and override workflow, appraisal report export PDF
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration feature set with shadow-mode for accuracy validation before legacy decommission

mvp_scope.value: Property submission, comp sales fetch, ML valuation model estimate, LLM narrative summary, shadow-mode legacy AVM parallel output, appraiser review UI
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP validates LLM narrative quality against legacy AVM before adding photo analysis

security_baseline.value: Auth0 MFA, TLS 1.3, financial data encrypted at rest with KMS, LLM API calls routed through VPC endpoint to avoid public internet exposure, audit log for every valuation request
security_baseline.rationale: High risk with financial data requires strong baseline including LLM API isolation

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for report pages, TypeScript for team consistency with backend

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Python ML ecosystem; FastAPI for async LLM orchestration pipeline

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for valuation records and comp sales; Redis 7 for LLM response caching by property fingerprint
data_platform.rationale: Relational for financial data integrity; Redis reduces LLM API costs on repeat queries for same property

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers; ECS for ML model serving without Kubernetes overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Valuation records retained 7 years per USPAP record-keeping requirements; comp sales data retained indefinitely as market reference; LLM prompts and responses retained 90 days for audit
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: USPAP mandates appraisal record retention for 5 years minimum; 7-year policy matches state licensing requirements

future_scope.value: Photo analysis via vision model for property condition scoring, MLS direct integration replacing manual comp searches, automated bias monitoring dashboard for Fair Housing compliance
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap pending shadow-mode validation results

constraints.value: Shadow-mode must run in parallel for minimum 90 days before legacy AVM decommission; LLM provider must support AWS VPC endpoint; model outputs must include confidence intervals for USPAP compliance
constraints.rationale: 90-day shadow period is the contractual commitment to appraisers for validation before transition

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, LLM call cost tracking per model_run, shadow-mode accuracy delta metrics between legacy and new estimates
observability_baseline.rationale: LLM cost visibility and accuracy comparison are critical operational requirements

execution_preference.value: 4-week sprints; shadow-mode validation phase runs concurrently with Phase 2 development
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel shadow mode and development maximizes validation window

compliance_context.value: USPAP (Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice) requires documented methodology and appraiser oversight of AI outputs; Fair Housing Act compliance requires bias monitoring on valuation estimates by neighborhood demographics; no AI-only final values - appraiser must review and certify
compliance_context.rationale: USPAP Standards Rule 1-1 requires appraisers to identify and analyze all relevant data; AI outputs are tools aids requiring human certification

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o via AWS PrivateLink VPC endpoint for narrative summary generation; structured prompt includes comp sales table, tax record fields, and AVM numeric estimate; response parsed for narrative body and confidence statement; cached in Redis by property fingerprint (address hash + comp_set_hash) for 24 hours
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o selected for narrative quality; VPC endpoint required by security baseline; caching reduces API cost on appraiser re-runs

project.goal: Migrate legacy rules-based AVM to LLM-augmented pipeline with shadow-mode parallel operation
project.target_users: Licensed appraisers and review appraisers
project.timeline: Shadow-mode live in 12 weeks; legacy decommission after 90-day validation

frontend.state: React Query for server state; local form state with React Hook Form

backend.framework: FastAPI with async endpoints for LLM pipeline orchestration

security.auth: Auth0 with MFA; RBAC appraiser/review_appraiser roles
security.transport: TLS 1.3; LLM API via VPC endpoint
security.encryption: Financial data AES-256 at rest with KMS

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for valuation records and comp_sales
data.cache: Redis 7 for LLM response cache keyed by property fingerprint

devops.ci: GitLab CI with pytest, coverage, Docker build, ECS deploy

constraints.confidence_intervals: Required in all model outputs for USPAP

open_questions.comp_data_source: Which comp sales data provider - CoreLogic or Zillow Zestimate API - is confirmed for integration?

reasoner_reviews.product: The shadow-mode parallel operation is the right risk mitigation strategy for migrating licensed appraisers off a tool they rely on for professional compliance. The 90-day validation period is sufficient to gather accuracy statistics across property types. The USPAP requirement for human appraiser certification of AI outputs must be a hard workflow gate, not an optional step.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Python 3.12 FastAPI with async LLM calls is appropriate for the pipeline orchestration pattern. The LLM response cache in Redis keyed by property fingerprint is a good cost control, but the cache invalidation strategy needs definition - comp sales data changes daily so a 24-hour TTL may produce stale narratives for active market properties. The shadow-mode comparison requires a dedicated shadow_runs table storing both legacy and new estimates for statistical comparison.
reasoner_reviews.security: Financial data encryption at rest with KMS is correct. LLM API calls via VPC endpoint prevent prompt data from traversing the public internet. The audit log for every valuation request is required for USPAP and Fair Housing compliance evidence. Prompt injection risk should be documented - comp sales data ingested from external providers could contain adversarial content that manipulates the LLM narrative.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The confidence interval requirement for USPAP means the ML valuation model must expose uncertainty bounds, not just a point estimate. This needs to be confirmed with the ML model team before committing to the architecture. The 90-day shadow period timeline is tight for Q2 delivery if the comp data source integration is not yet confirmed.

backend.service_design: Three FastAPI service modules: ValuationOrchestrator (coordinates comp fetch, tax record fetch, AVM model call, LLM narrative generation in async pipeline), ShadowModeRunner (parallel legacy AVM call with result stored to shadow_runs table for comparison), AppraisalReportService (assembles report from model_runs record, formats for PDF export). Background worker handles async LLM calls to avoid blocking appraiser UI.
backend.llm_integration_design: LLM pipeline in ValuationOrchestrator: build structured prompt including comp_sales table (last 6 comps by distance and recency), tax record fields (assessed_value, last_sale_date, last_sale_price, sqft), and AVM numeric estimate with confidence interval. Call OpenAI GPT-4o via AWS PrivateLink endpoint. Parse JSON response for narrative_body and confidence_statement fields. Cache response in Redis keyed by SHA-256 of (address_normalized + comp_set_ids sorted + avm_estimate). TTL 24 hours. On cache hit, log cache_hit metric. Prompt template versioned in DB as llm_prompt_templates table.
backend.api_patterns: POST /valuations (submit property address, returns valuation_request_id for async polling). GET /valuations/:id (poll status: pending/processing/complete/failed). GET /valuations/:id/report (full appraisal report JSON). POST /valuations/:id/override (review_appraiser role only - override estimate with reason). GET /shadow-runs/comparison (admin endpoint returning accuracy metrics between legacy and new estimates).
backend.backend_risks: LLM API latency (2-8 seconds) creates UX friction on valuation submission; mitigated by async processing with polling endpoint and UI progress indicator. Comp data provider API downtime blocks valuation pipeline; mitigated by retry with exponential backoff and cached comp data fallback for same-address repeat requests.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with three main feature areas: ValuationWorkspace (property submission, pipeline progress, report review), ShadowModeConsole (review_appraiser dashboard comparing legacy vs new estimates during 90-day period), AdminDashboard (model performance metrics, LLM cost tracking).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Property Submission: appraiser enters address and selects comp search radius; form submits to POST /valuations; polling loop against GET /valuations/:id shows pipeline stage (fetching_comps, running_avm, generating_narrative, complete). Report Review: displays AVM estimate with confidence interval, LLM narrative, comparable sales table with map, legacy AVM shadow estimate (labeled 'reference only'). Override Flow: review_appraiser can edit estimate field and submit override reason via POST /valuations/:id/override; override logged to audit_events.
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state (valuation polling, report data). Zustand slice for active valuation pipeline stage tracking. No Redux - data flow is request-response, not event-sourced.
frontend.performance_notes: LLM response cached in Redis reduces repeat submission latency. Next.js SSR used only for report pages (SEO not needed; appraiser tool is private). Static generation for admin dashboard skeleton. Image optimization via next/image for listing photo display.

security.auth_design: Auth0 SPA application for Next.js frontend with MFA enforcement for all roles. Auth0 Management API for user provisioning. Backend FastAPI validates Auth0 JWT on every request; 15-minute token TTL with silent refresh.
security.authorization_model: RBAC with two roles: appraiser (submit valuations, view own reports) and review_appraiser (view all reports, access shadow comparison, submit overrides). Role claim in Auth0 JWT validated per endpoint.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table records: valuation submission (appraiser_id, property_address, timestamp), LLM call (valuation_id, model_version, prompt_hash, response_hash, latency_ms, cost_usd), appraiser override (review_appraiser_id, original_estimate, override_value, reason). Required for USPAP and Fair Housing evidence.
security.security_risks: Prompt injection via malicious data in comp sales or tax records; mitigated by input sanitization before prompt assembly and output validation that narrative contains expected sections. LLM prompt logging (prompt_hash only, not full prompt) prevents sensitive financial data in logs.

data.entities: properties (id UUID PK, address_normalized TEXT, latitude NUMERIC, longitude NUMERIC, property_type ENUM, sqft INT, bedrooms SMALLINT, bathrooms NUMERIC, year_built SMALLINT). comparable_sales (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, sale_price NUMERIC, sale_date DATE, distance_miles NUMERIC, sqft INT, source TEXT, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tax_records (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, assessed_value NUMERIC, last_sale_date DATE, last_sale_price NUMERIC, tax_year SMALLINT, source TEXT). valuation_requests (id UUID PK, property_id FK, appraiser_id FK, status ENUM pending/processing/complete/failed, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_runs (id UUID PK, valuation_request_id FK, avm_estimate NUMERIC, confidence_low NUMERIC, confidence_high NUMERIC, llm_narrative TEXT, llm_model_version TEXT, llm_cost_usd NUMERIC, run_duration_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). shadow_runs (id UUID PK, valuation_request_id FK, legacy_estimate NUMERIC, new_estimate NUMERIC, delta_pct NUMERIC, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). appraisal_reports (id UUID PK, valuation_request_id FK, final_estimate NUMERIC, overridden BOOLEAN, override_reason TEXT, certified_by UUID FK, certified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_s3_key TEXT).
data.schema_notes: comparable_sales fetched fresh per valuation request; cached in Redis for 24h by address_normalized. model_runs stores both legacy and new estimates during shadow period. llm_prompt_templates table stores versioned prompt templates by template_version for audit reproducibility.
data.analytics_events: Datadog metrics: valuation_pipeline_duration_seconds (histogram by stage), llm_call_cost_usd (counter per model_run), shadow_mode_delta_pct (histogram for accuracy tracking), cache_hit_rate (gauge), appraiser_override_rate (gauge).

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: api service (FastAPI, 2-4 tasks, autoscale on CPU), frontend service (Next.js, 2 tasks), background-worker service (async LLM pipeline jobs, 1-3 tasks autoscale on SQS queue depth). RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ. Redis ElastiCache. SQS queue for async valuation pipeline jobs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: pytest with mocked LLM responses and synthetic comp data, 80% coverage gate, docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Staging environment runs shadow-mode against production comp data snapshot for accuracy testing.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy 100%. LLM prompt template changes versioned in DB - rollback by decrementing active_template_version flag, no code deploy needed.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an LLM-augmented property valuation system migrating from a legacy rules-based AVM. Key decisions: async FastAPI pipeline for LLM orchestration with SQS-backed background workers to decouple submission latency from LLM call time; Redis LLM response cache keyed by property fingerprint to control GPT-4o costs; shadow_runs table capturing legacy and new estimates in parallel for the mandatory 90-day validation period; audit_events table serving dual purpose for USPAP record-keeping and Fair Housing bias monitoring evidence. The moderate quality designation reflects that the photo analysis capability is deferred to Phase 2 and the comp data provider integration has an open question.
target_output.title: LLM-Augmented Property Valuation Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 and Python 3.12 FastAPI AI system migrating legacy rules-based AVM valuation to an LLM-augmented pipeline on AWS ECS Fargate. Appraisers submit property addresses; the system fetches comparable sales, tax records, runs an ML valuation model for numeric estimates with confidence intervals, and calls GPT-4o via AWS PrivateLink to generate USPAP-compatible narrative summaries. Shadow-mode parallel operation stores legacy and new estimates in shadow_runs for 90-day accuracy validation before legacy decommission. USPAP compliance enforced via mandatory appraiser certification workflow.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier ECS Fargate deployment: Next.js 14 frontend, Python 3.12 FastAPI API service, and async background worker service. Appraiser submits property address via Next.js; API creates valuation_request and enqueues pipeline job to SQS; background worker executes ValuationOrchestrator - fetching comp sales from external provider API, tax records from county data feed, running ML AVM model for numeric estimate with confidence interval, building structured LLM prompt, calling GPT-4o via AWS PrivateLink VPC endpoint, caching narrative in Redis by property fingerprint. Shadow-mode runner executes legacy AVM in parallel and writes shadow_runs comparison record. API polling endpoint serves pipeline status and final report to frontend. RDS PostgreSQL 16 stores all valuation records, comp sales, and audit events. Auth0 with MFA controls appraiser access.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, React Hook Form. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, asyncpg, httpx for comp/tax API calls, openai SDK via VPC endpoint. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, SQS standard queue, S3 for PDF report storage, AWS PrivateLink for OpenAI VPC endpoint, Auth0, ECR, GitLab CI. Monitoring: Datadog APM with LLM cost tracking metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Valuation Pipeline: address submission, async comp sales fetch (last 6 comps by distance/recency), tax record ingestion, ML AVM numeric estimate with confidence_low/confidence_high, GPT-4o narrative summary generation via structured prompt, Redis LLM response cache (24h TTL by property fingerprint). Shadow Mode: parallel legacy AVM execution, shadow_runs delta storage, accuracy comparison dashboard for review appraisers. Appraiser Workflow: pipeline progress polling via GET /valuations/:id, report review with comp sales table, confidence interval display, legacy shadow estimate labeled 'reference only'. Override Flow: review_appraiser can edit final estimate and submit reason, logged to audit_events. Report Export: PDF generation from appraisal_reports record with certified_by and certified_at fields for USPAP compliance.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate api service (FastAPI, valuation submission, polling, report endpoints, Auth0 JWT validation), ECS Fargate frontend service (Next.js 14 SSR for report pages), ECS Fargate background-worker (ValuationOrchestrator, ShadowModeRunner, PDF generator, SQS consumer), RDS PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ (all valuation records, comp sales, tax records, audit events), ElastiCache Redis 7 (LLM response cache keyed by property fingerprint, comp sales 24h cache), SQS standard queue (valuation pipeline jobs), S3 (PDF appraisal report storage with KMS SSE), AWS PrivateLink endpoint for OpenAI API (isolates LLM traffic from public internet), Auth0 (appraiser MFA authentication and RBAC).
