security_baseline.rationale: PCI compliance required for ACH and card processing; field encryption protects stored payment credentials beyond network-level TLS

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, React Query, Azure AD B2C MSAL for authentication
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in React; MSAL integrates natively with Azure AD B2C

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django 5, Django REST Framework, Stripe SDK, Celery for async jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Django ORM and admin for staff management; Celery for payment webhook processing and CMMS sync

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Django sessions and Celery broker
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL with automatic failover; Redis for session and async job queue

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service (Python runtime) for Django backend; Azure Static Web Apps for React frontend; Azure Blob Storage for lease documents
hosting_target.rationale: Azure ecosystem aligns with Azure AD B2C and Key Vault; App Service managed TLS

privacy_retention_policy.value: Rent payment records retained 7 years per financial record law; lease documents retained for duration of tenancy plus 3 years; communication logs retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State landlord-tenant law specifies minimum record retention periods

future_scope.value: Resident mobile app, package delivery notifications, community amenity booking, integration with smart lock system
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap features for resident experience enhancement

constraints.value: Must integrate with Yardi property management system via webhook; Azure-only deployment; PCI-DSS SAQ-D compliance required for card and ACH storage
constraints.rationale: Yardi is the system of record; Azure constraint from enterprise agreement; PCI required for payment method storage

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor + Application Insights for Django backend; custom alerts for payment failure rate > 2% and maintenance request backlog > 50 unassigned
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Azure Monitor investment; payment failures need immediate operational response

execution_preference.value: MVP in 10 weeks; security review before each phase launch; PCI assessment before payment feature goes live
execution_preference.rationale: PCI pre-assessment ensures compliance posture before real payment processing begins

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ-D for card and ACH payment method storage; GDPR compliance if EU tenants present; state landlord-tenant communication record requirements
compliance_context.rationale: ACH tokenization stored in Django backend triggers PCI SAQ-D scope; GDPR applies to any EU resident tenants

project.goal: Tenant self-service portal with rent payment, maintenance, and lease documents
project.target_users: Residential tenants and building management staff

backend.async: Celery with Redis broker for payment webhooks and CMMS sync

security.payment_pci: Stripe Elements for card tokenization; Stripe ACH for bank account
security.encryption: Field-level encryption on payment_methods table via django-encrypted-fields

data.db: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server
data.documents: Azure Blob Storage for lease PDFs

devops.hosting: Azure App Service + Azure Static Web Apps

open_questions.yardi_webhook_schema: Confirm Yardi webhook event schema for maintenance work order status updates

reasoner_reviews.product: The tenant portal feature set is well-scoped for MVP. Rent payment is the highest-value feature for adoption. Maintenance request with CMMS auto-routing eliminates the most common tenant complaint about slow response times.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django with DRF is a sound choice for this data model. The Yardi webhook integration is the highest integration risk — Yardi's API documentation quality is inconsistent; recommend building a dedicated webhook adapter service rather than inline Django view handling.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI SAQ-D scope is significant — storing ACH tokens in Django requires encryption at rest at field level, not just disk-level encryption. Azure Key Vault key rotation must be tested before production payment launch. MFA via Azure AD B2C must be enforced for all tenants, not just staff.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure App Service Python runtime supports Django 5 without issues. Celery with Azure Cache for Redis is well-supported. Azure Static Web Apps with React 18 is straightforward.

backend.service_design: Django 5 monolith with apps: tenants (profile, lease), payments (Stripe integration, ACH), maintenance (request, CMMS sync), documents (Blob Storage access), announcements (building feed). Celery worker for payment webhook processing and Yardi CMMS sync jobs.
backend.api_patterns: Django REST Framework ViewSets with router. JWT from Azure AD B2C validated via django-jwt-auth middleware. Stripe webhook endpoint at /api/webhooks/stripe/ with signature verification.
backend.failure_handling: Celery retry with exponential backoff for Yardi CMMS webhook delivery failures. Stripe payment failure triggers tenant notification via email and records payment_attempts entry.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with React Router v6. MSAL provider wraps app for Azure AD B2C token management. Route-based code splitting.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (MSAL redirect), Dashboard (rent balance, maintenance status), Pay Rent (/payments/new — Stripe Elements form), Maintenance (/maintenance — submit request with photo upload), Documents (/documents — lease PDF viewer), Announcements (/announcements)

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C user flows for sign-up, sign-in, and password reset. MFA enforced via Conditional Access policy. MSAL in React handles token refresh silently.
security.authorization_model: Django object-level permissions: tenants can only access Lease, RentPayment, and MaintenanceRequest objects linked to their tenant_id. Staff have building-scoped access via BuildingStaffAssignment join table.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for Stripe API keys, Django SECRET_KEY, database password. Azure App Service MSI (Managed Identity) for Key Vault access — no credentials in environment variables.

data.entities: tenants (id, azure_ad_oid, name, email, phone, unit_id FK, created_at), leases (id, tenant_id FK, unit_id FK, start_date, end_date, monthly_rent NUMERIC, document_blob_key, status ENUM active/expired), rent_payments (id, tenant_id FK, lease_id FK, amount NUMERIC, payment_method_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id, status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, paid_at), maintenance_requests (id, tenant_id FK, unit_id FK, category ENUM plumbing/electrical/hvac/general, description, photo_blob_keys ARRAY, status ENUM submitted/assigned/in_progress/completed, yardi_work_order_id, created_at), payment_methods (id, tenant_id FK, type ENUM card/ach, stripe_payment_method_id_encrypted, last4, created_at), lease_documents (id, lease_id FK, doc_type ENUM lease/addendum/inspection, blob_key, uploaded_at), building_announcements (id, building_id FK, title, body, published_at)
data.retention_and_deletion: rent_payments retained 7 years. maintenance_requests retained 3 years post-tenancy. payment_methods soft-deleted on tenant departure; hard-deleted after 90 days.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service Plan (B2 SKU) for Django backend. Azure Static Web Apps for React frontend. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (General Purpose, 2 vCores). Azure Cache for Redis (C1 SKU). Azure Blob Storage for lease documents and maintenance photos. Azure CDN for static web app assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps: lint (flake8, black check), test (pytest with 80% coverage), build Docker image, push to Azure Container Registry, deploy to App Service via az webapp deployment. React: eslint, jest, build, Azure Static Web Apps deploy action.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass plan for a security-hardened tenant portal. Weak quality per manifest — plan sections are functional and correct but deliberately thinner than a strong plan. Key decisions: Django with DRF for rapid development, Azure AD B2C for MFA authentication, Stripe for PCI-compliant payment processing with SAQ-D scope managed through field-level encryption on stored ACH tokens. Yardi webhook integration needs a dedicated adapter to handle Yardi's unpredictable payload format.
target_output.title: Security-Hardened Tenant Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack tenant portal built with React 18 and Django 5 on Azure, enabling multifamily tenants to pay rent via ACH or card, submit maintenance requests, and access lease documents with MFA enforced by Azure AD B2C. PCI-DSS SAQ-D compliance is maintained through Stripe tokenization and field-level encryption on stored payment method tokens. Yardi CMMS integration routes maintenance requests to work orders via Celery async jobs.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier architecture: React 18 SPA on Azure Static Web Apps communicates over HTTPS to Django 5 REST API on Azure App Service. Tenants authenticate via Azure AD B2C with MFA; MSAL handles token refresh in the React client. Stripe Elements handles card and ACH tokenization in the browser — no raw payment credentials reach the Django server. Celery workers on a separate App Service process Stripe payment webhooks and sync maintenance request status from Yardi via outbound webhook calls. Lease documents stored in Azure Blob Storage with SAS URL generation for time-limited tenant access. Azure Key Vault holds all application secrets accessed via Managed Identity.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Router v6, React Query, MSAL for Azure AD B2C, Stripe.js Elements. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework, Celery 5, django-encrypted-fields for payment token encryption, Stripe Python SDK. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server. Cache/Queue: Azure Cache for Redis 7 (Celery broker + Django sessions). Storage: Azure Blob Storage for documents and maintenance photos. Auth: Azure AD B2C. Secrets: Azure Key Vault with MSI. CI/CD: Azure DevOps.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Rent Payment: Stripe Elements form collects card or ACH; PaymentIntent confirmed server-side; payment_methods stored with field encryption; payment history in ledger. Maintenance Request: tenant submits category and description with photo uploads to Blob Storage; Celery job creates Yardi work order via API; tenant receives status updates as work order progresses. Lease Documents: tenant views and downloads PDFs from Azure Blob Storage via short-lived SAS URLs. Building Announcements: staff publishes notices visible to all tenants in assigned building. Staff Dashboard: management view of maintenance queue by building and assignment status.
target_output.system_components: Azure Static Web Apps: React 18 SPA with Azure CDN. Azure App Service: Django 5 API (B2 SKU). Azure App Service Worker: Celery async jobs. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16: primary datastore. Azure Cache for Redis 7: Celery broker and Django sessions. Azure Blob Storage: lease documents and maintenance photos. Azure Key Vault: Stripe keys, Django secret, DB password. Azure AD B2C: tenant and staff authentication with MFA.
target_output.workflows: Rent Payment: tenant opens Pay Rent page; React renders Stripe Elements card or ACH form; on submit, Stripe.js creates PaymentMethod token; React POSTs token to POST /api/payments/; Django creates Stripe PaymentIntent, confirms charge, writes rent_payments record; Stripe webhook delivers payment_intent.succeeded to /api/webhooks/stripe/; Celery task updates rent_payments.status to succeeded and sends receipt email. Maintenance Request: tenant fills category, description, and uploads up to 3 photos; React POSTs to POST /api/maintenance/; Django stores maintenance_request record and uploads photos to Blob Storage; Celery job calls Yardi API to create work order and stores yardi_work_order_id; Yardi sends status webhook to /api/webhooks/yardi/ as work order progresses; Django updates maintenance_requests.status and emails tenant.
target_output.data_model: tenants (id UUID PK, azure_ad_oid, name, email, phone, unit_id FK, created_at). leases (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, unit_id FK, start_date, end_date, monthly_rent NUMERIC, document_blob_key, status ENUM active/expired). rent_payments (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, lease_id FK, amount NUMERIC, payment_method_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id, status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, paid_at). maintenance_requests (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, unit_id FK, category ENUM plumbing/electrical/hvac/general, description, photo_blob_keys JSONB, status ENUM submitted/assigned/in_progress/completed, yardi_work_order_id, created_at). payment_methods (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, type ENUM card/ach, stripe_payment_method_id_encrypted BYTEA, last4, created_at, deleted_at). lease_documents (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, doc_type ENUM lease/addendum/inspection, blob_key, uploaded_at). building_announcements (id UUID PK, building_id FK, title, body, published_at).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/tenants/me/ — tenant profile. GET /api/leases/current/ — active lease details. POST /api/payments/ — initiate rent payment with Stripe token. GET /api/payments/ — payment history. POST /api/maintenance/ — submit maintenance request. GET /api/maintenance/ — tenant's request list. GET /api/documents/ — lease document list with SAS URL. GET /api/announcements/ — building announcements. POST /api/webhooks/stripe/ — Stripe event receiver (signature verified). POST /api/webhooks/yardi/ — Yardi work order status receiver (HMAC verified).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C MFA enforced for all tenants and staff. MSAL handles silent token refresh; tokens expire after 1 hour. PCI-DSS SAQ-D: Stripe Elements tokenizes card and ACH in browser; Django stores only stripe_payment_method_id encrypted via django-encrypted-fields with Azure Key Vault key. Django CSRF middleware enabled on all non-webhook endpoints. Webhook endpoints use Stripe signature verification and Yardi HMAC. Azure Key Vault MSI for secret access — no credentials in App Service configuration. Object-level permission checks on all DRF ViewSets ensure tenants cannot access other tenants' records. Azure App Service TLS minimum version 1.2.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps CI pipeline: flake8 lint, pytest with 80% coverage gate, build Docker image, push to Azure Container Registry. CD: az webapp deployment container config to deploy to App Service. React pipeline: eslint, jest unit tests, bun run build, Azure Static Web Apps deploy action. Environments: dev (App Service F1 free tier, PostgreSQL Burstable B1ms), staging (B1 + General Purpose 2 vCore), production (B2 + General Purpose 2 vCore, high-availability enabled). Database migrations via Django management command in release phase. Secrets rotated via Azure Key Vault 90-day rotation policy.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights SDK in Django for request tracing and exception tracking. Custom events: payment_succeeded, payment_failed, maintenance_request_created, yardi_sync_failed. Azure Monitor alerts: payment failure rate > 2% triggers PagerDuty; Celery queue depth > 100 jobs alerts ops channel; PostgreSQL CPU > 80% for 10 minutes alerts DBA.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure App Service B2 ~$75/month. PostgreSQL General Purpose 2 vCore ~$140/month. Azure Cache for Redis C1 ~$55/month. Azure Blob Storage ~$5/month. Total ~$275/month for MVP. Scaling: App Service scale-out to 3 instances on CPU 70% target covers rent payment peak on the 1st of each month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): React SPA with MSAL auth, rent payment via Stripe, maintenance request submission, lease document viewer, Django API, Yardi webhook adapter, PCI pre-assessment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Staff management dashboard, building announcements, payment history analytics, Celery monitoring dashboard. Phase 3 (future): Mobile app, smart lock integration, amenity booking.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: black formatter, flake8, mypy strict mode. Django: DRF serializers for all API inputs; no direct model access in views. Field-level encryption applied to all payment_methods.stripe_payment_method_id fields via django-encrypted-fields decorator. PR requires security team review for any payment or auth code changes. No payment data in application logs.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Yardi API reliability — Yardi webhooks are documented inconsistently and sometimes drop events. Mitigation: Celery periodic reconciliation job polls Yardi work order status for open maintenance requests every 15 minutes as a fallback. Risk: PCI SAQ-D scope creep if additional payment methods added beyond Stripe-tokenized card and ACH. Mitigation: all payment method handling routed through Stripe SDK; no direct card data processing. Tradeoff: Django monolith chosen over microservices for MVP development speed; module boundaries maintained for future service extraction.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Yardi webhook HMAC secret obtained from property management team. Stripe ACH tokenization uses Stripe Financial Connections for bank account verification. Azure AD B2C selected over Entra External ID for tenant auth due to existing B2C tenant in org. Lease document SAS URL expiry set to 15 minutes to minimize exposure window.

metadata.notes: First-pass tenant portal with PCI SAQ-D and Azure AD B2C MFA; weak quality per manifest with functional but thinner sections; Yardi CMMS integration is the key domain-specific integration challenge.

profile.domain: Real Estate Investor Market Analytics Mobile App

project_goal.value: Data-intensive mobile app for real estate investors aggregating market analytics across hundreds of metro areas — price-per-sqft trends, cap rate benchmarks, rental yield histories, and neighborhood demographic shifts — supporting portfolio acquisition decisions with watchlist alerts and submarket drill-downs
project_goal.rationale: Investors currently piece together data from 5 separate tools; unified mobile analytics platform reduces decision cycle time

target_users.value: Real estate investors and acquisition analysts managing multi-market portfolios
target_users.rationale: Target persona: institutional and high-net-worth individual investors tracking 10-50 metro markets simultaneously

project_class.rationale: Mobile-first product for investors accessing analytics on the go; React Native targets iOS and Android from shared codebase

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Mobile UI, FastAPI backend, ClickHouse analytics, Firebase Auth, nightly ETL from MLS and county recorder APIs

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source ETL pipeline, columnar analytics queries across billions of historical data points, real-time threshold alerts

risk_level.rationale: Contains proprietary market analytics; no payment processing or healthcare data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Market analytics data is proprietary business intelligence; investor watchlist preferences are internal user data

external_exposure.rationale: App requires Firebase Auth account; no anonymous market data access

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with subscription tier enforcement (free/pro/enterprise); ClickHouse query routing based on subscription tier — free tier limited to 10 markets, pro to 100, enterprise unlimited
access_model.rationale: Tiered access controls analytics depth and breadth per subscription

feature_scope.value: Metro and submarket market overview (price/sqft trends, cap rates, rental yields), neighborhood demographic heatmaps, investor watchlist with configurable metric thresholds, push notification alerts when thresholds crossed, portfolio acquisition calculator, historical comparison across user-selected date ranges
feature_scope.rationale: Core analytics and alerting features for acquisition decision support

mvp_scope.value: Metro-level price-per-sqft trends, cap rate benchmarks, watchlist with push alerts for 10 markets — no demographic heatmaps or portfolio calculator in MVP
mvp_scope.rationale: Core analytics and alerting validate product-market fit before building advanced features

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT, TLS 1.3, FastAPI JWT middleware, ClickHouse access via internal VPC only (no direct internet exposure), GCP Secret Manager for ETL API keys
security_baseline.rationale: ClickHouse contains proprietary analytics; must not be internet-accessible

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript, Expo SDK 51, Victory Native for charting, Firebase Auth SDK
frontend_stack.rationale: Expo managed workflow for faster CI/CD; Victory Native for performant mobile charting

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; ClickHouse Python driver for analytics queries
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async for low-latency mobile API responses; ClickHouse Python client for vectorized analytics

data_platform.value: ClickHouse on GCP (self-managed cluster) for analytics; Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore for API cache and alert state
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse columnar storage enables sub-second aggregation over billions of historical listing transactions; Redis caches ClickHouse query results for repeated mobile requests

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for FastAPI backend; ClickHouse cluster on GCE instances (n2-standard-8, 3 nodes); Cloud Scheduler for nightly ETL; Cloud Memorystore for Redis
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run scales to zero overnight; ClickHouse requires dedicated GCE nodes for IOPS predictability

privacy_retention_policy.value: Market analytics data retained indefinitely as business asset; investor watchlist and alert preferences retained for account lifetime plus 30 days post-deletion; ETL source data retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Historical market data is the core product value; investor preferences deleted promptly on account deletion

future_scope.value: Neighborhood demographic heatmaps with Census data integration, portfolio acquisition calculator, institutional deal sourcing alerts, desktop web companion
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP features for pro and enterprise tier differentiation

constraints.value: ClickHouse query p95 must be < 2 seconds on mobile; nightly ETL must complete before 6 AM ET to ensure fresh data for morning analysis sessions; GCP only
constraints.rationale: Morning session timing is when most investors review market data; stale data at 6 AM undermines the product value

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for FastAPI and ETL metrics; custom ClickHouse query latency dashboard; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Grafana stack; Firebase Crashlytics for mobile-specific crash data

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; ETL pipeline built in sprint 1 to validate data quality before analytics UI development
execution_preference.rationale: Analytics app is only as good as its data; ETL first approach ensures data quality before UI investment

project.goal: Mobile analytics app for real estate investors with ClickHouse-backed market data
project.target_users: Real estate investors and acquisition analysts

frontend.framework: React Native with TypeScript, Expo SDK 51
frontend.charts: Victory Native for price/sqft trend lines, cap rate bar charts
frontend.state: Zustand for market selection and watchlist state; React Query for API data fetching

backend.framework: FastAPI with async ClickHouse queries via clickhouse-driver
backend.cache: Redis for ClickHouse result caching (10-minute TTL for market aggregates)

data.analytics_db: ClickHouse cluster (3 nodes, GCE n2-standard-8) for listings_history and aggregations
data.cache: Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 for FastAPI response cache and alert state
data.etl: Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Run ETL jobs for nightly MLS and county recorder data ingestion

open_questions.demographic_data: Census API integration for demographic heatmaps deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The investor use case is well-defined. Watchlist threshold alerts are the stickiest feature — investors who configure market alerts will open the app daily. The MVP focus on 10 metro markets validates the data pipeline and alerting before scaling to hundreds of metros.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ClickHouse columnar storage is the right choice for aggregate queries over billions of listing transactions. The key design decision is the materialized view strategy — pre-computing monthly price-per-sqft and cap rate rollups per submarket at ETL time dramatically reduces query complexity and latency. Round 1 plan queried raw listings_history without materialized views, which is why p95 exceeded the 2-second SLA in load testing.
reasoner_reviews.security: ClickHouse cluster on GCE with no public IP is correct. The FastAPI VPC connector to ClickHouse must be validated — Cloud Run to GCE VPC connectivity requires Serverless VPC Access connector configuration that was missing from Round 1 plan.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ETL completion before 6 AM ET with nightly MLS data ingestion is achievable if the pipeline processes incrementally rather than full-reload. Round 1 ETL did a full ClickHouse table reload nightly — at current data volume this takes 7 hours and misses the 6 AM deadline. Incremental insert with deduplication is required.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: markets (metro and submarket analytics queries), watchlist (CRUD for investor watchlist and thresholds), alerts (threshold evaluation and FCM push dispatch), etl_status (ingestion run status).
backend.api_patterns: GET /api/v1/markets/{metro_id}/summary — returns pre-aggregated price_per_sqft_trend, cap_rate_benchmark, rental_yield_history from ClickHouse materialized views. GET /api/v1/submarkets/{submarket_id}/trends — submarket drill-down. POST /api/v1/watchlist/ — add market to watchlist with threshold config.
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Scheduler triggers ETL Cloud Run job nightly at 10 PM ET (8-hour window before 6 AM ET deadline). Threshold evaluation job runs every 15 minutes, queries ClickHouse for latest market metrics, compares against watchlist thresholds, dispatches FCM push via Firebase Admin SDK on breach.
backend.scaling_notes: Cloud Run scales to zero overnight. ClickHouse query cache in Redis prevents repeated identical queries from concurrent mobile users during morning peak session.

frontend.app_structure: Expo SDK 51 managed workflow. src/screens/ for Market Overview, Submarket Detail, Watchlist, Alerts History, Settings. src/components/ for MetricCard, TrendChart, SubmarketHeatmap (Phase 2). Navigation: React Navigation v6 bottom tab navigator.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Market Overview screen: metro selector, price/sqft trend chart (Victory Native LineChart, 24-month range), cap rate benchmark bar chart, rental yield sparklines. Submarket Detail: drill-down from metro overview, 5-year price history, neighborhood stats. Watchlist screen: list of watched markets with current metric values and threshold indicators. Alert push notification: taps open Market Overview screen for the triggered market.
frontend.performance_notes: React Query caches market API responses for 10 minutes — users revisiting a market within 10 minutes see instant load. Victory Native charts use SVG rendering; memoized with useMemo on dataset to prevent re-render on unrelated state changes. Expo Updates over-the-air for non-native bug fixes without App Store review.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth with email/password and Google Sign-In. FastAPI validates Firebase JWT on every request via firebase-admin verify_id_token. Subscription tier stored in Firebase custom claims — FastAPI reads tier claim to enforce market access limits.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency checks firebase_claims.subscription_tier before ClickHouse query execution. Free tier queries restricted to 10 market IDs via SQL WHERE metro_id IN (user_allowed_metros). Enterprise tier has no restriction.
security.secrets_management: GCP Secret Manager for MLS API keys, county recorder API credentials, Firebase service account JSON. Cloud Run and Cloud Run ETL jobs access secrets via Workload Identity.

data.entities: markets (id, metro_name, state, msa_code, timezone, active BOOL), submarkets (id, metro_id FK, name, neighborhood_type ENUM urban/suburban/rural, lat, lng), listings_history (metro_id, submarket_id, listing_id, sale_price FLOAT, sqft INT, cap_rate FLOAT, rental_yield FLOAT, property_type ENUM sfr/multifamily/condo, sale_date DATE) — ClickHouse MergeTree engine partitioned by sale_date. market_aggregates (metro_id, submarket_id, period_month DATE, avg_price_per_sqft FLOAT, median_cap_rate FLOAT, avg_rental_yield FLOAT, transaction_count INT) — ClickHouse materialized view refreshed by ETL. investor_watchlists (id, user_id, metro_id FK, threshold_metric ENUM price_per_sqft/cap_rate/rental_yield, threshold_value FLOAT, direction ENUM above/below, created_at) — PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL. cap_rate_benchmarks (id, metro_id FK, property_type, benchmark_cap_rate FLOAT, as_of_date DATE)
data.storage_design: ClickHouse cluster on GCE (3 nodes, ReplicatedMergeTree for HA) for listings_history and market_aggregates. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for investor_watchlists and user profiles. Cloud Memorystore Redis for API response cache.
data.analytics_events: ETL run metrics tracked in Cloud Monitoring: records_ingested, deduplication_rate, ETL_duration_minutes, source_api_errors. Push alert metrics: alerts_evaluated, alerts_triggered, FCM_delivery_rate.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run (us-central1): FastAPI backend (max 10 instances, concurrency 80). ClickHouse cluster: 3x GCE n2-standard-8 instances (us-central1), Serverless VPC Access connector for Cloud Run to ClickHouse private IP. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL (db-g1-small) for watchlist data. Cloud Memorystore Redis (M1 SKU). Cloud Scheduler: nightly ETL trigger at 10 PM ET, threshold evaluation every 15 minutes.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with coverage gate, build Docker, push to GCR, deploy to Cloud Run via gcloud run deploy. Expo EAS Build for React Native iOS and Android builds. EAS Submit for App Store and Google Play submission.
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloud Run traffic splitting allows instant rollback to previous revision via gcloud run services update-traffic. ClickHouse schema migrations use ADD COLUMN (non-breaking) pattern; no destructive schema changes without maintenance window.

ISS-001.title: ETL full reload misses 6 AM deadline at current data volume
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 ETL drops and reloads entire ClickHouse listings_history table nightly; at 2.4B rows this takes 7 hours starting at 10 PM ET, missing the 6 AM deadline by 1 hour

history.note: ETL timing failure identified in staging simulation

ISS-002.title: FastAPI to ClickHouse connectivity not configured for Cloud Run VPC
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan assumed direct Cloud Run to GCE connectivity; Cloud Run requires Serverless VPC Access connector to reach GCE instances on private IP; without it, ClickHouse must be publicly exposed which violates the security baseline

history.note: Infrastructure gap identified by security reviewer

focus_issues.title: ETL full reload misses 6 AM deadline at current data volume
focus_issues.detail: Full reload takes 7 hours starting at 10 PM ET; must switch to incremental insert with sale_date-based deduplication

focus_issues.title: FastAPI to ClickHouse connectivity not configured for Cloud Run VPC
focus_issues.detail: Cloud Run requires Serverless VPC Access connector to reach ClickHouse on GCE private IP; missing from Round 1 infrastructure plan

latest_recommendations: Switch ETL from full reload to incremental insert using sale_date >= last_run_date filter with ReplacingMergeTree deduplication on ClickHouse
Add Serverless VPC Access connector to Cloud Run service configuration; ClickHouse GCE instances must not have public IPs
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Real Estate Investor Analytics Mobile App — Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved; ETL deadline breach and VPC connectivity gap are production blockers

previous_audits.summary: Strong ClickHouse analytics design and mobile charting approach but ETL architecture and network connectivity are not production-ready
strengths: ClickHouse MergeTree with materialized view pre-aggregation is correct for sub-2s mobile query latency
Firebase Auth custom claims for subscription tier enforcement is clean and avoids backend DB lookup per request
React Query 10-minute cache correctly reduces ClickHouse load during morning peak sessions
concerns: ETL full reload at 2.4B rows takes 7 hours — misses 6 AM ET fresh data deadline by 1 hour
Cloud Run to GCE private IP requires Serverless VPC Access connector — not present in Round 1 plan
blocking_issues: ETL must switch to incremental with sale_date-based deduplication to complete within 8-hour window
Serverless VPC Access connector must be configured before ClickHouse can be kept off public internet
recommendations: ETL Cloud Run job: query ClickHouse for max(sale_date) per metro, fetch only records after that date from MLS API, insert with ReplacingMergeTree deduplication key (listing_id, sale_date)
Cloud Run service YAML: add vpcAccess.connector pointing to Serverless VPC Access connector in same region/VPC as ClickHouse GCE instances

issue_updates.title: ETL full reload misses 6 AM deadline
issue_updates.detail: Full reload takes 7h; window is 8h but leaves only 1h buffer — insufficient

issue_updates.title: Cloud Run VPC connector missing
issue_updates.detail: ClickHouse on GCE not reachable from Cloud Run without VPC connector

previous_plan.title: Real Estate Investor Analytics Mobile App — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React Native Expo app backed by FastAPI and ClickHouse for metro-level real estate market analytics. Round 1 nightly ETL did a full table reload (7h, misses 6 AM deadline) and Cloud Run lacked VPC connector to reach ClickHouse on GCE private IP.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI on Cloud Run querying ClickHouse on GCE for market aggregates. Round 1: no Serverless VPC Access connector; ClickHouse connectivity assumed direct IP (insecure). ETL: full reload of listings_history via Cloud Run nightly.
previous_plan.data_model: listings_history (MergeTree, full reload nightly — no incremental), market_aggregates (materialized view, recomputed on full reload), investor_watchlists (PostgreSQL).
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run FastAPI, GCE ClickHouse 3 nodes, Cloud Scheduler nightly ETL — VPC Access connector absent from infrastructure plan.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 closes ISS-001 (ETL incremental with ReplacingMergeTree deduplication reduces ETL runtime from 7h to ~90 minutes) and ISS-002 (Serverless VPC Access connector for Cloud Run to ClickHouse private IP). Moderate quality per manifest — plan is good overall with one or two sections slightly thinner than a strong plan. The ClickHouse materialized view strategy is the key architectural differentiator for sub-2s mobile query latency.

fix_report.action_taken: Switched ETL from full table reload to incremental insert: ETL Cloud Run job queries ClickHouse for MAX(sale_date) per metro, fetches only MLS records after that date, inserts with ReplacingMergeTree deduplication on (listing_id, sale_date) key; market_aggregates materialized view updated via OPTIMIZE TABLE after insert; estimated runtime reduces from 7 hours to 60-90 minutes at current 2.4B row volume
fix_report.expected_outcome: ETL completes by 1 AM ET, well within the 6 AM deadline; nightly data freshness SLA met; ClickHouse storage growth controlled by deduplication

fix_report.action_taken: Added Serverless VPC Access connector (vpc-connector-analytics) to Cloud Run service YAML: vpcAccess.connector = projects/re-analytics-prod/locations/us-central1/connectors/vpc-connector-analytics; ClickHouse GCE instances have no external IP; Cloud Run FastAPI reaches ClickHouse via connector on 10.0.1.x private range
fix_report.expected_outcome: ClickHouse cluster has no public internet exposure; FastAPI queries ClickHouse on private IP within VPC; security baseline satisfied

target_output.title: Real Estate Investor Analytics Mobile App — Round 2
target_output.executive_summary: React Native mobile app for real estate investors providing metro and submarket analytics — price-per-sqft trends, cap rate benchmarks, and rental yield histories — backed by ClickHouse columnar analytics on GCP. Nightly ETL ingests MLS and county recorder data incrementally; FastAPI on Cloud Run queries pre-aggregated ClickHouse materialized views for sub-2 second mobile responses. Round 2 resolves the ETL deadline breach (incremental ReplacingMergeTree inserts) and ClickHouse VPC connectivity gap (Serverless VPC Access connector). Firebase Auth enforces subscription tier access limits per investor account.
target_output.architecture_overview: Mobile-to-analytics architecture: React Native Expo app authenticates via Firebase Auth and calls FastAPI on Cloud Run, which queries ClickHouse for market analytics via a Serverless VPC Access connector on the GCE private VPC — ClickHouse cluster has no public IP. FastAPI caches frequent market aggregate responses in Cloud Memorystore Redis (10-minute TTL) to reduce ClickHouse load during morning peak sessions. Nightly ETL runs as a Cloud Run job triggered by Cloud Scheduler at 10 PM ET, incrementally fetching new listing transactions from MLS and county recorder APIs and inserting to ClickHouse with ReplacingMergeTree deduplication — completing in under 2 hours. A threshold evaluation Cloud Run job runs every 15 minutes, comparing latest market aggregates from ClickHouse against investor watchlist thresholds and dispatching FCM push notifications via Firebase Admin SDK on breach. Investor watchlists and user profiles stored in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74, TypeScript, Expo SDK 51, React Navigation v6, Zustand for market selection state, React Query for API caching, Victory Native for trend charts, Firebase Auth SDK. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, clickhouse-driver (async), firebase-admin for JWT verification, Redis client. Analytics DB: ClickHouse 24.x self-managed cluster (3 nodes, GCE n2-standard-8, ReplicatedMergeTree + ReplacingMergeTree). Operational DB: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (db-g1-small) for watchlists. Cache: Cloud Memorystore Redis 7. ETL: Python Cloud Run jobs. CI/CD: GitHub Actions + Expo EAS Build. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus, Firebase Crashlytics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Market Overview: metro-level price-per-sqft 24-month trend chart, cap rate benchmark compared to national median, rental yield history from ClickHouse market_aggregates materialized view. Submarket Drill-Down: neighborhood-level 5-year price history, transaction volume, property type breakdown. Watchlist with Threshold Alerts: investor configures metric thresholds (cap rate > 6.5%, price/sqft < $180) per market; 15-minute evaluation job triggers FCM push on breach. Historical Comparison: user-selectable date range query against listings_history for custom analytics. Subscription Tier Enforcement: Firebase custom claims restrict free users to 10 metros, pro to 100, enterprise unlimited — enforced in FastAPI before ClickHouse query.
target_output.system_components: React Native Expo app (iOS + Android): Firebase Auth, Victory Native charts, React Query API layer. Cloud Run FastAPI (us-central1): market analytics API, watchlist CRUD, alert dispatch. Serverless VPC Access connector: private connectivity from Cloud Run to GCE VPC. ClickHouse cluster (3x GCE n2-standard-8): ReplicatedMergeTree listings_history partitioned by sale_date, market_aggregates materialized view. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL: investor_watchlists, user_profiles. Cloud Memorystore Redis 7: FastAPI response cache and alert deduplication state. Cloud Run ETL jobs: nightly incremental MLS ingest and county recorder sync. Cloud Scheduler: ETL trigger (10 PM ET) and threshold evaluation trigger (every 15 minutes). Firebase Admin SDK: FCM push notification dispatch.
target_output.workflows: Nightly ETL (Incremental — ISS-001 fix): Cloud Scheduler triggers ETL Cloud Run job at 10 PM ET; job queries ClickHouse SELECT MAX(sale_date) FROM listings_history WHERE metro_id = ? for each active metro; for each metro, calls MLS API with date_after=max_sale_date to fetch only new records; inserts to ClickHouse listings_history using ReplacingMergeTree; after all metros processed, calls OPTIMIZE TABLE listings_history FINAL to deduplicate; materialized view market_aggregates refreshes automatically; ETL completes by midnight ET. Threshold Alert Evaluation: Cloud Scheduler triggers every 15 minutes; threshold evaluator queries market_aggregates for latest monthly metrics per watched metro; compares each investor watchlist threshold; for breached thresholds, checks Redis alert_deduplication key (prevents repeat alerts within 4-hour window); dispatches FCM push via Firebase Admin SDK; logs alert_triggered event to Cloud Monitoring.
target_output.data_model: listings_history (metro_id LowCardinality(String), submarket_id String, listing_id String, sale_price Float64, sqft UInt32, cap_rate Float32, rental_yield Float32, property_type Enum8(sfr=1, multifamily=2, condo=3), sale_date Date) — ClickHouse ReplicatedReplacingMergeTree partitioned by toYYYYMM(sale_date), deduplication key (listing_id, sale_date). market_aggregates — ClickHouse materialized view: GROUP BY metro_id, submarket_id, toStartOfMonth(sale_date) computing avg_price_per_sqft, median_cap_rate, avg_rental_yield, transaction_count; refreshed on listings_history insert. markets (id, metro_name, state, msa_code, timezone, active BOOL) — Cloud SQL. submarkets (id, metro_id FK, name, neighborhood_type, lat FLOAT, lng FLOAT) — Cloud SQL. investor_watchlists (id UUID PK, user_id, metro_id, threshold_metric ENUM price_per_sqft/cap_rate/rental_yield, threshold_value FLOAT, direction ENUM above/below, last_alerted_at, created_at) — Cloud SQL. cap_rate_benchmarks (metro_id, property_type, benchmark_cap_rate FLOAT, as_of_date DATE) — ClickHouse.
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/markets/{metro_id}/summary — price_per_sqft_trend array, cap_rate_benchmark, rental_yield_history, transaction_volume (ClickHouse market_aggregates, Redis cache 10min). GET /api/v1/markets/{metro_id}/submarkets — submarket list with latest metrics. GET /api/v1/submarkets/{submarket_id}/trends?range=5y — submarket price history. GET /api/v1/watchlist/ — investor watchlist with current metric values. POST /api/v1/watchlist/ — add market with threshold config. DELETE /api/v1/watchlist/{id}/ — remove from watchlist. GET /api/v1/alerts/history/ — investor alert history. GET /api/v1/etl/status/ — latest ETL run timestamp and records ingested (for data freshness indicator in app).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth JWT validated on every FastAPI request via firebase-admin verify_id_token; subscription tier read from custom claims without additional DB lookup. Serverless VPC Access connector ensures ClickHouse cluster GCE instances have no public IP — only reachable from Cloud Run via private VPC range. GCP Secret Manager for MLS API keys and county recorder credentials; Cloud Run and ETL jobs access secrets via Workload Identity. Cloud SQL investor_watchlist database accessed from Cloud Run via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy — no direct PostgreSQL port exposure. FCM push notifications use Firebase service account authenticated via Secret Manager. Rate limiting on FastAPI: 30 market queries per minute per user to prevent ClickHouse overload from a single investor account.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run FastAPI service (us-central1): max-instances 10, concurrency 80, vpcAccess.connector = vpc-connector-analytics for ClickHouse private IP access. ClickHouse cluster: 3x GCE n2-standard-8 instances (us-central1-a, us-central1-b, us-central1-c for rack diversity), ReplicatedMergeTree with ZooKeeper on co-located smaller instances for replica coordination, no public IPs. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL db-g1-small (us-central1). Cloud Memorystore Redis M1 SKU. Cloud Scheduler jobs: nightly ETL (10 PM ET), threshold evaluation (*/15 * * * *). GitHub Actions CI: pytest 80% coverage, Docker build, push GCR, gcloud run deploy. Expo EAS Build for iOS and Android binary builds; EAS Submit for App Store and Play Store. ETL rollback: ClickHouse ATTACH/DETACH partition allows restoring prior day partition if corrupt data inserted.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics from FastAPI: clickhouse_query_duration_seconds histogram (alert if p95 > 2s), cache_hit_ratio by endpoint, alert_evaluations_total. Grafana dashboard: ClickHouse query latency p50/p95/p99 by metro, ETL duration and records_ingested per run, alert trigger rate, Cloud Run instance count. Firebase Crashlytics for React Native crash reports with stack traces and device info. Cloud Monitoring: ETL completion timestamp vs 6 AM ET SLA (alert if ETL not completed by 4 AM ET), FCM delivery rate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run FastAPI ~$30/month (scale-to-zero overnight). GCE ClickHouse 3x n2-standard-8 ~$600/month (committed use discount available). Cloud SQL db-g1-small ~$25/month. Cloud Memorystore Redis M1 ~$35/month. Estimated total: ~$700/month. ClickHouse scales vertically — at 10B rows, upgrade to n2-standard-16 nodes. Cloud Run scales horizontally on concurrency.
target_output.phased_implementation: Sprint 1 (2 weeks): ETL Cloud Run job with incremental ReplacingMergeTree insert (ISS-001 fix); Serverless VPC Access connector; ClickHouse cluster provisioning; market_aggregates materialized view. Sprint 2 (2 weeks): FastAPI market summary and submarket endpoints with Redis caching; Firebase Auth integration with tier enforcement; Cloud SQL watchlist schema. Sprint 3 (2 weeks): React Native Expo app with Market Overview screen, Victory Native trend charts, Firebase Auth login. Sprint 4 (2 weeks): Watchlist CRUD, threshold alert evaluation job, FCM push integration. Sprint 5 (2 weeks): Historical comparison UI, Grafana dashboard, Expo EAS Build for App Store/Play Store submission.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: black, mypy, pytest with 80% coverage. ClickHouse query tests use test ClickHouse instance in CI (testcontainers-python). React Native: ESLint, Jest for unit tests, Detox for E2E on simulator. No direct ClickHouse queries outside the analytics service module — all queries through typed query functions. ETL jobs log record counts and source API response times to Cloud Monitoring for data quality tracking.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree deduplication is eventually consistent — OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL is called after ETL but a query during OPTIMIZE may see duplicates. Mitigation: market_aggregates materialized view reads from listing_id distinct count; brief duplicate window during OPTIMIZE is acceptable for analytics (not a financial transaction system). Risk: MLS API rate limits may delay incremental ETL for metros with high transaction volume. Mitigation: per-metro ETL jobs run in parallel Cloud Run tasks; rate limit errors trigger retry with exponential backoff. Tradeoff: ClickHouse self-managed on GCE chosen over ClickHouse Cloud for cost ($600/month vs ~$1500/month at this data volume) but requires manual cluster management.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Incremental ETL with ReplacingMergeTree resolving ISS-001 ETL deadline breach. Serverless VPC Access connector resolving ISS-002 ClickHouse connectivity gap. Firebase custom claims chosen for subscription tier over Cloud SQL lookup to minimize latency on every API request. Threshold evaluation every 15 minutes chosen over real-time streaming for cost efficiency — investors accept 15-minute alert delay per product research.

metadata.notes: Round 2 mobile analytics app; fixes ETL incremental loading and Cloud Run VPC connector; moderate quality with strong ClickHouse design but slightly thin cost section.

profile.domain: Luxury Residential Brokerage Marketing Landing Page

project_goal.value: Security-hardened marketing landing page for a luxury residential brokerage featuring property galleries, agent profiles, and an encrypted inquiry form protecting high-net-worth client contact data from scraping and spam.
project_goal.rationale: Brand protection and lead quality are paramount for luxury market positioning.

target_users.value: High-net-worth property buyers and sellers seeking luxury residential representation; also agents checking their own profile listings.
target_users.rationale: Primary conversion goal is qualified buyer and seller inquiry submissions.

project_class.rationale: Static content with serverless form relay; no backend persistence on this surface.

capabilities.rationale: Static site with conversion and visitor analytics; no backend runtime.

complexity_level.rationale: Static pages with serverless inquiry relay; complexity is in content not architecture.

risk_level.rationale: No credentials, payment, or health data; inquiry data forwarded immediately to CRM.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Inquiry form collects name, email, phone, and budget range — PII requiring GDPR/CCPA handling.

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing site; no authentication wall.

access_model.value: Fully public browsing; inquiry form available without login; no user accounts on this surface.
access_model.rationale: Minimizing friction for prospect inquiries is a business requirement.

feature_scope.value: Property showcase galleries with high-resolution imagery, agent profile pages, neighborhood guides, encrypted inquiry form with Cloudflare Turnstile bot detection, GDPR/CCPA cookie consent, analytics event tracking.
feature_scope.rationale: Full content surface for luxury brand positioning and lead capture.

mvp_scope.value: Homepage with property gallery, 5 agent profiles, inquiry form with Turnstile, Cloudflare Pages deployment.
mvp_scope.rationale: Core brand presence and lead capture for soft launch.

security_baseline.value: Cloudflare Turnstile on inquiry form, CSP headers, no raw contact data stored on hosting infrastructure, HTTPS only.
security_baseline.rationale: Bot scraping and spam submission prevention for high-value lead forms.

frontend_stack.value: Astro with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, headless CMS (Contentful) for property and agent content.
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro's island architecture minimizes JS for performance; Contentful enables non-technical content updates.

backend_stack.value: None; Cloudflare Pages Functions for inquiry form handling only.
backend_stack.rationale: Static site with serverless function boundary for form relay.

data_platform.value: None; content sourced from Contentful CMS; no database on hosting infrastructure.
data_platform.rationale: Data minimization — no PII stored on Cloudflare Pages infrastructure.

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages with global edge network.
hosting_target.rationale: Edge delivery for image-heavy property galleries; Pages Functions for serverless form relay.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No PII retained on hosting infrastructure; inquiry submissions forwarded to Salesforce CRM immediately; no logs retaining contact data on Cloudflare.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR/CCPA data minimization; retention governed by CRM policies.

future_scope.value: Virtual property tour embeds, agent scheduling widget, multi-language support for international buyers.
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap for international luxury market expansion.

constraints.value: No server-side database. All high-resolution images must load under 3 seconds on mobile. No PII stored on Cloudflare infrastructure.
constraints.rationale: Performance and privacy constraints from brand and legal requirements.

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Analytics for traffic and bot rate; custom GA4 events for inquiry form submissions, gallery interactions, and agent profile views.
observability_baseline.rationale: Lead funnel analytics and brand performance tracking.

execution_preference.value: Contentful webhook triggers Cloudflare Pages rebuild on content publish; GitHub Actions for code changes.
execution_preference.rationale: Non-technical content team needs self-serve content publishing without developer involvement.

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU visitors: cookie consent banner, data subject rights notice, no tracking without consent. CCPA for California visitors: opt-out notice. CAN-SPAM compliance for follow-up email sequences initiated from CRM after form submission.
compliance_context.rationale: Luxury brokerage serves international HNW buyers triggering GDPR jurisdiction; California operations trigger CCPA.

project.goal: Luxury brokerage brand presence with secure lead capture for high-net-worth buyer and seller inquiries
project.target_users: HNW buyers, sellers, and referral prospects

security.no_pii_storage: form data relayed to CRM, not stored on hosting

devops.environments: preview per branch, production on main

reasoner_reviews.product: Three rounds of iteration have tightened the design: gallery performance, Turnstile integration, and GDPR consent are all addressed. The remaining risk is ensuring the Contentful webhook rebuild does not expose a window where stale content is served during long rebuild cycles.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro with Contentful is the correct pairing for content-heavy luxury pages. Pages Functions for Turnstile verification and Salesforce relay keeps the architecture stateless. Recommend adding Cloudflare KV as a temporary submission buffer only if Salesforce API has latency issues — but default to direct relay.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 2 resolved the CSP inline script issue. Cloudflare Turnstile server-side token verification in the Pages Function is correctly implemented. GDPR cookie consent gating GA4 is compliant. CAN-SPAM compliance is the responsibility of the CRM email sequences, not the landing page.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Image performance constraint (3s on mobile) is achievable with Astro image optimization pipeline generating responsive srcsets and WebP variants at build time. Contentful webhook rebuilds should complete in under 60 seconds for the current page count.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 left the Contentful rebuild SLA undefined. Round 3 must specify the expected rebuild time and what happens to form submissions during a rebuild window — Pages Functions remain live during static rebuild, so form relay is unaffected.

target_output.title: Luxury Residential Brokerage Marketing Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A security-hardened Astro static site on Cloudflare Pages serving as the primary brand and lead capture surface for a luxury residential brokerage. Property showcase galleries, agent profiles, and neighborhood guides are sourced from Contentful CMS with automated edge deployments. An encrypted inquiry form protected by Cloudflare Turnstile and server-side token verification relays high-net-worth client contact data directly to Salesforce — with zero PII retained on Cloudflare infrastructure. GDPR and CCPA compliance enforced via Osano consent management.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generator builds 42 pages at deploy time from Contentful CMS content, outputting fully static HTML with island hydration only for the inquiry form and gallery lightbox components. Cloudflare Pages serves the static output from 300+ edge locations globally. A Cloudflare Pages Function at /functions/api/inquiry.ts handles Turnstile server-side token verification and Salesforce CRM relay — this function is deployed and runs independently from the static asset layer, remaining continuously live during any static rebuild triggered by Contentful content updates. Contentful webhooks trigger Cloudflare Pages build hooks on content publish, with p95 rebuild completing in 55 seconds for the current 42-page site. Build success triggers a confirmation webhook back to Contentful for deployment tracking. No PII is stored anywhere on Cloudflare infrastructure.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3, @astrojs/image for responsive srcset and WebP generation. CMS: Contentful with TypeScript SDK for typed content models. Bot Protection: Cloudflare Turnstile (managed challenge mode). Consent: Osano CMP for GDPR/CCPA. Analytics: Cloudflare Analytics (cookieless), GA4 (consent-gated). Form relay: Pages Function with Salesforce REST API. CI: GitHub Actions with @lhci/cli Lighthouse CI, wrangler pages deploy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Property Showcase Gallery: 3-column masonry grid on /properties with Contentful-sourced images, WebP/srcset optimization, lazy-load below fold; individual showcase pages at /properties/[slug] with high-resolution carousel and inquiry CTA. Agent Profiles: team grid on /agents, individual pages at /agents/[slug] with bio, credentials, active listing count, and direct inquiry form pre-filled with agent name. Neighborhood Guides: long-form content pages at /neighborhoods/[slug] with school ratings, transit scores, and market trend charts from static data. Inquiry Form: Turnstile-protected form on /contact and embedded on property/agent pages; fields: name, email, phone, inquiry_type (buy/sell/lease), budget_range, message; Salesforce relay on submit. GDPR/CCPA: Osano banner with granular consent toggles gating GA4 and any third-party embed scripts.
target_output.system_components: Cloudflare Pages CDN (static HTML, CSS, JS assets from Astro build; 300+ global PoPs), Astro Build Pipeline (Contentful content fetch at build time, @astrojs/image WebP conversion and srcset generation, 42 static pages), Contentful CMS (property_showcases, agent_profiles, neighborhood_guides, site_settings content models with field-level validation including alt text required on all media), Cloudflare Pages Function (/functions/api/inquiry.ts — independent of static rebuild; Turnstile verify, honeypot check, rate limit, Salesforce relay), Cloudflare Turnstile (managed challenge widget; server-side token verification endpoint challenges.cloudflare.com/turnstile/v0/siteverify), Osano CMP (cookie consent banner with GA4 gating and IAB TCF 2.2 compliance), Salesforce REST API (Cases object for inquiry ingestion), GitHub Actions CI (Astro build, Lighthouse CI mobile 90+ gate, wrangler deploy).
target_output.workflows: Buyer Inquiry: Prospect browses /properties/nob-hill-penthouse-collection, clicks 'Request Private Showing', inquiry form pre-fills property address; Turnstile managed challenge completes; on submit, Pages Function verifies Turnstile token via challenges.cloudflare.com, checks honeypot, enforces 3/10min IP rate limit, relays name/email/phone/message to Salesforce Cases API, returns 200; prospect receives email from Salesforce automation sequence. Content Update: Content editor publishes updated agent profile in Contentful; Contentful webhook fires to Cloudflare Pages build hook URL; Pages build pipeline fetches updated Contentful content, runs Astro build, deploys new static output in 55 seconds; Pages Function at /functions/api/inquiry.ts remains live throughout with no form submission interruption; build success triggers confirmation webhook back to Contentful marking deployment as confirmed.
target_output.data_model: No database on hosting infrastructure. Contentful content models: property_showcases (title, slug, headline_image, gallery_images[], bedrooms, bathrooms, sqft, neighborhood_ref, agent_ref, status ENUM active/sold/off_market, alt_text required). agent_profiles (name, slug, headshot, bio, license_number, specializations[], active_listing_count). neighborhood_guides (name, slug, hero_image, school_rating, transit_score, walkability_score, market_summary_text, featured_properties[]). Salesforce Case record created per inquiry: Subject (inquiry_type + property address), Description (message), Contact fields (name, email, phone), Custom fields: budget_range, source_page_url.
target_output.api_design: No public API surface. Cloudflare Pages Function endpoints: POST /api/inquiry (accepts multipart form: name, email, phone, inquiry_type, budget_range, message, turnstile_token, website[honeypot]; validates Turnstile token via POST to challenges.cloudflare.com/turnstile/v0/siteverify with SECRET_KEY from Pages environment variable; rejects if honeypot populated; rate-limits 3 req/10min per CF-Connecting-IP; POSTs to Salesforce REST API /services/data/v59.0/sobjects/Case; returns 200 JSON success or 429/400 on rejection). No other server endpoints. All CMS content fetched at build time only.
target_output.security_and_compliance: CSP via Cloudflare Pages _headers file: default-src 'self'; frame-src challenges.cloudflare.com; script-src 'self' challenges.cloudflare.com static.osano.com (no unsafe-inline); style-src 'self' 'nonce-{astro-nonce}'. Cloudflare Turnstile managed challenge on inquiry form with server-side token verification — turnstile secret key stored in Pages environment variable, never in client bundle. Honeypot field (name='website', display:none via CSS) rejected by Pages Function before Salesforce relay. WAF rate limit rule: 3 requests per IP per 10 minutes to /api/inquiry. GDPR: Osano CMP gating GA4 gtag.js load until explicit consent; cookie notice links to /privacy-policy; CCPA opt-out notice per California reqs. CAN-SPAM: downstream Salesforce email sequences handled by CRM, not this surface. No PII logged in Pages Function logs — only submission timestamp, Turnstile verification result, and HTTP status.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on push to main: bun install, astro build (with CONTENTFUL_ACCESS_TOKEN env var), @lhci/cli mobile Lighthouse run (performance >= 90, accessibility >= 95), wrangler pages deploy --project-name luxury-brokerage --branch main. PR branches trigger wrangler pages deploy --branch {branch} for preview URL. Contentful webhook: on entry.publish or entry.unpublish, POST to Cloudflare Pages deploy hook URL; Pages build completes in p95 55 seconds; on success, Cloudflare Pages webhook callback (configured in Pages settings) POSTs to Contentful webhook receiver marking deployment confirmed. Pages Functions deploy atomically with static assets but remain live during any pending rebuild. Rollback: Cloudflare Pages dashboard one-click rollback to any prior deployment; Pages Function version rolls back with static deployment atomically.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Analytics (cookieless, no consent required): unique visitors, top pages, geographic distribution, bot score distribution, WAF triggered events. GA4 (consent-gated): custom events inquiry_form_submit (with inquiry_type and source_page parameters), property_gallery_view (property_slug), agent_profile_view (agent_slug), neighborhood_guide_view. Lighthouse CI mobile scores tracked as GitHub Actions artifacts per deployment. Pages Function logs: submission count, Turnstile pass/fail rate, Salesforce relay success/error rate — no PII in logs. Alert: GitHub Actions notifies Slack channel on Lighthouse CI failure or wrangler deploy error.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages: free tier covers unlimited requests and 500 builds/month; Pages Functions: 100K free invocations/month, estimated 2K-5K inquiry submissions/month well within limit. Contentful: Community plan for content model with 25K API calls/month; rebuild calls are build-time only. Total estimated cost: $0-20/month for current traffic projections. Scaling: Cloudflare edge auto-scales; no capacity planning needed for static delivery. If inquiry volume exceeds 100K/month, upgrade to Pages $25/month plan.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — MVP (4 weeks): homepage with featured properties carousel (5 properties), 5 agent profile pages, /contact inquiry form with Turnstile and Salesforce relay, GDPR Osano cookie consent, GitHub Actions CI, Cloudflare Pages deployment, Lighthouse CI gate. Phase 2 — Full Content (3 weeks): complete property gallery (30+ properties), neighborhood guides for 6 key markets, agent grid page, embedded inquiry forms on property and agent pages, GA4 event tracking. Phase 3 — Enhancement (post-launch): virtual tour iframe embeds, agent scheduling widget integration, multi-language support for Mandarin and Spanish for international buyer markets.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode across all Astro components and Pages Functions. Contentful content models enforce alt text as required field — build fails if images lack alt text. @astrojs/image used for all property images — no raw img tags with external Contentful URLs allowed. Lighthouse CI must pass (mobile performance >= 90, accessibility >= 95) before merge. Pages Function unit-tested with Vitest for Turnstile verification logic, honeypot detection, and rate limit enforcement. No PII in any log statement — enforced by ESLint custom rule banning common PII field names in console calls.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful CMS outage prevents content updates but does not affect the live site (content is baked into static build). Mitigation: site remains fully functional from last successful build during any Contentful downtime. Risk: Turnstile managed challenge creates friction on mobile for legitimate prospects. Mitigation: managed challenge mode is typically invisible for human users; non-interactive challenge is the UX default. Tradeoff: Astro over Next.js for this use case — Astro's zero-JS-by-default approach is superior for gallery-heavy pages but limits future interactivity additions to explicit island components. Tradeoff: Salesforce relay over a standalone form backend — avoids database dependency but creates a hard dependency on Salesforce API availability; mitigated by async retry in Pages Function with 3 attempts before returning 503.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-002 resolved: Pages Functions are independent of static rebuilds and remain live during any Contentful-triggered build. 60-second rebuild SLA documented and monitored. ISS-001 resolved in round 2: CSP allows challenges.cloudflare.com frame-src for Turnstile cross-browser compatibility. Selected Osano over OneTrust CMP for GDPR consent due to Cloudflare Pages compatibility and free tier availability.

metadata.notes: Round 3 luxury brokerage landing page; resolves Contentful rebuild SLA and Pages Function continuity; all issues resolved.

project_goal.value: Automate utility bill ingestion, per-unit RUBS allocation, and resident billing for multifamily operators to eliminate manual spreadsheet processes
project_goal.rationale: Core cost-reduction objective replacing error-prone manual allocation workflows

target_users.value: Property managers, accounting staff, and residents of multifamily communities
target_users.rationale: Primary operators and bill recipients identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Requires manager UI, resident billing portal, and backend allocation engine

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, payments, batch_jobs, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Billing automation requires payment processing, nightly batch allocation, and PMS integration

complexity_level.rationale: Complex allocation logic but well-bounded domain with established RUBS formulas

risk_level.rationale: Handles financial billing data; no card storage; ACH via NACHA-compliant processor

data_sensitivity.rationale: Utility charges and ACH payment records constitute financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Resident portal and manager dashboard both require authentication

access_model.value: JWT RBAC with roles: property_manager, accounting_admin, resident; residents access only their unit charges
access_model.rationale: Residents must not access other units billing records

feature_scope.value: Utility bill upload via CSV and email parse, RUBS allocation engine, resident charge generation, Stripe ACH payment, Yardi/AppFolio PMS ledger sync, dispute resolution workflow
feature_scope.rationale: Full billing lifecycle from master meter to resident payment

mvp_scope.value: CSV bill upload, square-footage RUBS allocation, resident charge statements, Stripe ACH, dispute workflow, manager dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Manual CSV upload covers initial rollout; email parse is Phase 2

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 for stored payment references, RBAC at API layer, insert-only charge audit log
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires encryption and tamper-evident audit trail

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; aligns with existing operator tooling UI components

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Express
backend_stack.rationale: JavaScript end-to-end with team familiarity; adequate for allocation computation load

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity critical for financial allocation and payment records

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform
hosting_target.rationale: Cost-effective managed hosting within $400/month budget for SMB operator segment

privacy_retention_policy.value: 7-year retention for billing records per IRS and state utility regulations; ACH records retained per NACHA 2-year minimum
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial record retention obligations

future_scope.value: Email-based bill ingestion via SendGrid inbound parse, sub-meter IoT API integration, multi-property portfolio cost dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2-3 automation roadmap

constraints.value: Budget under $400/month; must integrate with Yardi and AppFolio via REST API; no on-premises deployment
constraints.rationale: SMB operators have limited infrastructure budget

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for error tracking, Grafana + Prometheus for API metrics, structured logging with correlation IDs per billing run
observability_baseline.rationale: Allocation errors must be traced to specific bill import and unit records

execution_preference.value: Kanban with 1-week iteration reviews
execution_preference.rationale: Small team continuous delivery model

compliance_context.value: RUBS state regulations vary; NACHA Operating Rules for ACH processing; PCI-DSS SAQ A for Stripe-hosted payment flows
compliance_context.rationale: Utility submetering and ACH processing carry state and federal regulatory obligations

project.goal: Automate utility billing allocation and resident payment collection for multifamily operators
project.target_users: Property managers, accounting staff, residents

open_questions.dispute_sla: How many days for manager to respond to a resident dispute?

reasoner_reviews.product: After 3 prior rounds the allocation engine and CSV upload are well-specified. The remaining gap is the dispute workflow — residents need a structured recourse path with state machine transitions and email notifications before the product can go live.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PostgreSQL schema with allocation_rules JSONB handles RUBS formula variants well. Stripe ACH via payment intents is the right pattern. PMS sync via outbound webhooks with Redis retry queue avoids polling Yardi/AppFolio APIs on every request cycle.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 3 audit flagged that charge_audit_log allowed UPDATE/DELETE at the application layer. NACHA and state billing regulations require an append-only audit log — this must be enforced via PostgreSQL trigger and role-level grants in round 4.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: DigitalOcean App Platform Basic tier handles nightly allocation batch load comfortably. Staying under $400/month is achievable with current resource sizing and managed database tier.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Dispute workflow was left as future scope in all prior rounds but is now a production blocker. Round 4 must include the complete state machine, manager resolution UI, and resident notification flow.

backend.service_design: Express app with domain modules: bills (CSV ingestion), allocations (RUBS engine), charges (resident billing), payments (Stripe ACH), disputes (state machine), pms_sync (Yardi/AppFolio webhook sender)
backend.api_patterns: REST endpoints at /api/v1; allocation run triggered via POST /api/v1/billing-runs; PMS inbound confirmations at /webhooks/pms/:provider
backend.background_jobs: node-cron job at 02:00 UTC scans utility_bills with status=pending, runs RUBS allocation, inserts resident_charges, notifies property manager; separate cron for PMS sync retry queue flush
backend.failure_handling: Allocation runs wrapped in database transactions; partial failures roll back entire billing run and insert error record to billing_run_errors table with reason

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting: /manager routes (bill upload, allocation runs, dispute queue) and /resident routes (charges, pay now, dispute form)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Manager: Bill Upload wizard, Allocation Run Preview, Charge Approval, Dispute Queue with resolution UI. Resident: My Charges, Pay Now via Stripe, Dispute a Charge, Payment History
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state (billing runs, charge lists, dispute status); Zustand for local UI state (selected property, active billing period)

security.auth_design: Custom JWT: 15-min access token, 7-day refresh in httpOnly cookie; resident tokens include unit_id claim scoped to their unit
security.authorization_model: RBAC middleware: property_manager gates billing run triggers and charge approval; accounting_admin gates allocation rule edits; resident restricted to own unit charges and disputes
security.audit_and_logging_controls: charge_audit_log table is INSERT-ONLY: PostgreSQL BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raises exception; DB role for application has no UPDATE/DELETE grants on this table

data.entities: utility_bills, allocation_rules, units, residents, resident_charges, payment_records, disputes, charge_audit_log
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Database; charge_audit_log partitioned by month; Redis for PMS retry job queue
data.schema_notes: All money values as NUMERIC(10,2); allocation_rules config stored as JSONB with method and formula parameters; disputes table has status ENUM open/under_review/resolved
data.retention_and_deletion: Billing records retained 7 years; ACH payment records 2 years per NACHA; monthly charge_audit_log partition archive to DO Spaces after 24 months

devops.deployment_topology: DigitalOcean App Platform: web service (2 Basic instances, autoscale to 4), worker service (1 Basic instance), managed PostgreSQL 16, managed Redis 7
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ESLint + TypeScript check + Jest unit tests on PR; integration tests against ephemeral PG on merge; deploy to staging on merge to main; production deploy on git tag
devops.rollback_strategy: App Platform instant rollback to previous deploy; node-pg-migrate down scripts tested in CI; DB migration runs as release command before app start

fix_report.action_taken: Designed full dispute workflow: disputes table with state machine (open->under_review->resolved), manager dispute queue in /manager/disputes, resident dispute submission form, SendGrid email notifications to resident on each state transition, dispute_audit_log for resolution notes and actor tracking.

fix_report.expected_outcome: charge_audit_log is tamper-evident and compliant with NACHA append-only audit requirements; ISS-002 resolved

target_output.title: Utility Expense Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack React 18 and Node.js 20 Express application on DigitalOcean App Platform that automates the complete utility billing lifecycle for multifamily operators: CSV bill ingestion, RUBS allocation engine distributing master meter costs across units by square footage or occupant count, resident charge generation with Stripe ACH payment collection, a dispute resolution workflow for contested charges, tamper-evident charge audit logging enforced via PostgreSQL trigger, and bidirectional ledger sync with Yardi and AppFolio property management systems.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served from DigitalOcean App Platform static hosting communicates over TLS 1.3 with a Node.js 20 Express REST API on App Platform web service. A separate worker service executes node-cron jobs for nightly RUBS allocation at 02:00 UTC, charge statement dispatch via SendGrid, and PMS ledger sync with Redis-backed exponential-backoff retry queue. PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Database persists all billing, allocation, charge, payment, and dispute records. charge_audit_log is protected by a PostgreSQL trigger preventing any UPDATE or DELETE. Stripe handles ACH payment intents with no bank data stored on our servers. Sentry captures application errors; Grafana and Prometheus expose allocation run duration and payment failure rate metrics per property.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, React Hook Form. Backend: Node.js 20, Express, Zod validation, node-pg-migrate, node-cron, Stripe Node SDK v14, @sendgrid/mail. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Database. Queue: Redis 7 on DigitalOcean Managed Redis for PMS retry jobs. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Error tracking: Sentry. Metrics: Prometheus + Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Utility Bill Ingestion: CSV upload with drag-and-drop column mapping wizard supporting electric, gas, water, and trash bill types per property per billing period. RUBS Allocation Engine: configurable per-property formulas — square footage ratio or occupant count ratio — applied to master meter totals generating per-unit resident_charges. Charge Approval Workflow: manager previews allocation breakdown before posting charges to residents. Resident Billing Portal: itemized charge statements showing utility type, billing period, and allocated amount; one-click Stripe ACH payment; payment history. Dispute Resolution: resident submits dispute with free-text reason; manager triages open disputes in a queue, sets status to under_review, adds resolution note, resolves; resident receives SendGrid email at each state transition. PMS Ledger Sync: confirmed ACH payments pushed to Yardi or AppFolio ledger via REST webhook with Redis retry queue on failure.
target_output.system_components: DigitalOcean App Platform web service (Node.js Express API, 2 Basic instances autoscaling to 4 on CPU >70%). Worker service (node-cron allocation and sync jobs, 1 Basic instance). Managed PostgreSQL 16 (1 vCPU, 2 GB RAM, daily automated backups). Managed Redis 7 (1 GB, PMS retry queue and allocation job deduplication lock). Stripe for ACH payment intents and webhook event delivery. SendGrid for resident charge statements, dispute notifications, and payment receipts. GitHub Actions for CI/CD pipeline. Sentry for exception tracking. Prometheus + Grafana for operational dashboards.
