previous_plan.title: PropTech ML Appreciation Predictor Landing Page v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 SSG landing page on AWS S3 + CloudFront for a PropTech ML platform targeting institutional investors. Demo request form with hCaptcha (server-side validation in Lambda) integrates with HubSpot CRM. Plausible cookieless analytics.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 static export deployed to S3 + CloudFront. Lambda handles demo form POST with hCaptcha validation and HubSpot API call. Signal explainer sections use static image assets. Model accuracy statistics hardcoded in page HTML.
previous_plan.workflows: Visitor views signal explainer images. Fills demo request form. hCaptcha validated in Lambda. HubSpot contact and deal created. Plausible records demo_form_submit_success event.
previous_plan.data_model: No database. Static content in JSX. Signal widgets: static images. Model accuracy: hardcoded strings in component.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 closes two credibility issues: replacing static image signal explainers with data-driven pre-rendered SVG charts from JSON data files, and implementing an automated weekly model accuracy refresh via ISR + GitHub Actions. The core architecture from prior rounds is solid — Next.js SSG on S3 + CloudFront, Lambda for form handling, hCaptcha server-side validation, HubSpot CRM. Round 4 is additive: specify the JSON data file pattern for signal widgets and the ISR + weekly workflow pattern for accuracy stats.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced static image signal explainers with pre-rendered SVG charts: each of the three signal types (gentrification index, infrastructure investment pipeline, migration net flow) now has a JSON data file in /data/signals/. Next.js getStaticProps reads these files at build time and passes data to SignalExplainerChart components that render lightweight SVG line/bar charts using Recharts in server-side rendering mode — zero client JS added.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Signal explainer sections show actual platform data trends as interactive-looking SVG charts, significantly improving credibility with institutional investors; ISS-001 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented two-track model accuracy refresh: ISR with revalidate: 604800 (7 days) on the ModelAccuracyStats block so Next.js automatically revalidates from the updated stats JSON; a weekly GitHub Actions cron workflow fetches updated model_accuracy.json from the internal ML metrics API, commits it to the repo (triggering a new build), and calls CloudFront invalidation on /. Accuracy statistics are now always within 7 days of the latest model evaluation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model accuracy statistics reflect the most recent weekly model evaluation without manual HTML edits; ISS-002 resolved

target_output.title: PropTech ML Appreciation Predictor Platform Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page for an institutional investor-targeted PropTech ML platform, deployed to AWS S3 and CloudFront. Showcases the platform's three predictive signal categories — neighborhood gentrification index, infrastructure investment pipeline, and net migration flow — through pre-rendered SVG charts driven by JSON data files. Model accuracy statistics (MAPE by market tier, 12-month backtests) refresh automatically each week via ISR and a GitHub Actions cron workflow. A demo request form protected by hCaptcha feeds qualified institutional leads into HubSpot CRM via a Lambda function. Plausible cookieless analytics tracks the full institutional conversion funnel.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 SSG site exported to S3 and served via CloudFront global CDN. Three signal explainer sections render pre-built SVG charts from JSON data files (gentrification_signals.json, infrastructure_pipeline.json, migration_flows.json) via getStaticProps at build time — no client-side chart rendering. ModelAccuracyStats component uses getStaticProps with revalidate: 604800 reading model_accuracy.json; a GitHub Actions weekly cron fetches updated JSON from the internal ML metrics endpoint, commits to repo, and triggers CloudFront invalidation. Demo request form submits to AWS API Gateway + Lambda TypeScript function which validates hCaptcha token server-side via hcaptcha.com/siteverify, validates form fields, and calls HubSpot Contacts API to create a contact and Sales pipeline deal. CloudFront response headers policy enforces CSP, HSTS, and X-Frame-Options.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Recharts (server-side SVG rendering only, no client hydration on chart components). Serverless: AWS Lambda TypeScript, deployed via AWS SAM, triggered by API Gateway. Analytics: Plausible cookieless analytics. Bot protection: hCaptcha. CRM: HubSpot Marketing API. Hosting: S3 + CloudFront. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: AWS CloudWatch for Lambda. Weekly data refresh: GitHub Actions cron.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: institutional investor value proposition — 'Identify appreciation markets 18-24 months ahead of price movement' — with primary CTA button anchoring to demo form. ML Signal Explainer Widgets (3 interactive-looking SVG charts): Gentrification Index showing 5-year trend of arts permits, co-working space density, and restaurant opening rate for a sample market; Infrastructure Investment Pipeline showing committed public transit and road investment spending per metro over a 10-year horizon; Net Migration Flow chart showing in/out migration rates by demographic cohort for top 20 growth markets. Model Track Record Block (ISR, weekly refresh): MAPE by market tier (tier-1: 4.2%, tier-2: 6.1%, tier-3: 8.8%), 12-month backtest alpha versus NCREIF index, model version and last evaluation date. Case Study Cards: two institutional investor case studies showing portfolio construction decisions informed by early appreciation signals and realized returns. Demo Request Form: firm name, contact name, email, AUM range dropdown, primary use case, hCaptcha, GDPR consent checkbox. Media Coverage Strip: logos and pull quotes from financial press coverage.
target_output.system_components: AWS CloudFront distribution (global CDN, response headers policy for CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options). S3 buckets (staging and production for static Next.js export). AWS API Gateway (POST /demo-request endpoint). AWS Lambda TypeScript function (hCaptcha validation, HubSpot API call). hCaptcha (bot protection for demo form). HubSpot CRM (demo lead pipeline). JSON data files in repo (gentrification_signals.json, infrastructure_pipeline.json, migration_flows.json, model_accuracy.json). GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. GitHub Actions weekly cron for model accuracy refresh. Plausible analytics script (cookieless). AWS CloudWatch for Lambda monitoring.
target_output.workflows: Signal Widget Rendering: At Next.js build time, getStaticProps in index.tsx reads gentrification_signals.json, infrastructure_pipeline.json, and migration_flows.json from /data/signals/. Data passed as props to SignalExplainerChart components. Recharts renders SVG bar and line charts during server-side rendering; SVG output inlined in the static HTML. No JavaScript shipped to browser for chart rendering — charts appear interactive but are static SVG. Model Accuracy Refresh: Weekly GitHub Actions cron job (Mondays 06:00 UTC): fetch GET https://metrics.internal/model-accuracy/latest and validate response schema. Commit updated model_accuracy.json to main branch. Next.js ISR revalidates the ModelAccuracyStats block on next page request after commit (within the 7-day revalidation window). Additionally triggers CloudFront invalidation on / to force immediate refresh of the production CDN cache. Demo Request Flow: Visitor fills form with firm name, contact, email, AUM range, use case, and hCaptcha challenge. POST to /demo-request API Gateway endpoint. Lambda function: (1) calls hCaptcha siteverify with token and HCAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY; (2) validates email format and required fields via regex; (3) calls HubSpot POST /contacts with firstname, lastname, email, company; (4) calls HubSpot POST /deals with deal_name='{firm_name} - Demo Request', pipeline='Sales', stage='Demo Requested', associated contact. Returns {success: true, reference_id: hubspot_deal_id}. Plausible records demo_form_submit_success custom event.
target_output.data_model: No database or persistent storage on landing page infrastructure. Static JSON content assets (committed to repo): gentrification_signals.json (array of {market, year, arts_permits_index, coworking_density, restaurant_openings_qoq}), infrastructure_pipeline.json (array of {metro, committed_spend_bn, project_type, completion_year}), migration_flows.json (array of {market, in_migration_rate, out_migration_rate, net_per_1000, year}), model_accuracy.json ({mape_tier1: float, mape_tier2: float, mape_tier3: float, backtest_alpha_vs_ncreif: float, model_version: string, evaluated_at: date}). Case study content: case_studies.json (array of {firm_type, headline, signal_used, realized_return, quote}). All JSON files have TypeScript type definitions in types/content.ts.
target_output.api_design: Single serverless endpoint: POST /demo-request. Request body: {firm_name: string, contact_name: string, email: string, aum_range: string, use_case: string, hcaptcha_token: string, gdpr_consent: boolean}. Response 200: {success: true, reference_id: string} (HubSpot deal ID). Response 200 with flag: {already_registered: true} if HubSpot returns existing contact. Response 400: {error: 'invalid_captcha' | 'invalid_email' | 'missing_required_fields'}. Response 500: {error: 'server_error'} on HubSpot API failure. No other endpoints — all other content is statically generated.
target_output.security_and_compliance: hCaptcha: demo form includes hCaptcha challenge; Lambda validates token via POST https://hcaptcha.com/siteverify with HCAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY environment variable before any HubSpot API call — client-side token alone not trusted. AWS WAF on CloudFront: rate limit rule blocks more than 100 POST requests per IP per 5 minutes to /demo-request path. CSP via CloudFront response headers policy: script-src 'self' hcaptcha.com assets.hcaptcha.com newassets.hcaptcha.com plausible.io; frame-src hcaptcha.com; HSTS max-age=31536000; X-Frame-Options: DENY. Lambda secrets: HCAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY and HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN stored as AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store SecureString values, referenced via Lambda environment variables — never in source code or logs. GDPR: consent checkbox on form; gdpr_consent field sent to HubSpot legal_basis property. Privacy policy linked from form and footer. Plausible analytics: cookieless, no GDPR consent banner required.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on pull request: next build, next export, HTML validation, Lighthouse CI (fail if Core Web Vitals LCP > 2.5s or CLS > 0.1 on mobile). On merge to main: aws s3 sync out/ to staging S3 bucket; CloudFront invalidation on staging distribution. Production deploy on semver git tag: aws s3 sync to production S3 bucket; CloudFront invalidation on /. Lambda deploys independently: aws sam build + aws sam deploy on Lambda code changes. Weekly model accuracy cron: separate GitHub Actions workflow (schedule: cron('0 6 * * 1')) fetches model_accuracy.json from internal metrics API, commits update, triggers main deploy workflow which includes CloudFront invalidation. Lambda alias strategy: production alias points to latest published version; rollback by updating alias to previous version via aws lambda update-alias.
target_output.observability: Plausible custom events for full funnel: hero_cta_click (tracks clicks on hero CTA button), signal_explainer_view (IntersectionObserver fires when each chart enters viewport), case_study_expand, demo_form_view (form enters viewport), demo_form_submit_success, demo_form_submit_error (with error type). AWS CloudWatch Lambda metrics: invocation count, error rate, duration p95 — alert to Slack if demo form error rate exceeds 5% over 1 hour. CloudFront access logs to S3 for raw traffic analysis. Weekly model accuracy cron logs fetch success/failure to CloudWatch; Slack alert on fetch failure so data team is notified before the JSON becomes stale.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: S3 static hosting: ~$1/month for storage + requests at typical landing page traffic. CloudFront: free tier covers 1TB/month data transfer. Lambda: free tier covers 1M requests/month — demo form submissions well within free tier. API Gateway: $3.50 per million API calls. Plausible: $9/month for analytics. HubSpot: existing CRM subscription cost, no incremental per-lead charge. Total incremental infrastructure: ~$10-15/month. Scaling: CloudFront handles global traffic spikes without configuration changes; Lambda scales automatically.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (3 weeks): Hero with investor-focused value proposition, three signal explainer SVG charts from JSON data files, model accuracy statistics block with ISR, one case study card, demo request form with hCaptcha and HubSpot CRM integration, Plausible analytics, Core Web Vitals all green, CloudFront CSP/HSTS headers. Phase 2 Post-launch (4 weeks): Second and third case study cards, media coverage strip with financial press logos, interactive market map visualization showing appreciation probability heatmap as a static SVG generated at build time, gated PDF report download requiring demo request completion. Phase 3 (ongoing): Weekly model accuracy refresh automation tuned, A/B test on CTA copy and form length, investor portal login for registered demo attendees.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout. All content JSON files have TypeScript type definitions in types/content.ts — no untyped JSON imports. Signal explainer chart components use Recharts in server rendering mode with no 'use client' directive — charts are SSR-only SVG. Any update to signal data JSON files triggers a new Next.js build automatically via GitHub Actions on push to main. Lighthouse CI gate in PR pipeline: LCP <= 2.5s, FID <= 100ms, CLS <= 0.1 on mobile simulation — fail CI if violated. No third-party scripts added without performance impact review. Lambda function has 10-second timeout; hCaptcha siteverify call must complete within 3 seconds or Lambda returns a user-friendly error.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: internal ML metrics API unavailable during weekly model accuracy cron — stale JSON remains in repo and site shows last week's numbers, which is acceptable given weekly update cadence. Mitigation: CloudWatch alert on cron fetch failure notifies data team. Risk: hCaptcha challenge rate too high for high-intent institutional investors, reducing form completion. Mitigation: hCaptcha Enterprise mode configured for lower challenge rate for non-bot traffic; can switch to invisible challenge mode. Risk: Recharts SVG output size for signal explainer charts increases HTML payload. Mitigation: cap each chart at 200 data points; SVG output should remain under 5KB per chart. Tradeoff: pre-rendered SVG charts over client-side rendered — loses interactivity (tooltips, zoom) but gains Core Web Vitals score and eliminates JS bundle size.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Signal data sources for explainer charts: use real platform data export as JSON files committed to repo — research team provides a sanitized sample dataset for each signal type. Model accuracy statistics source: internal ML metrics API endpoint returns JSON matching model_accuracy.json schema after each weekly retraining cycle. AUM range options on demo form: <$100M, $100M-$500M, $500M-$2B, >$2B.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision upgrading static image signal widgets to data-driven pre-rendered SVGs and implementing automated weekly model accuracy refresh via ISR and GitHub Actions cron.

profile.domain: Offline-First Property Field Inspection Mobile App

project_goal.value: Performance-optimized field inspection mobile app for property inspectors, enabling offline-first condition assessments with photo capture, voice-to-text observations, and defect tagging — syncing to a central platform when connectivity is restored.
project_goal.rationale: Inspectors operate in properties with unreliable connectivity; offline-first is non-negotiable for adoption.

target_users.value: Licensed property inspectors conducting pre-purchase, pre-lease, and insurance condition assessments. Field supervisors reviewing completed reports on desktop.
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in field research with 3 inspection companies.

project_class.rationale: Native-capable mobile app with offline-first SQLite local store; React Native for cross-platform iOS and Android coverage.

capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend, server sync API, local and server databases, JWT auth, and PDF report generation integration.

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution and photo batch upload adds meaningful complexity over a standard CRUD app.

risk_level.rationale: Internal operational data; no PHI, financial data, or external partner access.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Inspection data is internal operational records; commercially sensitive but not regulated.

external_exposure.rationale: App requires JWT login; sync API is not publicly accessible.

access_model.value: Custom JWT with inspector and supervisor roles. Mobile app stores JWT in secure keychain. Sync API validates JWT on every request.
access_model.rationale: Standard mobile JWT auth; no SSO required for inspector field users.

feature_scope.value: Offline condition assessment with inspection_items checklist, photo capture, voice-to-text observations, defect tagging on property diagram, sync queue with conflict resolution, server-side PDF report generation, supervisor review dashboard.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for end-to-end inspection workflow from field to report.

mvp_scope.value: Offline inspection with checklist, photo capture, sync to server, basic PDF report generation. iOS and Android via React Native.
mvp_scope.rationale: Core offline-to-sync workflow for pilot with 10 inspectors.

security_baseline.value: JWT auth with 8-hour expiry for field sessions, HTTPS on all sync API calls, SQLite database encrypted at rest on device via SQLCipher, S3 SSE for photo storage.
security_baseline.rationale: Device encryption and transport security for field devices that may be lost or stolen.

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript; WatermelonDB for offline-first SQLite sync; Expo (managed workflow) for build and OTA updates.
frontend_stack.rationale: WatermelonDB provides lazy-loaded SQLite with built-in sync primitives matching the offline-first requirement.

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Fastify for sync API; background worker for PDF report generation.
backend_stack.rationale: Node/Fastify matches team expertise; Fastify's schema-based serialization is performant for batch sync payloads.

data_platform.value: SQLite (WatermelonDB on-device) for local store; PostgreSQL 16 (RDS) for server-side canonical store; S3 for photo and voice note storage.
data_platform.rationale: SQLite for offline; PostgreSQL for server sync target and report generation queries.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate (Node.js sync API and PDF worker); RDS PostgreSQL in private VPC.
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers; RDS for operational simplicity.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Inspection records retained 7 years. Photos retained for 7 years per inspection record lifecycle. On-device SQLite data purged after successful sync confirmation.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Property inspection records may be subpoenaed in real estate disputes; 7-year retention standard.

future_scope.value: AI defect recognition from photos, integration with home inspection report platforms (HomeGauge, Home Inspector Pro), multi-inspector parallel inspection mode.
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap for AI-augmented inspections and platform interoperability.

constraints.value: App must function fully offline for minimum 8 hours without any network access. Photo batch uploads must not block inspection workflows. OTA update mechanism required — no mandatory app store update cycle for field teams.
constraints.rationale: Field operational constraints from inspector feedback.

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for React Native crash reporting, structured logs on Fastify sync API, custom metrics for sync success rate, photo upload queue depth, and PDF generation latency.
observability_baseline.rationale: Sync reliability is critical for inspector productivity; failure visibility required.

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with Bitrise CI for React Native builds; automated TestFlight and Play Console beta distribution.
execution_preference.rationale: Fast mobile release cycle for field pilot feedback.

project.goal: Offline-first field inspection app syncing to central platform when connectivity restored
project.target_users: Property inspectors (field), supervisors (desktop review)
project.timeline: Q2 for iOS/Android MVP pilot with 10 inspectors

frontend.build: Expo managed workflow with EAS Build

security.encryption: SQLCipher on-device, S3 SSE for photos, HTTPS for sync

data.photos: S3 SSE with presigned upload URLs

devops.ota: Expo OTA updates for JS bundle patches

reasoner_reviews.product: Three rounds have progressively sharpened the offline sync design. The last remaining audit concern was the conflict resolution strategy for concurrent edits to the same inspection_item from multiple devices. Round 3 should specify the last-write-wins per inspection_item semantics explicitly and document the edge case where two inspectors tag the same defect simultaneously.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WatermelonDB's sync protocol (pull/push with changed-since timestamps) is correct for this use case. The round 2 plan added server-side sync endpoint detail but left conflict resolution semantics implicit. Explicitly specifying last-write-wins by updated_at timestamp per inspection_item row eliminates ambiguity. Photo upload queue in a background React Native task (react-native-background-fetch) ensures photo uploads do not block checklist workflow.
reasoner_reviews.security: SQLCipher on-device database encryption is correctly specified. JWT storage in iOS Keychain and Android Keystore is correct. The 8-hour JWT expiry is appropriate for field sessions. No security gaps identified in round 2.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Offline-8-hour constraint is met by WatermelonDB SQLite + local photo queue in MMKV. Photo background upload via react-native-background-fetch. Expo OTA satisfies no-forced-app-store-update constraint. All constraints are addressed.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 conflict resolution was described as 'last-write-wins' but did not specify what the write timestamp is (client device clock vs server receipt time) — device clocks can drift or be manipulated. Round 3 must clarify: server-assigned updated_at timestamp is authoritative for conflict resolution, not client device clock.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with Expo managed workflow; WatermelonDB Models for Inspection, InspectionItem, Defect, Photo, VoiceNote; NavigationContainer with native stack navigators for offline-capable screens
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home (assigned inspections list), InspectionDetail (checklist with offline status indicator), ItemAssessment (condition select, voice-to-text, photo capture), DefectTagging (property diagram with defect pin overlay), SyncStatus (queue depth, last sync time, manual sync trigger), Login
frontend.performance_notes: WatermelonDB lazy-loading ensures checklist renders even with 500+ inspection items; photo thumbnails generated on-device via react-native-image-resizer before display; background photo upload via react-native-background-fetch to not block UI thread
frontend.frontend_security_notes: JWT stored in iOS Keychain via react-native-keychain; SQLCipher key derived from device biometric authentication fallback to PIN; no inspection data in AsyncStorage (unencrypted)

backend.service_design: Fastify Node.js 20 API with route modules: sync (pull/push endpoints), photos (presigned S3 upload URL generation), reports (PDF job queue trigger), auth (JWT issue and refresh)
backend.api_patterns: WatermelonDB-compatible sync API: GET /api/v1/sync/pull?last_pulled_at= returns changed records since timestamp; POST /api/v1/sync/push accepts changed and deleted record arrays; server assigns updated_at on all upserts
backend.background_jobs: PDF generation worker: ECS Fargate task polling SQS queue for report_generation_requested jobs; uses Puppeteer to render HTML report template to PDF; uploads to S3; updates report status in PostgreSQL
backend.failure_handling: Photo upload presigned URL expiry 15 minutes; client retries with fresh presigned URL on expiry. Sync push failures return 409 Conflict with server-canonical record for client-side merge. PDF worker SQS dead-letter after 3 retries.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT issued by /api/v1/auth/login; 8-hour expiry for field sessions; refresh token 30 days in HttpOnly cookie for web supervisor dashboard. Mobile stores access JWT in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore
security.authorization_model: inspector role: can read/write own assigned inspections only. supervisor role: read all inspections, trigger report generation, manage inspector assignments
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager for DB password and S3 KMS key ARN; ECS task environment variables sourced from Secrets Manager at startup

data.entities: inspections (id, property_id, inspector_id, inspection_type ENUM pre_purchase/pre_lease/insurance, status, scheduled_date, synced_at, created_at), inspection_items (id, inspection_id FK, category ENUM structural/electrical/plumbing/hvac/exterior, description, condition ENUM pass/fail/needs_attention, notes, updated_at, created_at), defects (id, inspection_item_id FK, x_pct FLOAT, y_pct FLOAT on diagram, severity ENUM minor/moderate/major, description, resolved BOOLEAN, created_at), photos (id, inspection_item_id FK, s3_key, thumbnail_s3_key, caption, captured_at, synced BOOLEAN), voice_notes (id, inspection_item_id FK, s3_key, transcript TEXT, duration_seconds, synced BOOLEAN), sync_queues (id, device_id, entity_type, entity_id, operation ENUM create/update/delete, queued_at, synced_at NULLABLE)
data.storage_design: On-device: WatermelonDB SQLite (SQLCipher encrypted) with local tables mirroring server schema. Server: RDS PostgreSQL 16. Photos and voice notes: S3 bucket with SSE-KMS; presigned POST URLs for direct device-to-S3 upload bypassing sync API.
data.retention_and_deletion: Server PostgreSQL records: 7-year retention. S3 photos/voice notes: lifecycle rule retains 7 years aligned with inspection record. On-device SQLite: purged post sync confirmation to manage device storage — keeps last 30 days of synced inspections for offline reference.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: sync-api task (2-4 tasks, auto-scale on CPU 60%), pdf-worker task (1-3 tasks, SQS-driven). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. S3 bucket for photos and reports. React Native: Bitrise CI for iOS (Xcode cloud) and Android (Gradle) builds; Expo EAS for OTA JS bundle updates
devops.ci_cd_design: Bitrise workflow: npm install, jest unit tests (80% coverage), Expo prebuild, iOS and Android builds, TestFlight and Play Console beta distribution. Server GitHub Actions: npm test, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with health checks; automatic rollback on task failure. Expo OTA: rollback via expo publish --rollback to prior bundle hash. App store builds: previous version remains in TestFlight/Play Console for re-distribution if needed

ISS-001.title: Conflict resolution timestamp authority undefined — device clock vs server receipt time
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 described last-write-wins conflict resolution but did not specify whether the winning write is determined by client device clock (updated_at from device) or server receipt timestamp. Device clocks can drift up to 5 minutes in field conditions, causing incorrect merge outcomes for concurrent edits.

history.note: Raised by critic in round 2 audit after last-write-wins strategy was partially described.

ISS-002.title: Background photo upload did not specify platform-specific background fetch mechanism
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 mentioned background photo upload but did not specify how the React Native app maintains background upload on iOS (which aggressively kills background tasks). Round 2 addressed this with react-native-background-fetch and BGTaskScheduler.

history.note: iOS background task lifecycle unaddressed.
react-native-background-fetch with BGTaskScheduler registered on app launch documented in round 2.

focus_issues.title: Conflict resolution timestamp authority undefined — device clock vs server receipt time
focus_issues.detail: Round 2 last-write-wins strategy ambiguous: device clock vs server-assigned timestamp for conflict resolution authority.

latest_recommendations: Specify that server-assigned updated_at timestamp is the authoritative conflict resolution key — client device clock is recorded as device_updated_at for debugging but sync API overwrites updated_at with server receipt time on all pushed records.

previous_audits.summary: ISS-002 resolved with react-native-background-fetch. ISS-001 partially addressed with last-write-wins mention but timestamp authority (device clock vs server) remains ambiguous.
strengths: react-native-background-fetch + BGTaskScheduler correctly documented
WatermelonDB pull/push sync endpoints well-specified
Presigned S3 upload URL pattern is performant and correct
