previous_plan.title: Property Field Inspection Mobile App — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Offline-first React Native app with WatermelonDB SQLite, background photo upload via react-native-background-fetch, Fastify sync API on ECS Fargate, and server-side PDF generation.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React Native with WatermelonDB offline SQLite store; Fastify pull/push sync API; S3 for photo storage; SQLCipher device encryption; last-write-wins conflict resolution.
previous_plan.workflows: Inspector works offline; photo upload via background fetch; sync triggered on connectivity restore using WatermelonDB push/pull. Conflict resolution: last-write-wins.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Bitrise CI for React Native builds; ECS Fargate for sync API; Expo OTA for JS bundle updates. react-native-background-fetch with BGTaskScheduler for iOS background uploads.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is round 3 of the offline-first property inspection app. ISS-002 (iOS background upload) was resolved in round 2. ISS-001 is the final focus: the conflict resolution strategy specifies last-write-wins but the timestamp authority is ambiguous. I need to explicitly specify that the server assigns updated_at on all pushed records at sync receipt time, overriding device clock. The client records device_updated_at for debugging. This eliminates clock drift as a source of incorrect conflict resolution outcomes.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified in workflows and data_model that the sync API sets updated_at = NOW() (server-side PostgreSQL CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) on all INSERT and UPDATE operations in the /api/v1/sync/push handler. Client device timestamp is stored as device_updated_at (separate column) for debugging and audit. WatermelonDB conflict resolution on pull uses the server-assigned updated_at as the canonical comparison timestamp. Documented this in both the data_model (device_updated_at column on inspection_items and defects) and the workflows section (sync conflict resolution flow).
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved: server-assigned updated_at is unambiguously the conflict resolution authority; device clock drift cannot cause incorrect merge outcomes.

target_output.title: Property Field Inspection Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native application for licensed property inspectors enabling full condition assessments — checklist completion, photo capture, voice-to-text observations, and defect tagging on property diagrams — without network connectivity. WatermelonDB provides an encrypted SQLite local store (via SQLCipher) with a pull/push sync protocol synchronized against a Fastify Node.js API on ECS Fargate and PostgreSQL 16. Background photo uploads via react-native-background-fetch bypass the sync queue for large binary assets. Server-assigned timestamps are authoritative for last-write-wins conflict resolution, eliminating device clock drift issues. Expo OTA enables field-deployable JS bundle updates without mandatory app store release cycles.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native app (iOS and Android) built with Expo managed workflow; WatermelonDB Models synchronized via a custom pull/push sync adapter against the Fastify server. On device: WatermelonDB SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher using a key derived from device biometric or PIN. Photo and voice note binaries bypass the sync endpoint and upload directly to S3 via presigned POST URLs requested from the Fastify API — keeping binary blobs out of the sync protocol payload. Fastify sync API on ECS Fargate: POST /api/v1/sync/push receives changed and deleted record arrays, sets updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP server-side on all upserted rows (overriding any client clock), and returns 200 with server-canonical records. GET /api/v1/sync/pull?last_pulled_at= returns all records updated after the given server timestamp. Conflict resolution: last-write-wins by server-assigned updated_at — the record with the higher server timestamp wins on merge. PDF report generation runs in a separate ECS Fargate worker task consuming an SQS queue, rendering an HTML template to PDF via Puppeteer, and uploading the result to S3. Sentry collects React Native crash reports and Fastify API errors.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.73 with TypeScript, Expo SDK 50 (managed workflow), WatermelonDB 0.27 (SQLite via react-native-quick-sqlite, SQLCipher), react-native-background-fetch 4.x (photo upload daemon), react-native-camera (photo capture), @react-native-voice/voice (voice-to-text), react-native-keychain (JWT in iOS Keychain/Android Keystore). Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify 4, pg (PostgreSQL client), @aws-sdk/client-s3. PDF Worker: Puppeteer 22 on Node.js 20. CI: Bitrise (React Native builds, EAS Build). Server CI: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Sentry React Native SDK, Sentry Node SDK, CloudWatch.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Offline Inspection Workflow: inspector opens assigned inspection, works through checklist of inspection_items organized by category (structural, electrical, plumbing, hvac, exterior), marks each as pass/fail/needs_attention, adds voice-to-text notes and photos per item — all persisted to local WatermelonDB SQLite. Defect Tagging: inspector pins defects on a property floor plan image with x/y percentages, assigns severity (minor/moderate/major), and adds description — stored locally. Background Photo Upload: react-native-background-fetch daemon checks photo sync queue every 5 minutes, requests presigned S3 POST URLs for unsynced photos, uploads directly to S3, marks photos as synced in local DB. Sync: inspector taps 'Sync' or connectivity-restore triggers automatic pull/push sync; WatermelonDB push sends changed inspection_items, defects, and voice notes; server assigns authoritative updated_at; pull fetches server-updated records. PDF Report: supervisor requests report generation from web dashboard; SQS job triggers PDF worker; PDF uploaded to S3; supervisor notified with download link.
target_output.system_components: React Native App (iOS 15+ and Android 10+; WatermelonDB local SQLite; Expo OTA updates), Fastify Sync API (ECS Fargate, 2-4 tasks, auto-scale CPU 60%; /sync/pull, /sync/push, /photos/presign, /auth, /reports endpoints), PDF Worker (ECS Fargate 1-3 tasks; Puppeteer rendering; SQS-driven; S3 upload), RDS PostgreSQL 16 (inspections, inspection_items, defects, photos, voice_notes, sync_queues, users tables; Multi-AZ; private VPC), S3 propco-inspections bucket (SSE-KMS; /photos/ and /voice-notes/ and /reports/ prefixes; 7-year lifecycle retention), SQS report_generation_requested queue (with DLQ after 3 retries), Sentry (React Native crash reporting, Fastify error tracking), Bitrise CI (iOS Xcode build + TestFlight distribution, Android Gradle + Play Console beta), EAS Build (Expo managed builds and OTA bundle publishing).
target_output.workflows: Offline Assessment: Inspector authenticates (JWT cached in Keychain for 8-hour session), opens assigned inspection from local WatermelonDB cache, completes checklist items offline — each item save writes to local SQLite with device_updated_at = Date.now() and marks record dirty for sync queue. Background Photo Upload: react-native-background-fetch triggers every 5 minutes (BGTaskScheduler on iOS, WorkManager on Android); fetches photos where synced=false from local DB; requests presigned S3 POST URL from Fastify /photos/presign; uploads photo bytes directly to S3; marks photo synced=true in local DB. Sync Conflict Resolution: On connectivity restore (NetInfo event) or manual sync tap, WatermelonDB push adapter POSTs changed_records array to /api/v1/sync/push. Fastify handler processes each record: for each inspection_item or defect UPSERT, sets updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP (server clock), stores client timestamp in device_updated_at column. On conflict (same id pushed from two devices), the record with the higher server-assigned updated_at wins — enforced by PostgreSQL upsert ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE excluded.updated_at > inspection_items.updated_at. Pull response includes all records updated since client last_pulled_at using server updated_at. WatermelonDB applies server records, replacing any local version with lower server timestamp.
target_output.data_model: inspections (id UUID PK, property_id UUID, inspector_id UUID FK, inspection_type ENUM pre_purchase/pre_lease/insurance, status ENUM scheduled/in_progress/synced/report_ready, scheduled_date DATE, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). inspection_items (id UUID PK, inspection_id UUID FK, category ENUM structural/electrical/plumbing/hvac/exterior, description TEXT, condition ENUM pass/fail/needs_attention, notes TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP set by server on push, device_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ recorded from client for debugging, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). defects (id UUID PK, inspection_item_id UUID FK, x_pct FLOAT, y_pct FLOAT, severity ENUM minor/moderate/major, description TEXT, resolved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ server-assigned, device_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). photos (id UUID PK, inspection_item_id UUID FK, s3_key TEXT, thumbnail_s3_key TEXT, caption TEXT, captured_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). voice_notes (id UUID PK, inspection_item_id UUID FK, s3_key TEXT, transcript TEXT, duration_seconds INT, captured_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced BOOLEAN). sync_queues (id UUID PK, device_id TEXT, entity_type TEXT, entity_id UUID, operation ENUM create/update/delete, queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE).
target_output.api_design: Fastify REST API. POST /api/v1/auth/login (returns 8-hour access JWT + 30-day refresh token). POST /api/v1/sync/push (body: {changed: [{table, id, ...fields, device_updated_at}], deleted: [{table, id}]}; server sets updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on all upserts; PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE excluded.updated_at > table.updated_at; returns {synced_at: server_timestamp}). GET /api/v1/sync/pull?last_pulled_at={iso_timestamp} (returns {changes: {table: [records]}, timestamp: server_now}; all records where updated_at > last_pulled_at). POST /api/v1/photos/presign (body: {inspection_item_id, filename, content_type}; returns {presigned_url, s3_key, expires_in: 900}). POST /api/v1/reports/generate (supervisor role; body: {inspection_id}; enqueues SQS job; returns {job_id}). GET /api/v1/reports/{inspection_id} (returns report status and S3 presigned download URL when ready). All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer {jwt}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT (HS256, 8-hour expiry) stored in iOS Keychain via react-native-keychain and Android Keystore — not AsyncStorage. SQLCipher encryption key derived from device biometric authentication (TouchID/FaceID on iOS, Biometric API on Android) with PIN fallback; key not stored in plaintext. HTTPS enforced on all Fastify endpoints; no HTTP fallback. S3 SSE-KMS for all photo, voice note, and report objects. Presigned POST URLs expire in 15 minutes. Inspector role enforces read/write to own assigned inspections only via PostgreSQL row-level security policy (inspector_id = current_user_id). Supervisor role has read access to all inspections. No PHI or financial data; risk level low.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: React Native CI: Bitrise workflow on PR — npm install, jest (80% coverage gate), Expo prebuild (iOS and Android), Xcode build + archive, Android Gradle build, TestFlight upload (iOS), Play Console internal track upload (Android). Expo EAS for OTA JS bundle updates — inspectors receive over-the-air patches without app store release. Server CI: GitHub Actions — npm test, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy to staging, ECS rolling deploy to production (auto on main). ECS Fargate: sync-api (2-4 tasks, ALB, auto-scale CPU 60%), pdf-worker (1-3 tasks, SQS-driven). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS), staging (multi-AZ, TestFlight), production (multi-AZ, App Store, Play Store). Rollback: ECS rolling deploy auto-rollback on health check failure; Expo OTA rollback via expo publish --rollback.
target_output.observability: Sentry React Native SDK: crash reports with WatermelonDB sync context (last_pulled_at, push payload size); JS error boundary per screen. Sentry Node SDK on Fastify: API error tracking with inspector_id and inspection_id tags. CloudWatch custom metrics: sync_push_duration_p99 (SLA <2s for 100 records), photo_upload_queue_depth (alert if >50 items unsynced per device for >30 min), pdf_generation_latency_p95 (SLA <30s), sync_conflict_rate (% of pushes with conflict resolution triggered). Alarm: SNS alert if sync error rate > 5% over 10 minutes; PagerDuty if PDF worker SQS DLQ receives any message.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $680/month: ECS Fargate sync-api 2-4 tasks ~$200, ECS pdf-worker ~$80, RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ ~$240, S3 storage ~$80 (photos + reports at 7-year retention), SQS ~$5, Sentry Team plan ~$26, Bitrise CI ~$50. Lambda could replace Fargate for sync API at lower volume — revisit at 100+ concurrent inspectors. S3 photo storage will grow with adoption; Intelligent-Tiering lifecycle should be added post-pilot.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — MVP Pilot (10 weeks): offline checklist with WatermelonDB, photo capture and background upload, voice-to-text notes, sync push/pull with server-assigned conflict resolution, JWT auth, Bitrise CI, ECS Fargate deployment, Expo OTA, TestFlight and Play Console beta for 10-inspector pilot. Phase 2 — Full Feature (4 weeks): defect tagging on property diagram, PDF report generation via Puppeteer worker, supervisor review dashboard (Next.js web), full App Store and Play Store release. Phase 3 — AI and Integrations (post-launch): defect recognition ML from captured photos via Rekognition, HomeGauge/Home Inspector Pro export integration, multi-inspector parallel inspection with per-inspector assignment scoping.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in React Native and Fastify. WatermelonDB Models typed with strict field definitions — no raw SQL in app code. All sync API handlers must set updated_at server-side — ESLint custom rule banning client timestamp pass-through to PostgreSQL upserts. 80% jest coverage gate for React Native; 85% for Fastify API. Fastify schema validation on all sync/push and sync/pull request/response bodies. No unsynced inspection data in AsyncStorage — only WatermelonDB SQLCipher store for local persistence. Expo OTA updates tested in staging TestFlight flight before production publish.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB migration between schema versions requires careful migration script testing — a bad migration can corrupt local SQLite data on inspector devices. Mitigation: schema migrations tested on device restore simulation in Bitrise; Expo OTA can deliver JS-only fixes but native SQLite schema changes require app store update. Risk: presigned S3 URLs expiring before large photo uploads complete on slow field connections. Mitigation: 15-minute expiry covers uploads up to 50MB at 0.5Mbps; client requests fresh presigned URL on 403 response. Tradeoff: WatermelonDB over a custom sync protocol — WatermelonDB's pull/push model fits perfectly but ties mobile data layer to its SQLite implementation; acceptable for this use case.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: server-assigned CURRENT_TIMESTAMP is the conflict resolution authority on all sync/push upserts; device_updated_at stored separately for debugging. ISS-002 resolved in round 2: react-native-background-fetch with BGTaskScheduler handles iOS photo upload background task lifecycle. Selected WatermelonDB over Realm for offline SQLite — WatermelonDB is open-source and provides a cleaner pull/push sync protocol without Realm Atlas dependency. Expo managed workflow chosen for OTA update capability without ejecting to bare workflow.

metadata.notes: Round 3 offline-first inspection app; resolves server-vs-device clock conflict resolution ambiguity; all issues resolved after 3 rounds.

profile.domain: Commercial Real Estate - Lease Administration Platform

project_goal.value: A greenfield commercial lease administration web application for corporate tenants managing a portfolio of office and retail leases, providing critical date tracking, rent escalation schedule management, option exercise workflows, and CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation tools to eliminate spreadsheet-based processes across a 300-lease portfolio
project_goal.rationale: Current spreadsheet process causes missed option deadlines costing hundreds of thousands in lost renewal opportunities; a single lease administration system is a strategic priority for the corporate real estate team

target_users.value: Corporate real estate managers responsible for lease portfolio, lease administrators tracking critical dates and CAM charges, finance team reviewing rent escalations and ASC 842 ROU asset calculations, legal team managing option exercises and amendments
target_users.rationale: Four distinct user roles with different data access needs and workflow priorities

project_class.rationale: Full-featured browser-based application with complex workflow logic, role-based dashboards, and data-heavy views; not a static site or API-only service

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Complex UI workflows, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL financial data model, Okta auth, BI-style analytics views, admin configuration panel, and ERP integration for accounting system sync

complexity_level.rationale: ASC 842 ROU asset and lease liability calculation logic, multi-currency rent escalation scheduling, CAM reconciliation workflows, and ERP webhook integration add domain-specific complexity beyond standard CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect rent escalation calculations or missed critical dates could result in material financial loss; ASC 842 calculation errors create SEC reporting exposure for public companies

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores lease financial terms, rent amounts, CAM charges, and ROU asset calculations subject to ASC 842 and SOX controls for public company filings

external_exposure.rationale: Internal corporate application requiring Okta SSO authentication; no public access

access_model.value: Okta SSO with SAML 2.0; four RBAC roles: real_estate_manager (full read/write), lease_administrator (lease CRUD, no financial config), finance_analyst (read-only financial views, ASC 842 exports), legal (read-only with amendment history)
access_model.rationale: Corporate Okta instance already deployed; SAML federation with four roles mapped to job functions

feature_scope.value: Lease intake form with document upload; critical date calendar with 180/90/30-day automated reminders via email; rent escalation schedule builder (fixed, CPI-linked, and percentage-of-rent increase types); option exercise workflow (exercise, waive, negotiate actions with deadline tracking); CAM reconciliation tool comparing estimated vs actual charges with dispute workflow; ASC 842 ROU asset and lease liability calculation engine with amortization schedule export; portfolio analytics dashboard (total lease exposure, upcoming expirations, CAM variance trends); admin panel for reminder templates and user management; SAP S/4HANA integration for posting rent accruals
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature scope for commercial lease administration replacing spreadsheets

mvp_scope.value: Lease intake with critical date tracking and 180/90/30-day email reminders; rent escalation schedule display (fixed increases only for MVP); option exercise workflow with deadline alerts; basic portfolio dashboard showing upcoming expirations and overdue actions
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP delivers the highest-value feature (critical date management preventing missed options) without the complexity of ASC 842 calculations and CAM reconciliation, which ship in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Okta SAML 2.0 SSO with MFA enforced; HTTPS everywhere; financial data encrypted at rest with GCP KMS; field-level encryption for lease financial terms; comprehensive audit log of all data changes; SOX-compliant change management for production deployments
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data sensitivity and public company SOX requirements mandate strong encryption and audit controls

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS; Tanstack Table for lease grid views; React Hook Form with Zod validation for lease intake; date-fns for critical date calculations
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js App Router with server components for lease list views; Tanstack Table for complex sortable/filterable portfolio grids; team proficiency with TypeScript

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM; Celery with Redis for background jobs (reminder emails, ERP sync, ASC 842 batch recalculations); APScheduler for critical date scan cron job
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI with async support for concurrent lease data API calls; Celery for reliable background job execution with retry semantics for ERP integration

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (GCP) with read replica for analytics queries; Redis 7 on Memorystore for Celery broker and session cache
data_platform.rationale: Cloud SQL managed PostgreSQL eliminates DBA overhead; read replica separates analytics from transactional load; Redis required by Celery

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for both Next.js frontend and FastAPI backend; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16; Memorystore Redis; Cloud Tasks for deferred jobs; Cloud Storage for lease document uploads
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run eliminates container infrastructure management; autoscales to zero during off-business-hours reducing costs; GCP-native services for tight integration

privacy_retention_policy.value: Lease financial terms retained for the life of the lease plus 10 years per SOX document retention; audit logs retained 7 years; document uploads retained per lease term; GDPR: lease counterparty contact data (EU landlord contacts) subject to data subject access requests
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOX and ASC 842 require long-term retention of lease financial records for audit

future_scope.value: IFRS 16 lease accounting standard support for international subsidiaries; AI-assisted lease abstraction from uploaded PDF documents; portfolio benchmarking against market rent comparables; mobile app for field lease inspections
future_scope.rationale: International expansion and AI abstraction are Year 2 roadmap items pending MVP validation

constraints.value: ASC 842 calculation logic must be validated by the company's external auditors before the finance team uses it for SEC reporting; SAP S/4HANA integration must use SAP's approved OData API (no direct database access); launch timeline 16 weeks for MVP; team of 4 engineers
constraints.rationale: Auditor validation is a hard dependency for the ASC 842 module; SAP integration is constrained by SAP's supported API surface

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for FastAPI backend with trace correlation; structured JSON logging with lease_id and user_id in every log line; PagerDuty alerts for critical date reminder job failures and ERP sync errors; custom Datadog dashboard for upcoming critical dates by deadline proximity
observability_baseline.rationale: Critical date reminder failures are high-priority incidents given the financial exposure of missed option deadlines

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; MVP phases with finance and legal user acceptance testing gates before each phase ships to production; feature flags for ASC 842 module pending auditor validation
execution_preference.rationale: UAT gates ensure financial calculation accuracy before use in SEC reporting workflows

compliance_context.value: ASC 842 lease accounting standard: ROU asset and lease liability calculations must be validated by external auditors before use in SEC reporting; SOX controls: change management approval for production deployments touching financial calculation logic; GDPR: EU landlord counterparty data subject to access and deletion requests; state law: lease records may be subject to discovery in commercial litigation requiring preservation holds
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=financial trigger ASC 842, SOX, and GDPR compliance requirements for a public company lease administration system

project.goal: Commercial lease administration web app for 300-lease corporate portfolio
project.target_users: Real estate managers, lease administrators, finance analysts, legal team

frontend.forms: React Hook Form with Zod validation

backend.jobs: Celery with Redis for background tasks
backend.scheduler: APScheduler for nightly critical date scan

security.authorization: RBAC four roles: real_estate_manager, lease_administrator, finance_analyst, legal
security.encryption: GCP KMS for data at rest, field-level encryption for financial terms

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with read replica
data.documents: Cloud Storage for lease PDF uploads

devops.hosting: GCP Cloud Run for frontend and backend

constraints.asc842: External auditor validation required before finance use
constraints.sap: SAP S/4HANA OData API integration only

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope is correctly prioritized around critical date management — preventing missed option deadlines is the highest-value feature and the primary business justification for the system. Deferring ASC 842 calculations to Phase 2 is the right call given the auditor validation dependency; that hard dependency would block the MVP launch if bundled in. The four-role RBAC model matches real-world commercial real estate org structures precisely.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with Celery is the right pairing for this workload: synchronous lease CRUD via FastAPI, background reminder scheduling via Celery beat, and ERP sync jobs via Celery workers. Cloud Run is appropriate for the anticipated traffic profile (200 daily active users during business hours, near-zero overnight). The ASC 842 calculation engine should be isolated as a separate Python module with a comprehensive unit test suite that external auditors can review, rather than embedding the logic in the API layer. Tanstack Table is the correct frontend choice for the portfolio grid views — 300 leases with multi-column sort, filter, and row-level expand is exactly the use case it handles well.
reasoner_reviews.security: Field-level encryption for lease financial terms (monthly_rent, escalation amounts) using GCP KMS is important but adds complexity to query filtering on those fields — ensure the data model uses separate plaintext summary fields for filter/sort operations. Audit log must be append-only with no DELETE capability for any role including admins to satisfy SOX requirements. Okta SAML assertion must include group membership for role mapping; validate that Okta group names map correctly to FastAPI dependency injection role checks.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The SAP S/4HANA OData API constraint means the ERP integration cannot use bulk INSERT patterns; SAP OData is request-per-record for journal entry posting. This makes the integration fundamentally async — Celery workers posting rent accruals one record at a time with retry logic is the correct architecture. The 16-week MVP timeline is achievable with 4 engineers given the MVP scope excludes ASC 842 and CAM reconciliation.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application structured as domain modules: /leases (CRUD, intake, document upload), /critical_dates (scan, remind, acknowledge), /rent_schedules (escalation schedule management), /options (exercise/waive/negotiate workflow), /cam (reconciliation workflow), /asc842 (calculation engine, isolated module), /admin (user management, reminder templates), /erp (SAP OData integration); shared dependencies for auth (Okta JWT validation), audit logging (append-only audit_events table), and database session management
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with FastAPI automatic OpenAPI docs at /docs; versioned at /api/v1; all financial write operations require idempotency key header to prevent duplicate rent accrual postings; bulk export endpoints for ASC 842 amortization schedules return streaming CSV response to handle large datasets without memory pressure
backend.business_modules: CriticalDateScanner: APScheduler job running nightly at 2AM, scanning leases for critical dates within 180/90/30-day windows, enqueuing Celery reminder tasks; RentEscalationEngine: computes next escalation amount based on increase_type (fixed/CPI/percentage); OptionWorkflowManager: tracks option exercise deadlines and transitions option_status through exercise/waive/negotiate/expired states; SAPIntegrationWorker: Celery worker posting rent accruals to SAP S/4HANA via OData PATCH /A_JournalEntryItem with retry on 503
backend.background_jobs: daily_critical_date_scan: APScheduler cron 0 2 * * * scanning all active leases for upcoming deadlines; critical_date_reminder: Celery task sending reminder email via SendGrid with lease name, deadline date, and action required; sap_accrual_post: Celery task posting monthly rent accrual to SAP S/4HANA OData API with 3-retry exponential backoff; asc842_batch_recalculate: Celery task triggered on lease amendment to recalculate ROU asset and lease liability for the modified lease
backend.failure_handling: Celery task failure triggers DLQ (dead letter queue in Redis) after 3 retries; DLQ messages alert PagerDuty with task_id, lease_id, and error detail; critical_date_reminder failures are high-severity PagerDuty pages; SAP integration failures are medium-severity with 24-hour resolution SLA

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with server components for data-fetching lease list views; client components for interactive portfolio grid (Tanstack Table), lease intake form (React Hook Form), and critical date calendar; layout with sidebar navigation (leases, critical dates, options, CAM, analytics, admin) and persistent lease context breadcrumb
frontend.pages_and_flows: Portfolio Overview (Tanstack Table with 300 leases, sortable by expiration, annual_rent, option_deadline; row expand shows quick summary); Lease Detail (tabbed: overview, rent schedule, critical dates, options, CAM, documents, audit history); Lease Intake (multi-step React Hook Form: property info, financial terms, critical dates, document upload, review); Critical Date Calendar (calendar view with color-coded urgency: red <30 days, amber 30-90 days, green 90-180 days); Option Exercise Workflow (timeline view of option deadlines with exercise/waive/negotiate action buttons); CAM Reconciliation (comparison table of estimated vs actual charges with dispute annotation); Analytics Dashboard (exposure by landlord, geography, expiration year; CAM variance trend charts)
frontend.state_management: React Query (Tanstack Query) for server state: lease list, lease detail, critical dates; optimistic updates for option exercise status changes; invalidation on lease mutation. Zustand for UI state: active filters, selected leases for bulk operations, sidebar collapsed state
frontend.performance_notes: Portfolio grid: Tanstack Table with row virtualization via @tanstack/react-virtual for 300+ lease rows without DOM weight; server components for initial lease list hydration to reduce client bundle; next/image for landlord logos and property photos

security.auth_design: Okta SAML 2.0 SP-initiated SSO; FastAPI validates Okta-issued JWT via JWKS endpoint; role extracted from Okta groups claim and mapped to application roles in FastAPI dependency: real_estate_manager, lease_administrator, finance_analyst, legal; session tokens stored in httponly secure cookies, 8-hour expiry with sliding renewal
security.authorization_model: RBAC: real_estate_manager role full CRUD on all resources; lease_administrator can create/edit leases and critical dates but cannot edit financial terms or export ASC 842 data; finance_analyst read-only on all resources with export access for ASC 842 amortization schedules and rent escalation reports; legal read-only on all resources including amendment history and audit log; no role can DELETE audit_events records
security.audit_and_logging_controls: append-only audit_events table: event_id, user_id, user_role, action ENUM create/update/delete/export/view_financial, resource_type, resource_id, old_values_encrypted, new_values_encrypted, ip_address, timestamp; no application-level DELETE endpoint for audit_events; PostgreSQL row-level security prevents even admin role from deleting audit records; structured FastAPI logs include lease_id, user_id, trace_id in every line

data.entities: leases (lease_id UUID PK, property_id FK, landlord_id FK, lease_type ENUM office/retail/industrial/land, commencement_date DATE, expiration_date DATE, base_rent_monthly ENCRYPTED DECIMAL, rent_increase_type ENUM fixed/cpi/percentage, annual_escalation_pct DECIMAL, lease_status ENUM active/expired/holdover/terminated), properties (property_id UUID PK, address, city, state, country, property_type, sqft, portfolio_region), landlords (landlord_id UUID PK, company_name, contact_name, contact_email_encrypted, jurisdiction), critical_dates (date_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, date_type ENUM option_deadline/expiration/rent_increase/cam_reconciliation/renewal_window, due_date DATE, reminder_sent_180 BOOLEAN, reminder_sent_90 BOOLEAN, reminder_sent_30 BOOLEAN, acknowledged_by UUID, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ), rent_schedules (schedule_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, effective_date DATE, monthly_rent DECIMAL, escalation_basis VARCHAR, is_current BOOLEAN), lease_options (option_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, option_type ENUM renewal/expansion/termination/purchase, exercise_deadline DATE, option_status ENUM unexercised/exercised/waived/expired, exercised_by UUID, exercised_at TIMESTAMPTZ), cam_reconciliations (recon_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, reconciliation_year INT, estimated_cam DECIMAL, actual_cam DECIMAL, variance DECIMAL, dispute_status ENUM none/disputed/resolved, dispute_notes TEXT), amendments (amendment_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, amendment_date DATE, amendment_type ENUM rent_modification/extension/expansion/termination, summary TEXT, document_id FK, effective_date DATE), audit_events (event_id UUID PK, user_id, user_role, action, resource_type, resource_id, old_values JSONB, new_values JSONB, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 primary for all transactional writes; read replica for analytics queries (ASC 842 reports, portfolio dashboards, CAM trend analysis); Cloud Storage bucket for lease PDF and amendment documents with uniform bucket-level ACL; field-level KMS encryption for lease financial columns using AEAD encryption with separate key per tenant company
data.schema_notes: Separate rent_schedules table instead of single monthly_rent on leases to represent the full escalation timeline; option status state machine enforced via PostgreSQL check constraint; audit_events populated via SQLAlchemy event listeners on all model mutations to ensure no write path bypasses audit logging

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run: lease-admin-frontend service (Next.js, min 1 max 5 instances, 2 vCPU 2Gi) and lease-admin-api service (FastAPI, min 2 max 10 instances, 2 vCPU 4Gi); Celery workers on Cloud Run Jobs triggered by Cloud Tasks; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with private IP, no public endpoint; Memorystore Redis for Celery broker; Cloud Storage for documents; GCP Secret Manager for database credentials and API keys
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR runs pytest (90% coverage gate), mypy type checking, ruff linting, Next.js build check, Lighthouse CI for frontend; on merge to main deploys to staging Cloud Run with automated smoke tests; production promotion requires manual approval and change management ticket for any PR touching financial calculation logic per SOX controls
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: Cloud Run instrumentation for FastAPI traces with lease_id and user_id span tags; custom Datadog dashboard: upcoming critical dates by proximity (< 30 days, 30-90 days), option exercise deadlines, CAM reconciliation due dates, Celery task queue depth; PagerDuty integration for critical_date_reminder failures (high) and SAP sync failures (medium)

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a greenfield commercial lease administration platform for a corporate tenant's 300-lease office and retail portfolio. The domain has several distinct complexity drivers: ASC 842 ROU asset calculation logic requiring external auditor validation, multi-type rent escalation scheduling (fixed/CPI/percentage), option exercise deadline tracking with material financial consequences, CAM reconciliation with dispute workflow, and SAP S/4HANA OData integration for rent accrual posting. Key architectural decisions: FastAPI with Celery for async reminder and ERP jobs, isolated ASC 842 calculation module for auditor review, PostgreSQL rent_schedules table to represent full escalation timelines, field-level KMS encryption for financial terms, and Tanstack Table with row virtualization for the 300-lease portfolio grid. MVP scope correctly excludes ASC 842 and CAM reconciliation to unblock launch pending auditor validation.
target_output.title: Commercial Lease Administration Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A greenfield web application for corporate tenant lease portfolio management built with Next.js 14, FastAPI, and PostgreSQL on GCP Cloud Run. Manages critical date tracking with automated 180/90/30-day reminder emails, rent escalation schedule management, option exercise workflows, CAM reconciliation, and ASC 842 ROU asset calculations across a 300-lease portfolio. Okta SAML 2.0 SSO with four-role RBAC; field-level KMS encryption for lease financial terms; append-only audit log for SOX compliance. SAP S/4HANA integration via OData API for monthly rent accrual posting. MVP delivers critical date management and option exercise tracking; ASC 842 and CAM modules follow in Phase 2 pending external auditor sign-off.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Next.js 14 App Router frontend on Cloud Run serving lease portfolio management UI with server components for data-heavy lease list views and client components for Tanstack Table grids and React Hook Form lease intake. FastAPI backend on Cloud Run structured as domain modules (leases, critical_dates, options, cam, asc842, erp). PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with a read replica separating analytics queries from transactional operations. Celery workers on Cloud Run Jobs handle three background workloads: nightly critical date scanning via APScheduler cron, SendGrid reminder email dispatch, and SAP S/4HANA OData rent accrual posting with retry semantics. All lease financial terms encrypted at field level using GCP KMS AEAD encryption before persistence. Okta SAML 2.0 SSO with JWT validation in FastAPI; four RBAC roles mapped from Okta group claims. An append-only audit_events table records every financial data change with no DELETE capability for any role, satisfying SOX audit requirements. The ASC 842 calculation engine is isolated as a standalone Python module with its own test suite for external auditor review, gated behind a feature flag until audit sign-off.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Tanstack Table v8 with @tanstack/react-virtual for 300-lease grid, React Hook Form with Zod validation, Tanstack Query for server state, Zustand for UI state, date-fns for critical date arithmetic. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM, Alembic migrations, Celery 5.3 with Redis broker, APScheduler for cron jobs. Auth: Okta SAML 2.0 with python-jose JWT validation. Email: SendGrid transactional email for critical date reminders. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL. Cache/queue: Redis 7 on Memorystore. Documents: GCP Cloud Storage. Encryption: GCP KMS with AEAD field-level encryption. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Cloud Run deployments.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Lease Portfolio Grid: Tanstack Table with 300+ rows, sortable by expiration_date, annual_rent, option_deadline, property_region; row virtualization via @tanstack/react-virtual; click-through to Lease Detail. Lease Intake Form: multi-step React Hook Form capturing property, financial terms, rent escalation type, critical dates, and document upload to Cloud Storage; Zod validation with CRE-specific rules (commencement_date before expiration_date, escalation_pct > 0 when type=percentage). Critical Date Calendar: color-coded urgency calendar (red under 30 days, amber 30-90, green 90-180); click-to-acknowledge with acknowledgment recorded in audit log. Option Exercise Workflow: timeline showing exercise deadline, current option_status, and action buttons (Exercise, Waive, Negotiate); status transitions trigger Celery task to notify real estate manager and log to audit_events. Rent Escalation Schedule: table showing effective_date, monthly_rent, and escalation_basis for each period; RentEscalationEngine computes future schedule from escalation type and rate. CAM Reconciliation: comparison table of estimated vs actual CAM charges per year; dispute annotation workflow with dispute_status transitions; variance trend chart across reconciliation years. ASC 842 Module (Phase 2, feature-flagged): ROU asset and lease liability calculation with discount rate input, payment schedule generation, amortization schedule export as CSV. Portfolio Analytics: total lease exposure by landlord and geography, expiration timeline histogram, CAM variance trend by property type. SAP Integration: monthly Celery job posting rent accruals for all active leases to SAP S/4HANA via OData API.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run lease-admin-frontend: Next.js 14 server, min 1 max 5 instances, handles SSR and API route for document uploads. Cloud Run lease-admin-api: FastAPI 0.111, min 2 max 10 instances, handles all lease data API operations. Cloud Run Jobs (Celery workers): critical_date_reminder_worker, sap_accrual_worker, asc842_recalculate_worker — triggered via Cloud Tasks queue. APScheduler within FastAPI process: nightly cron at 2AM ET enqueuing critical date scan tasks. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: private IP, multi-zone HA, read replica for analytics. Memorystore Redis 7: Celery broker and result backend, session cache. Cloud Storage: lease_documents bucket with uniform bucket ACL and CMEK encryption. GCP KMS: key ring per environment with separate key for lease financial field encryption. Datadog APM sidecar integrated via DD_TRACE_ENABLED env var. SendGrid API for reminder email delivery.
target_output.workflows: Critical Date Reminder Workflow: APScheduler cron triggers daily_critical_date_scan task at 2AM; task queries all active leases joining critical_dates where due_date between TODAY and TODAY+180 and corresponding reminder_sent flag is false; for each matching date enqueues Celery critical_date_reminder task with lease_id, date_id, reminder_type (30/90/180); Celery worker renders SendGrid email template with lease_name, property_address, deadline, required_action; marks reminder_sent_N=true and writes audit_event; on Celery failure after 3 retries enqueues DLQ message and triggers PagerDuty high-priority page to on-call lease administrator. Option Exercise Workflow: lease administrator opens Option Exercise view for a lease; selects option and clicks Exercise; frontend calls PATCH /api/v1/options/{option_id} with {status: 'exercised', exercised_by: user_id}; FastAPI validates user role (only real_estate_manager may exercise), updates option_status, writes audit_event, enqueues Celery notification task to email real_estate_manager and legal team with exercise confirmation; lease_options record updated with exercised_at timestamp. CAM Reconciliation Workflow: at year-end, lease administrator navigates to CAM Reconciliation for a lease; enters actual_cam from landlord invoice; system computes variance against estimated_cam; if variance exceeds 5% of estimated, dispute_status automatically set to 'disputed' and real estate manager notified via email; manager reviews, annotates dispute_notes, and resolves via PATCH /api/v1/cam/{recon_id}/resolve; resolved reconciliations included in annual CAM variance portfolio report.
target_output.data_model: leases (lease_id UUID PK, property_id FK, landlord_id FK, lease_type ENUM office/retail/industrial/land, commencement_date DATE, expiration_date DATE, base_rent_monthly_enc BYTEA encrypted via KMS AEAD, rent_increase_type ENUM fixed/cpi/percentage, annual_escalation_pct DECIMAL, lease_status ENUM active/expired/holdover/terminated, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). properties (property_id UUID PK, street_address, city, state, country_code CHAR(2), property_type ENUM office/retail/industrial/land, sqft INT, portfolio_region VARCHAR). landlords (landlord_id UUID PK, company_name, contact_name, contact_email_enc BYTEA, jurisdiction VARCHAR). critical_dates (date_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, date_type ENUM option_deadline/expiration/rent_increase/cam_reconciliation/renewal_window, due_date DATE, reminder_sent_180 BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, reminder_sent_90 BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, reminder_sent_30 BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, acknowledged_by UUID FK users, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). rent_schedules (schedule_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, effective_date DATE, monthly_rent DECIMAL(14,2), escalation_basis VARCHAR, is_current BOOLEAN). lease_options (option_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, option_type ENUM renewal/expansion/termination/purchase, exercise_deadline DATE, option_term_months INT, option_status ENUM unexercised/exercised/waived/expired, exercised_by UUID, exercised_at TIMESTAMPTZ). cam_reconciliations (recon_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, reconciliation_year INT, estimated_cam DECIMAL(12,2), actual_cam DECIMAL(12,2), variance DECIMAL(12,2), dispute_status ENUM none/disputed/resolved, dispute_notes TEXT, resolved_by UUID, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). amendments (amendment_id UUID PK, lease_id FK, amendment_date DATE, amendment_type ENUM rent_modification/extension/expansion/termination, summary TEXT, document_id UUID FK, effective_date DATE). audit_events (event_id UUID PK, user_id UUID, user_role VARCHAR, action ENUM create/update/delete/export/view_financial, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, old_values JSONB, new_values JSONB, ip_address INET, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Lease financial entities: lease_payment_schedules (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, payment_number INT, due_date DATE, base_rent_cents BIGINT, cam_charges_cents BIGINT, total_payment_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3)), payment_transactions (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, schedule_id FK, payment_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), method ENUM ach/wire/check, status ENUM on_time/late/partial/missed, posted_at TIMESTAMPTZ), ledger_entries (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, entry_date DATE, entry_type ENUM debit/credit, amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), balance_after_cents BIGINT, description VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: RESTful FastAPI at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0 auto-docs. Lease operations: GET /leases (paginated, filterable by status/region/expiration_year), POST /leases (intake), GET /leases/{lease_id} (full detail), PATCH /leases/{lease_id} (update with audit event), GET /leases/{lease_id}/rent-schedule (escalation table). Critical dates: GET /critical-dates?days_until_due=30 (upcoming), PATCH /critical-dates/{date_id}/acknowledge. Options: GET /leases/{lease_id}/options, PATCH /options/{option_id} (exercise/waive/negotiate, role-gated). CAM: GET /leases/{lease_id}/cam, POST /cam/{recon_id}/reconcile, PATCH /cam/{recon_id}/resolve. ERP: POST /erp/accruals/trigger (admin-only, enqueues SAP sync Celery task). Analytics: GET /analytics/portfolio-summary (aggregates for dashboard). All mutating endpoints require X-Idempotency-Key header for financial operations. Admin: GET/POST /admin/users, GET/PUT /admin/reminder-templates.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Okta SAML 2.0 SP-initiated SSO; FastAPI validates Okta JWT via /keys JWKS endpoint; session stored in httponly secure cookie with 8-hour sliding expiry. Authorization: FastAPI Depends injection for role checking; PATCH /options/{option_id} with status=exercised restricted to real_estate_manager role; finance_analyst role blocked from all POST/PATCH endpoints except CAM dispute resolution; no role has DELETE on audit_events (enforced by PostgreSQL row-level security policy on audit_events table). Encryption: GCP KMS key ring lease-admin-{env}; field-level AEAD encryption for base_rent_monthly, contact_email, and any column tagged @encrypted in SQLAlchemy model metadata; encrypted columns stored as BYTEA; plaintext summary columns (base_rent_monthly_display DECIMAL) maintained for sort/filter queries. SOX controls: production deployment of any PR touching /asc842 or /cam modules requires change management ticket with finance lead approval; GitHub Actions enforces this via CODEOWNERS file mapping those paths to finance-lead review requirement. Audit: every SQLAlchemy model write triggers after_flush event listener writing to audit_events with old_values and new_values as JSONB; no application code path can bypass this listener.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: on PR runs pytest with 90% coverage gate, mypy strict type checking, ruff linting, Next.js build, Lighthouse CI (Performance > 85, Accessibility > 95); on merge to main deploys to staging Cloud Run with automated smoke tests (lease create, critical date scan, option exercise API calls). Production promotion: Cloud Run traffic migration; any PR touching financial calculation modules requires CODEOWNERS-enforced finance lead approval plus change management ticket per SOX. Cloud Run deployment: gcloud run deploy with --tag for preview deployments; traffic split commands for gradual production rollout. Celery workers deployed as Cloud Run Jobs triggered by Cloud Tasks queues; separate job per worker type for independent scaling. Alembic database migrations run as pre-deploy Cloud Run Job task before new API version receives traffic. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager for DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, OKTA_CLIENT_SECRET, SENDGRID_API_KEY, SAP_ODATA_API_KEY; mounted as Cloud Run environment variables via --update-secrets.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: FastAPI instrumented via ddtrace with custom span tags: lease_id, user_id, user_role, operation_type. Custom Datadog dashboard: upcoming critical dates histogram (< 30 days, 30-90 days, 90-180 days), option exercise deadline countdown list, Celery task queue depth per queue, SAP OData integration error rate and retry count, Cloud SQL connection pool utilization. PagerDuty integrations: Celery critical_date_reminder failure after retries (high severity, immediate page), SAP accrual sync failure (medium severity, 4-hour response SLA), Cloud SQL replication lag > 60 seconds (high severity). Structured JSON logs from FastAPI include: request_id, user_id, lease_id, duration_ms, http_status, trace_id for correlation with Datadog APM spans. Alerting: Datadog monitor for any lease with option_deadline within 7 days where option_status='unexercised' and no action acknowledged — daily digest email to real estate manager.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3,200/month at 300-lease scale: Cloud Run frontend min 1 instance approximately $70/month, Cloud Run API min 2 instances approximately $200/month, Celery workers as Jobs on-demand approximately $50/month, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-n1-standard-4 with read replica approximately $1,800/month, Memorystore Redis 1GB approximately $100/month, Cloud Storage and KMS approximately $100/month, Datadog approximately $600/month, SendGrid professional tier approximately $100/month. Well within typical enterprise SaaS budget for a 300-lease portfolio. Scaling path: Cloud Run autoscaling handles lease query spikes during quarterly reporting periods; read replica absorbs ASC 842 batch report generation load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP (Weeks 1-16): lease intake form with Cloud Storage document upload; critical date tracking with APScheduler nightly scan; 180/90/30-day SendGrid reminder emails; option exercise workflow with status state machine; basic portfolio Tanstack Table dashboard; Okta SAML SSO and four-role RBAC; field-level KMS encryption; append-only audit log; SAP OData integration for rent accruals (Celery worker); GitHub Actions CI/CD with staging Cloud Run environment; Datadog APM and PagerDuty alerting. Phase 2 - ASC 842 and CAM (Weeks 17-26): ASC 842 ROU asset and lease liability calculation engine (isolated module, external auditor review required before enabling feature flag); amortization schedule CSV export; CAM reconciliation comparison tool with dispute workflow; CAM variance trend analytics. Phase 3 - Advanced Analytics (Weeks 27-32): portfolio exposure heatmap by geography; rent escalation projection charts for next 5 years; landlord benchmark comparison; amendment history timeline view; bulk critical date import from legacy spreadsheet for initial data migration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints required on all FastAPI route handlers and service functions; mypy strict mode in CI. SQLAlchemy event listeners for audit logging must be tested with unit tests that assert audit_event creation on every model mutation. ASC 842 calculation module must have 100% unit test coverage with test cases reviewed by external auditors before feature flag enablement. CODEOWNERS: /api/asc842 and /api/cam paths require finance lead approval on any PR. React Hook Form schemas must match FastAPI Pydantic models exactly; any schema change requires coordinated frontend/backend PR. All Celery tasks must be idempotent and tested with unit tests simulating retry scenarios. Alembic migrations must be reversible; downgrade() method required for all migrations touching financial tables. No direct database queries in Next.js server components — all data access via FastAPI API routes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ASC 842 calculation errors in ROU asset or lease liability output could result in incorrect SEC footnote disclosures; mitigation: isolated calculation module, 100% unit test coverage, external auditor review, feature flag disabled until sign-off, finance analyst role cannot export until flag enabled. Risk: SAP OData rate limits (100 requests per minute per RFC) could delay monthly rent accrual posting for 300 leases; mitigation: Celery worker uses token bucket rate limiter set to 80 requests/minute with jitter, full 300-lease cycle completes in approximately 4 minutes. Risk: AEAD field-level encryption on base_rent_monthly prevents SQL-level filtering and aggregation on encrypted values; mitigation: maintain plaintext base_rent_monthly_display DECIMAL column for query operations, accepting that this column is visible to database admins (acceptable given SOX-controlled access). Tradeoff: Cloud Run over GKE for operational simplicity with a 4-engineer team; acceptable for the anticipated traffic profile with Cloud Run autoscaling handling quarterly reporting spikes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL selected over Spanner: Spanner's global distribution is unnecessary for a single-region internal tool and adds operational complexity for a small team. Celery selected over Cloud Tasks native for background jobs: Celery provides richer retry semantics and DLQ support needed for SAP OData integration with variable error conditions. Tanstack Table selected over AG Grid: AG Grid community license restrictions on commercial use make Tanstack Table (MIT license) the correct choice. Feature flag for ASC 842 module implemented via GCP Secret Manager flag checked at module import rather than a full feature flag service, keeping the implementation simple for a single toggle needed until auditor sign-off.

metadata.notes: Commercial lease administration web app for 300-lease corporate portfolio; FastAPI/Next.js on GCP Cloud Run with Okta SAML, KMS field encryption, ASC 842 module, and SAP OData integration.

profile.domain: Real Estate Data API Developer Landing Page

project_goal.value: Developer-facing marketing and documentation landing page for a real estate data API product, driving developer sign-ups and showcasing endpoint capabilities with an interactive API explorer.
project_goal.rationale: Core business objective is developer acquisition and self-serve onboarding to the API product.

target_users.value: Software developers building property search, analytics, or investment applications who need structured real estate data feeds.
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified in go-to-market strategy.

project_class.rationale: Static content site with no server-side persistence; API sign-up flows offload to Stripe and external provisioning.

capabilities.rationale: Static site with conversion tracking; no backend runtime required.

complexity_level.rationale: Content-heavy static site with serverless form handling; no complex state.

risk_level.rationale: No sensitive data stored on landing page infrastructure.

data_sensitivity.rationale: No PII or financial data collected or stored on this surface.

external_exposure.rationale: Developer discovery site must be publicly accessible without authentication.

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication required to browse docs or interactive explorer. Sign-up redirects to Stripe-hosted checkout.
access_model.rationale: Frictionless developer onboarding is a conversion priority.

feature_scope.value: Endpoint documentation pages, interactive API explorer with static fixture responses, pricing tier comparison table, developer testimonials, sign-up CTA linking to Stripe checkout, CMS-managed pricing copy.
feature_scope.rationale: Covers all content surfaces needed for developer evaluation and conversion.

mvp_scope.value: Static docs pages, pricing table, interactive explorer with 3 sample endpoints using static fixtures, sign-up CTA linking to Stripe.
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum content to enable self-serve developer sign-up.

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced, CSP headers, no raw API keys in client bundle, Vercel WAF for bot mitigation on form endpoints.
security_baseline.rationale: Standard hardening for public-facing static site.

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, MDX for documentation pages via Contentlayer.
frontend_stack.rationale: Team familiarity and strong static generation support for docs.

backend_stack.value: None (static site); Vercel Edge Functions for form submission forwarding only.
backend_stack.rationale: No server-side persistence required; edge functions sufficient for contact form relay.

data_platform.value: None; pricing content managed via Contentlayer with MDX files in repository.
data_platform.rationale: CMS-lite approach avoids database dependency for a static site.

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN distribution.
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config deployments, preview URLs per PR, and Edge Functions co-located with CDN.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data retained on landing page. Form submissions forwarded to CRM; landing page infrastructure stores nothing.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data minimization by design; all retention governed by CRM data policies.

future_scope.value: Personalized docs based on detected API key tier, changelog RSS feed, SDK download tracking.
future_scope.rationale: Post-launch iteration roadmap.

constraints.value: No server-side database. Deploy in under 2 minutes. Interactive explorer must not proxy real API calls from the landing page domain.
constraints.rationale: Security and operational simplicity constraints.

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page views and Core Web Vitals; custom event tracking for CTA clicks and explorer interactions.
observability_baseline.rationale: Developer funnel analytics and performance monitoring.

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment via GitHub Actions on merge to main; PR preview deployments for content review.
execution_preference.rationale: Marketing team needs fast content iteration cycle.

project.goal: Drive developer sign-ups for the real estate data API through self-serve documentation and interactive exploration
project.target_users: Developers evaluating property search and analytics APIs

frontend.content: Contentlayer with MDX for endpoint docs

security.transport: HTTPS enforced, strict CSP, no API keys in client bundle
security.bot_mitigation: Vercel WAF on Edge Function routes

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Vercel CLI deploy
devops.environments: preview per PR, production on main

reasoner_reviews.product: The sign-up funnel from interactive explorer to Stripe checkout is the critical conversion path. Ensuring the explorer loads sample responses from static fixtures rather than live API calls eliminates security risk and latency variance for developer evaluators.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static generation with Contentlayer is the right fit for MDX-based endpoint docs — page rebuilds on content change are fast. Edge Functions for form relay avoid any database dependency while keeping the submission path on-domain for attribution tracking.
reasoner_reviews.security: Prohibiting live API proxying from the landing page domain is correct — any API key would be visible in browser network tabs. Static fixtures for the explorer and Stripe-hosted checkout for sign-up are the correct security boundaries.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Vercel CDN with ISR satisfies the sub-2-minute deploy constraint. Contentlayer MDX compilation happens at build time, adding no runtime latency.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan underspecified the interactive explorer implementation — it was unclear whether it used live fixtures or proxied calls. This revision must explicitly define the static fixture approach, name the 3 sample endpoints and their fixture JSON files, and add a CI check enforcing the no-proxy constraint.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with static pages for /docs/[endpoint], /pricing, /about, and a client-side interactive explorer component loading static fixture JSON
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home (hero + CTA), /docs/endpoints (MDX sidebar nav with 3 documented endpoints), /pricing (tier table + Stripe CTA), /explorer (client-side component fetching /public/fixtures/*.json), /changelog
frontend.performance_notes: next/image for API dashboard screenshot assets, Shiki syntax highlighting for code blocks compiled at build time, Lighthouse CI gating on 90+ performance score on mobile
frontend.frontend_security_notes: CSP disallowing unsafe-inline scripts; no API keys in JS bundle; explorer component fetches only same-origin /public/fixtures/ paths
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA for pricing table and docs navigation; keyboard-navigable API explorer with aria-live region for sample response output

security.auth_design: No authentication on public pages; Stripe handles sign-up identity and payment
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel WAF rate-limiting on Edge Function form submission route; honeypot field on email capture forms
security.privacy_controls: No cookies set beyond Vercel Analytics first-party script; GDPR-compliant cookie banner for EU visitors

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network serving Next.js static output; Edge Function for /api/contact form relay to HubSpot CRM
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: bun install, bun run build (next build with Contentlayer), Lighthouse CI mobile run (performance >= 90), fixture-proxy grep check (exit non-zero if Explorer fetch targets non-/fixtures/ URL), vercel deploy — total under 90 seconds
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics for CWV and page views; custom Vercel Edge event for CTA clicks and explorer interactions

ISS-001.title: Interactive explorer implementation unspecified — live proxy vs static fixtures ambiguous
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not clarify whether the API explorer sends live requests to the API backend or uses static fixture JSON. This creates a security gap (API key exposure) and a reliability risk (explorer dependent on API uptime).

history.note: Identified by critic reviewer; plan section on explorer was vague.

focus_issues.title: Interactive explorer implementation unspecified — live proxy vs static fixtures ambiguous
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan did not clarify whether the API explorer sends live requests to the API backend or uses static fixture JSON. This creates a security gap (API key exposure) and a reliability risk.

latest_recommendations: Explicitly define the API explorer as static-fixture-based with no live proxy; document the 3 sample endpoints and their fixture JSON files; add CI grep check to enforce no external fetch in the explorer component.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is round 2 of a developer-facing real estate API landing page. The critical issue from round 1 is resolving the API explorer ambiguity — it must be explicitly static-fixture-based with no live proxy. I need to document the 3 sample endpoints, their fixture JSON structure, and enforce this constraint in CI with a grep check. All other sections from round 1 are sound.

fix_report.action_taken: Explicitly defined the interactive API explorer as a client-side React component that fetches static fixture JSON files from /public/fixtures/; documented 3 sample endpoint fixtures (properties/search, listings/nearby, markets/trends) with response schemas; added CI grep check that scans the Explorer component source for any fetch call not targeting /fixtures/ and fails the build if found.
